1111.content: This is Berk, land of one of the most powerfull viking tribe that ever existed. Not only was powerful because of their leader Stoick the vast, but also because thanks to his son Hiccup they were able to make peace with the dragons. However someone wasn't entirely happy about all this success, that somebody was Astrid, a young and very skilled female warrior. She always was very proud, and even though she liked the changes that happened to Berk, she was a little disappointed by the fact that once she was the most respect young wiking of all Berk, while now Hiccup took her place thanks to the dragons. One day, while she was flying alone above the sea with her dragon Stormfly, she was thinking "Oh man, nobody now pays attention to me anymore. Hiccup takes all the credits for himself. I'm sure he is not doing that on purpose, but still I can't help of seing him very happy every time the people of Berk complimented him. Man I wish there is someway for me to do to change all this, to make me back on top of the hill." And when she said those words she noticed something glowing on a very little island "Wow what is that? Stormfly down" said Astrid and so her dragon flied down. As soon as she landed on the island, Astrid got down from stormfly and looked for the source of that light. After a few seconds she found it and once she took a closer look at it she was speechless. It was

Astrid didn't know what to think and then she decided to try it out "All right let's see... Got it!" After that Astrid closed her eyes I wish that Stormfly become all red" then Astrid opened her eyes and looked at her dragon.

Astrid was really curious about the gem and wondered what to do, but it was getting late and she was a few miles away from the village, so she flew Stormfly to the Village and then let Stormfly fly off for the night to rest and Astrid walks into the village.p

1111111.content:
Stormfly caught sight of her own tail she flicked it around, and took Astrid's feet from her. As she lay on her back, Stormfly trotted over until her face was looming over Astrid's own. The dragon was looking a little confused and even annoyed. There was something deeper that was different too. Astrid just couldn't quite put her finger on it. All she had changed was Stormfly's colour, but she looked .... was she bigger, more muscular ....more aggressive. Her lip curled up at the edge and a menacing wisp of smoke emerged.

"Okay girl. I can take a hint." She grasped the gem firmly. "I wish Stormfly was back to her normal colouring."

Astrid watched carefully this time. Stormfly returned to herself, she was almost purring in delight and nudged her head playfully against Astrid's chest.

"You know I just wish I could understand dragons better." she muttered under her breath, glad her friend wasn't angry at her anymore.

She hadn't been entirely serious, but as Astrid watched, she rather liked the sister she saw developing there. As Hicca replaced Hiccup, Astrid felt her bond shift felt her relationship change.

With a soft groan, a realisation hits her. She's a year younger than Hicca, and perhaps things haven't changed as much as she'd have liked. Their father Stoick loved them both. This she knew bone-deep, and undeniably. However, as the first-born Hicca was the one being put forward whenever the opportunity arose. Whenever a responsibility was to be shared.
111111211.title: Sister Hicca...

choices: Astrid tries to over-rule Hiccup as the bigger sister.
Hiccup is eager to greet and bond with the dragon.
Fishlegs is already there, and taking notes.
Snotlout greets them both as if they were potential partners...
1111112112.content:
Astrid felt herself gaining a few extra inches. Gaining a few inches height and in some other places too. As first born, she'd be the one trusted to lead the village. Hiccup could be free. She told herself, free to invent and earn that dismissive viking name.

The gem glowed in Astrid's palm, as it strained to comply. Only a final flare of light coming from it before, the light vanished, and it looked a plain, piece of quartz. Looking about the home, she recognised the chief's place... Well, hers now.... hers and Hiccups.

Spotting her little sister snuggling in the furs, the nearby slab of stone covered by Toothless. She spotted her own bed. The stone slab for Stormfly at the foot of her bed, too.

Hiccup turned restlessly, moaning softly in her sleep, as if facing a nightmare. Kissing her forehead she settled, and Astrid went to bed.

The dull gemstone, she tucked beneath the bed. It didn't show any promise, but she didn't want to lose it... just yet.

- - - - -

The night passed quickly, with her waking to the smell of bacon being fried for breakfast. Looking sideways, Hiccup was yawning and stretching, "Morning Sis..." she said brightly, spotting Astrid's gaze.

The sound of Stoick humming a tune to himself downstairs made Astrid happier than she'd ever been. With both her parents dead, even having a father felt like a luxury.

"Breakfast is ready." the voice calling through, as the humming stopped.

She felt selfish, and a little guilty. But as she took in the new female Hiccup, she was still the same person that Astrid remembered. Still happy, inventive and rather petite. As a young viking woman, she wasn't scrawny, or weak... but petite definitely suited her.

Compared to some of the viking women, Astrid knew she wasn't bulky herself, but she towered a head over her sister, rather than vice versa. It was an odd sensation. Both dragons playfully scuffled before racing out the front door to hunt for their own breakfast.

Fed and watered, they headed down to the arena. Hiccup eager to find out more about the dragon they'd discovered and recovered the previous night. After a restful night it should be calmer, and more willing to accept help for it's injuries.

They chatted about how to gain the dragon's confidence, and how to feed it, help it, and learn from it.

They reached the arena... Well, dragon training academy now.

https://66.media.tumblr.com/1f5318ca7116...

It's a passionate discussion, with them both having particular ideas.

111111221111111211122212.content:
Astrid squeezed the stone tightly as she visualised the changes she wanted.

"Obviously I'd love to show you the dragons... But they can react badly to men. I know it can be hard to tell with our names. But didn't daddy.... sorry Stoick tell you all us dragon riders are women."

Runa's face took on a slightly confused look, as the magic went to work. However after a second, she shook her head, and her usual open smile returned to her face.

Ruff and Tuff, were now identical twin sisters. Astrid just re-imagined them as bimbos, Ruff remained largely the same. However, for Tuff the changes were deeper. She lost a few inches, and dressed like her sister, but with accents in blue rather than green.

While Fishlegs was a rather stocky girl, but not without her charms. Her intelligence and friendly nature remained.

But as Astrid thought of Snotlout, she recalled all the sexist and offensive comments that he'd made. She considered how he'd manage having to face them on a daily basis.

Snotlout lost his stubby nose, replaced with a delicate button nose. She gained some razor sharp cheek bones, and entrancing wide limpid pools for eyes. They were a fascinating emerald, and the talk of the village when she was born.

Astrid concentrated harder, as she imagined Snotlout as a shapely beauty. The sort of girl that had the guys falling over themselves to help, who wasn't allowed to even attempt any task.

She found a foul taste filling her stomach as she wrought the changes. With a realisation, she released the stone, allowing it to fall to the bottom of her pocket.

11111122111111121112221211.content:
Grinding her teeth, Astrid saw just how annoying Snotlout could be. As she manipulated Isaac. So Astrid decided that Snotlout should take on more feminine characteristics.

Suddenly Snotlout's expression softened, and she looked slightly scared, her gaze shifting from Isaac and back to Runa and Astrid. Her eyes were begging for help, from her friends. She slouched her shoulders, and tried to minimise her curves. Nervously she twirled the ends of her hair around her fingers. She was well used to the boys of Berk. Few of them could look her in the face. Their attention rather obviously focused on her chest. Snotlout recalled the one time, she snapped and demanded Nero look at her face when he was talking to her.

He blushed the brightest red, flatly denying that he'd been ogling her body, before fleeing the scene. The whole situation was unsettling, as she'd been friends with him for years, growing up together, and hanging out together.

Looking on, Astrid was horrified to see that Isaac was clearly more interested in this insecure viking girl, than the brash Snotlout. While she was looking to Astrid and Runa for help. Isaac was enjoying her well padded ass. His gaze would snap up towards her face as soon as he detected the merest hint of a turn.

Astrid was still trying to find a good solution when Runa piped up.

"Would you like to see our dragons." she smiled and nodded to Astrid and Snotlout.

The three girls were still all very proud of their friends, and more than happy to introduce them to a stranger.
11111122111111121112221211.title: Make a nicer and shy girl

choices: "Would you like to feed her?" Astrid asks.
Astrid feels for the gem in her pocket.
She rather likes how Isaac's hand grip her arms.
111111221111111211122212112.content:
Astrid leads them to where she left Stormfly with her head buried in a barrel of fish. Her tail was flicking from side to side as she gulped down her meal.

Astrid called out, and her head popped up, a couple of fish still hanging from her open mouth. Looking around Stormfly recognises her rider, and quickly swallows them. She trotted over, and started to purr, a deep resonating sound when Astrid scratched her under the chin.

Isaac did a good job of not running away. But he moved to Astrid's side.... sort of behind her.

The dragon started snuffling and sniffing, taking in the scent of the new arrival. Her nostrils flared and shifted as she did.

"She's not ... not going to ..."

"No... No." Astrid reassured him.

Snotlout piped up, "He's not very brave, for a real viking." her voice clear in Runa's ear.

"He'd make a better viking wife, than any of us." he added. Snotlout's dad, Spitelout, had been looking for a husband for his daughter. An idea that didn't sit well with her. She didn't want to settle down into the role of a viking wife, the daily routine, and the grinding monotony. She also didn't want to give up Hookfang.

Her voice was a little too loud this time though. Runa scowled as she saw Isaac's face flush. Astrid quickly hid a smirk, and Snotlout didn't care whether she was heard or not.
111111221111111211122212112.title: Isaac appears intimidated by the dragons.

choices: Astrid uses the gem to help Isaac.
"Perhaps, you could be the first male dragon rider." Runa suggested.
In the warmth of the dragon stables they have a quiet chat.
1111112211111112111222121121.content:
Snotlout looks around the unfriendly faces, and makes her exit. Not without flashing Isaac her lovely ass as she mounts up on Hookfang. She settled onto the saddle making great play of wiggling deeper. Leaning forward, she showed more of her rounded and generously, peach of an arse. She took a glance over her shoulder, catching the eye of Isaac and making him blush in the process.

With a single bound, and a cry Hookfang leaps into the air and soars away.

"She's right though." Isaac confides in the two girls.

"I am my father's youngest." he looks shame-faced, "But, I'm his eldest son.... his only son."

Astrid is aware how girls can cruelly ostracise others. Her own childhood memories were not often happy, her choice in life to fight to be seen as a warrior... a real warrior meant that she didn't have many of the other girls as friends growing up. She saw the pain in his eyes, and recognised that he'd probably been bullied by his sisters his entire life.

"Ulrich wants me to lead the clan after him."

Runa looked at him with sympathy. It was an idea that Stoick was trying to get her used to, as well.
1111112211111112111222121121.title: "Would you like to feed her?" Astrid asks.

choices: Isaac wants to ride Stormfly with Astrid.
Isaac isn't sure about the wiseness of this choice.
Isaac confesses his father is looking for a bride for him.
11111122111111121112221211212.content:
"Just because we're all girls, doesn't mean that all riders have to be female." Runa added trying to sound optimistic.

"Wouldn't that secure your place as the next leader...."

At that point Stormfly's tongue snuck out and licked Isaac's hand.

"She likes me...." he said excitedly. Turning towards Astrid for confirmation.

Astrid was distractedly fidgeting with the gem. She let it drop deeper into her pocket.

"Yeah... Cool..." she added distractedly, not having been paying attention.

She didn't like how quickly Stormfly was bonding with Isaac.

"What type of Dragon would be a good match for him?" Runa asked.

"That's as much a choice for the dragon as the rider." Fishlegs said, startling the three of them with her arrival.

"Although if we go to Dragon Island, then we can easily see..." She played with her hair, as the others turned their attention towards her. Even now, she was shy, only comfortable talking about her wide knowledge of dragons brought her out of her shell.

She hugged Meatlug, "What do you say, girl?" rubbing her behind the stubby wings. She licked the rider and let her mount up.

"Ya finished eating girl?" Astrid asked, leaping onto Stormfly's back.

Runa was as ever always ready to fly, an itch that never truly left her. With a solid -snick- her artificial foot clipped on and she mounted up. She reached forward and scratched Toothless beneath his chin. A deep purring groan came from his mouth.
11111122111111121112221211212.title: "Perhaps, you could be the first male drago...

choices: Barf and Belch arrive with the twins.
When they land Fishlegs spots some unusual tracks.
111111221111111211122212112121.content:
Isaac looked between the three mounted riders. The squat dragon with the curvaceous girl didn't look strong enough to carry another body. He turned his gaze towards Runa, but was unsettled by the darkness of the dragon, besides he wasn't sure he trusted Runa to be as safe a flyer with her leg.

The choice was easy for him to make, he caught Stormfly's eye, and -well- she seemed to approve.

As Astrid reached out her arm, she helped him to mount up. He settled down behind her on the dragon's muscular neck. It shifted beneath him in a rather unsettling way. So he quickly grabbed onto Astrid.

"Hey... Hey... not so tight." Astrid objected quickly.

"And keep your hands a little lower too." she added. Runa couldn't tell who was blushing more Astrid or Isaac, but before he could decide Stormfly kicked off and with a powerful sweep of her wings was airborne.

Astrid was feeling a little self-conscious as she'd aged the riders a few years. The extra years had given her a more generous set of curves. But still keeping her taut and toned body, athletic and lithe. Even now, she felt a little insecure, looking over to Runa, who already being a year older was more generously shaped. She snorted, even as Hiccup, he'd been less fit than her. Although as Runa she had to admit to a degree of jealousy about Runa's body.

Meatlug wasn't far behind the steady purring of his wings as he launched.

Runa leaned forward, "Let's show them, how fast a dragon can really fly." she whispered to Toothless's ears.

He took a quick couple of steps and launched. With his wings extended and paws lifted, he flew. Just inches above the grass he quickly caught up with and overtook Meatlug, and with a whoosh, passed beneath the claws of Stormfly.

The three dragons soared onwards, and flew over the cliff. With a yelp, Isaac announced his fear, looking downwards didn't help as the sea raced past a hundred or so feet beneath them.

Astrid didn't tell him off for the sudden clutching at her waist. With a chuckle she recalled her own first dragon flight. Toothless had given her a terrifying first experience. Although it had left her excited and eager for more, with an enthusiasm that stayed with her now.

She talked to Isaac the entire flight, to keep him distracted, telling him of the adventures of the riders. All the while she made sure that Stormfly flew steadily and ignored Runa's swooping passes and displays. Everytime she was up, she wanted to push her dragon to her potential, it seemed a shame to hold her back. But now wasn't the time for such indulgences. Stormfly seemed to appreciate the novice rider too, and kept her wings fully extended and flew very smoothly.

Dragon Island appeared on the horizon, and she pointed it out to Isaac.

As she looked over her shoulder she saw the sense of wonder and magic written all over his face. He was transfixed by the flight, by the beauty.... by the whole situation.

He pulled in close to her, "I can never thank you enough for this.... this whole experience...."

Shyly he gave her a kiss, a chaste peck on the cheek.

Astrid flinched in surprise and turned her gaze to the front, unsure how to react.
111111221111111211122212112121.title: Isaac wants to ride Stormfly with Astrid.

choices: "It's beautiful." Isaac added, automatically reaching out to it's nose.
Toothless, Stormfly and Meatlug move to surround the new arrival.
There's a bellow from above, and a whoosh of wings.
1111112211111112111222121121213.content:
Oddly it's Fishlegs, who leads the riders downwards.

"Runa.... I think I saw something...."

Snotlout muttered some comment under her breath. But Runa directed Toothless to follow Fishlegs, and landed with grace beside Meatlug. As Astrid touched down, it was clear that none of the dragons were very happy. Agitatedly they looked around, snapping at the air.

Gesturing to the sky. "Something's Up. See if you can scout it out... see what's upset the dragons."

"Huh... Oh right." Answered Tuffnut.

"She's talking to me." barked Ruff, trying to head-butt her sister.

"You go down the beach, and I'll head up." barked Snotlout to the twins. She pointed them in the right direction before heading off.

Fishlegs was on his hands and knees, examining a print in the sand.

"See. See. I knew I saw something." she was nodding as she looked to Runa and Astrid for confirmation.

"Perhaps it'd be safest if you took Isaac away." Runa suggested.

However, Astrid wasn't going to be deterred by danger. So she slid off, Stormfly, and gave Isaac a hand as he jumped / fell from the back of the dragon.

He landed off balance, and toppled forward. As he went he landed on top of Astrid, he head fell between her breasts. A pained groan came from his lips.

As Astrid realised how he'd landed she gave him a tap on the helmet with her axe... in a friendly fashion...

Isaac sat back and shook his head, still unsteady he put a hand out.

It landed where it wasn't appreciated. Astrid grabbed him by the wrist and giving it a twist, she threw him off her body.

Seeing Runa observe this made her blush rather more than she liked. Even more when Isaac finally worked out how he'd ended in the sand, and where his hand had been resting.

For discretion's sake, she turned back towards Fishlegs.

"Can you tell what it is?" Runa asked hopefully.

"Err... Runa... Yeah... I think I can..."

She turned and crashed into Runa, as the two of them clutched each other. The reason for Fishlegs' fear became obvious... became visible... as a changewing dropped it's camouflage.

It's hot breath hissing just inches from their faces. There was a gentle gurgling from it's stomach and a pale emerald glow deep in it's mouth. Runa knew that it's vitriol was ready to fly.

"It's a changewing." said Fishlegs, pointing unnecessarily.

"Just get slowly to your feet." Astrid advised Isaac. Who appeared rather hypnotised into immobility.
1111112211111112111222121121213.title: When they land Fishlegs spots some unusual tracks.

11111122111111121112221211212131.content:
Runa watched in horror as she listened to the gurgling of the Changewings venom. But then she noticed something odd, perhaps it was intuitive, but Isaac wasn't making eye contact or any sudden movements, or any of the things that might upset or scare the dragon.

The whole situation was too dangerous for her to do anything to interfere, as she watched from the corner of her eye. Until finally there was snuffling contact, and the dragon's snout rested in the open outstretched hand. The large nostrils flared and a puff of grey smoke came out.

However, it was clear the danger had passed.

"Looks like we have a natural." Astrid hissed to Runa.

The gurgling from the dragon took on a more relaxed tone, almost like purring a 25 feet long cat purring. The smile on Isaac face was impossible to remove as he beamed from ear to ear.

He nodded to Runa. Runa nodded back.

When he nodded again, Runa paid more attention. She directed her gaze down the beach.

There wasn't anything to see at first. But as she stared, a shimmering became visible. A small pile of rocks wasn't just a small pile of rocks. Before their eyes a baby changewing dropped it's disguise and took on it's natural deep red hue.

Fishlegs hissed in sympathy, it's wing was trapped under some stones. From where the vikings stood it was hard to tell if it was trapped or injured. However pain was written on the young one's face.

Astrid moved towards it, but stopped instantly as a warning hiss emanated from the dragons.

"I think she wants me to help." Isaac suggested.

Astrid wishes that Isaac and Hiccup fall in love
111111221111111212.content:
Both of the girls groaned. However, when Isaac came walking up, his tall, broad, Viking frame and traditional Viking hair made both of the girls catch their breath. He was very handsome. Both girls were brought back to reality though, when he stepped up and frowned at them. He then looked at Stoic and his father as he said, "Is this the best breeding stock that Berk has to offer?"

Both girls turned bright red. Stoick knew they were ready to go for their axes, and as big as he was, Isaac would never survive both of them! "Girls! STOP! Go inside while I talk to Ulrich and his son." Astrid stomped in, wanting to wring that smug, Viking neck, and being her twin sister, Hiccup was too!

Astrid thought about what to do. She could make sure that Hiccup is selected. Maybe even make her happy about it. Or, a new thought entered her mind, she could make herself male. That way she would not only be forced to marry some idiot from another island, but would be the next Chief. But did she really want to give up being female?
111111221111111212.title: They both groan

choices: Astrid tries to figure out how undo what she has done
1111112211111112122.content:
The thought of becoming the next Chief of Berk appeals to Astrid, but she is not sure she is quite ready to become male yet. So she grabs the stone and wishes that Isaac and Hiccup would fall deeply in love the next time they see each other.

Just as she put the stone back, Stoick walked in with a look on his face just daring his daughters to speak. They both knew better. He stared at them a while then said, "Look girls, we have had this conversation before. We need treaties of cooperation with other islands, and since you are females, the best way to do that is to have a marriage that unites our peoples. Cass is especially important, since they are our closest neighbor. Now, come back out and mind your manners. And if one of you won't volunteer to marry the boy, I will pick one of you. I really hope that I won't have to do that." He waited and said, "Now come back outside, and this time, keep your manners."

The girls looked stubborn, but followed their father out. Fortunately for Astrid, the wish worked, and as soon as Hiccup looked at Isaac again, she almost swooned, and veered into her sister! Isaac was clearly smitten with her as well.

Stoick had them introduce themselves. Astrid stepped up and said, "I am Astrid, daughter of Stoick, and rider of Stormfly." Her gaze held no love for the tall man in front of her. In contrast, Hiccup stepped forward shyly. After a few moments, she finally said, "I, um, I am Hiccup, um, daughter of Stoick, and, um, rider of Toothless." Astrid couldn't believe it, but she was actually blushing!

When Issac saw her, he was he grinned, "If she is willing, I have chosen. Hiccup, would you be the mother of my children?"

To everyone's surprise, she jumped up, throwing her arms around him, and said happily, "I would be honored to bear your children!" Then they kissed passionately!
1111112211111112122.title: Astrid wishes that Isaac and Hiccup fall in love

11111122111111121222.content:
Astrid was walking around the village trying to figure out how to undo some of this. She liked being the Chief's daughter now. She didn't really want to give that up. But she was feeling very guilty about what she had don to Hiccup/Runa. She probably shouldn't have turned Hiccup into a girl. She only made it worse by panicking and making her sister fall in love with that handsome jerk. But if she turned Hiccup back to a male, she could wind up married to him instead.

She didn't know the full extent of the gem's power. She could turn Issac into a girl, and Hiccup back to a male, but what if the range didn't go to Issac's home island. That could be a problem!

She wasn't paying attention where she was going when suddenly she bumped into somebody and suddenly was flying through the air. She landed with a thud!

She shook her head and saw __________ standing there, and worse yet, he was holding the gem!
11111122111111121222.title: Astrid tries to figure out how undo what she ha...

111111221111111212221.content:
Snotlout grinned down at Astrid as he held the gem, "I like that pose Astrid. I wish we were married and that is how you welcomed me home each night." The gem glowed in his hand.

Astrid panicked, what pose? Then she realized that when she had fallen, she had thrown her legs and feet out to get balance. As she landed on her back, her legs were widely spread! Then, to her horror, she saw her armor fade away to be replaced by clothing more common to married women on Berk. Also, her braided pigtails were replaced by her long hair pulled back into a pony-tail, also common to the younger married women of Berk. She was no longer a shield maiden. She was Snotlout's wife!

She saw the shocked look on his face as he saw her transformation. Then he noticed the braided band of hair on his wrist. It was the custom for the bride to braid a band made from her hair, and give it to her husband on their wedding day as a symbol of her devotion to him. He stuttered, "It, it, it worked. We 'are' married!" He looked at his hand, "This gem grants wishes?"

Astrid jumped up, "That is mine!! Give it back!"

He easily held her away, "You know Astrid, I wish you wouldn't fight me, but be a totally obedient wife."

Just like that, Astrid stopped struggling with him. "Please Snotlout! We can't be married! Please return my gem!"

He pulled her close and kissed her passionately. To her dismay, she couldn't help kissing him back! She also did not complain or try to stop him when he grabbed or massaged different, intimate parts of her body. Inside she was screaming, but on the outside, she was his wife, so it was okay.

He kissed her again, "This is going to be fun. Let's..."
111111221111111212221.title: Snotlout

1111112211111112122212.content:
Snoutlout looked at Astrid with no attempt to hide his lust, "Well, since we are married now, let's go find our house. Then let's go inside and start making babies."

Astrid was in turmoil on the inside. This CAN'T be happening! She just can't stay married to a smelly slob like Snotlout! She HAD to find a way to get that gem back! And she better do it soon, or she might really get pregnant by Snotlout! That thought made her sick! She couldn't show it though. His wish made sure that she had to act like a good wife and not fight him.

So when they found their cottage, they both walked in as if they owned the place, which they did of course. It was also a horrid mess! This was no shock, since Astrid always wanted to be a shield maiden, not a housewife, so it was doubtful she put much effort into the home in that way. Combined with Snotlout's slob-like ways, it even smelled. It was so bad that even Snotlout thought it was a smelly mess! He looked at Astrid, "Wow! I never appreciated what mom did at home. I wish that, from now on, you can't stand a mess in this house, and when you are not doing something to please me, you will work to make this the neatest, cleanest house in Berk."

Astrid shuddered in fear as he spoke, but when he was done, she felt a tingle in her head. When it was done, she was so overwhelmed with disgust for the condition of their home that she had to run outside and throw up! When Snotlout called her back in, he said, "Now, let's get to know each other a lot better."

She couldn't resist him, but the thought of making love to the filthy Snotlout on 'that' filthy bed was now sickening to her, so she begged, "Please don't do this! Especially not now! Please! At least let me clean up first!" She couldn't believe she was saying that. Until his wish, she hated cleaning and housework!

He smiled, "Time enough for that. And from now on, call me 'My Love' when you address me. In private or public." And he held her close and began to kiss her with great passion!

When they were done, Astrid cried in deep sobs over the thought that it was now possible she could have Snotlout's child! For his part though, the thought of being married to Astrid, and having her bear his children, was the fulfillment of his greatest fantasy.

He got up and dressed, "I am going to go flying. I will be back for the evening meal. Make sure it is ready on time." And with that, he walked out the door.

Without Snotlout to please, Astrid soon found herself compelled by his last wish to, first clean herself up, and then dig in and start cleaning their filthy house! She knew it was the wish, but it didn't matter, she couldn't bear to see her house this dirty!

It was an exhausted young woman who reluctantly began to cook the evening meal. He had told her to have it ready when he returned home, so even though she was wanting to lay down and rest, she couldn't.

The food was ready when Snotlout came walking in. He looked around, "Wow! It looks a lot better! Come here and let me thank you." She walked up to him. She couldn't help it. And even though he smelled awful, she returned his kiss as he kissed her.

They ate, and then he said, "Come here woman, and tell me that you want me to make love to you."

Of course, she did. She had no choice. And after he was done, he fell asleep. Astrid cleaned up after the meal, then cleaned until she passed out!

The next morning, she woke up on the bed. At first, she thought that it was all some terrible nightmare. But looking around at her personal items mixed with Snotlout's, she realized to her horror that it really was true. She really was Snotlout's wife! She had to find a way to escape that fate before it was too late!

She made herself some breakfast, then searched her husband's things as she continued to clean the house. Unfortunately for her, after almost three days of cleaning, she had only found blisters and exhaustion! And worse, of course, was that every time Snotlout came home, he made love to her over and over.

Finally, she needed to go to the market and buy them some food. She was still the Cheif's daughter, but as the wife of Snotlout, people were upset with her. After a while, she understood what was wrong. She was the eldest daughter, and by marrying Snotlout, as her husband, he would be in line as the next chief! She had to admit, that was a scary thought!

At the market, she ran into...

choices: Making sure night furries don't end, and strange results
11111122111112.content:
Now that Astrid had changed Hiccup to be her twin sister, she couldn't bring herself to change who Hiccup was on the inside. She used the gem to make sure that she was the first-born of Stoic, and that Hiccup's name was now Ingrid. However, only herself and Ingrid would remember the existence of Hiccup. Even Toothless would only remember Ingrid. And just so that she wouldn't feel too odd, Astrid made sure that Ingrid was attracted to males, and only felt normal in female clothing. She also made sure that her sister could only use or respond to the name of Ingrid. Then she released her from the trance that she was in.

She shook her head, then took in the changes. Astrid found it a bit amusing to watch her feel her body, especially her missing manhood. At last, she looked at Astrid, "What have you done to me? Why did you do this?"

Astrid smiled, "I couldn't help it. As the first-born daughter, I will become the queen some day. If I left you a male, you would have been king no matter what." She smirked, "Besides. Admit it. Your small body and peaceful spirit is more suited to a girl than a viking warrior anyhow."

Ingrid sputtered, but for some reason couldn't deny that last part was true. "It is still not right. You have no right to do this to me."

Astrid smiled, "Look. You really are more suited to being a female. I will make you a deal. We will walk to the dragons. If you don't find a single male attractive as we walk through the village, I will change you back. But if you do. Even one. Then you will have to stay as you are until the winter solstice."

Hiccup/Ingrid reluctantly agreed, "Oh! Blast you! Okay! Be ready to change me back as soon as we get there though!"

Astrid smiled at she agreed. With the wish she made, her poor sister would never make it without finding a male attractive! It did make her think of one thing though. One of the things that had made Hiccup and Toothless such a good team was the bond they shared. To make sure that continued, she wished Toothless to also be female, and for Ingrid and Toothless to have a very special connection between them. So even to Hiccup/Ingrid, toothless had always been female as well.

When there were in the village, they ran into Erik. A prince who had recently been rescued. He was the last of his tribe. He was tall, strong, and very handsome. To her dismay, Hiccup/Ingrid was very, very, attracted to him! She looked over at her sister, and turned red in in a blush as she saw Astrid looking at her. So there was no denying it. She was attracted to males! Maybe Astrid was right. Maybe she was better off female.

She didn't say anything as they got to the dragons. Hiccup/Ingrid was deep in thought as she saddled Toothless.Astrid let her alone. For her part, Ingrid was amazed at how much closer she felt to Toothless. It was like they understood each other's thoughts and feelings.

When they flew, Ingrid found it a strange feeling to sit with her legs spread in the saddle and have no pressure in her crotch. It was odd, but she soon forgot about it as she flew with Toothless. It was strange, but wonderful, that since becoming female, she seemed more in tune with Toothless. It was like Toothless knew what she wanted to do without her saying it, or giving a signal. Maybe it was because they were both female now. If so, it might be worth while for her to stay female permanently.

Seeing Ingrid flying Toothless, Astrid was inspired. What she had done so far was selfish, and she was feeling bad about that. She could use this gem for good. And seeing them flying together, Astrid realized that Toothless was the last of the night furies. They are beautiful creatures. It would be a terrible loss to see them go extinct. As they were exploring a new island, Astrid saw a lone dragon. It was perfect for her plan. With one wish, she turned that dragon into a night fury. A MALE night fury! With the next wish, she made sure the Toothless and he would not only be attracted to each other, but have an intense desire to produce many offspring. That would ensure the return of night furies.

She got the new night fury to follow her. Sure enough, when they encountered Ingrid and Toothless, it was love at first sight. Toothless almost threw Ingrid off as she greeted the newcomer. When they settled down, Astrid flew next to Ingrid and said, "Isn't this incredible? Another night fury!"

Ingrid seemed to shake herself out of a trance. She smiled a bit distantly, "Yeah. We love him."

Astrid looked a bit confused, "We?"

Ingrid shook her head, looking confused too, "I, um, I meant Toothless loves him. Astrid, she wants to mate with him! I think we better head back."

They did, and Astrid thought that Ingrid acted strangely the whole way back. When they had landed, in a private cove, Ingrid unsettled Toothless, then took a ride back with Astrid.

They were back in the village, getting ready to go to eat, and Ingrid was mooning over Erik, when suddenly, Ingrid got a strange look on her face. She turned bright red, and seemed to be sweating.

Astrid looked at her, "Ingrid. What is wrong?"

Ingrid shook her head, "I feel such a bond with Toothless that..."

11111212.content:
After the first failed experiment, Astrid considers throwing it away. But, as she turns it over in her palm, she remembers its' beauty. Even if it's not one of the fabled gems, it's still cute. Tucking it into her belt pouch, she mounts Stormfly.

Shaking her head, that she believed in magic stones. She kicked her heels into Stormfly's flank, and with a powerful lunge, she took to the air. Astrid mused on what she 'knew' of the magic gems. She was sure she'd read something about them when she was younger.

A challenge she'd given herself, to stand out as a young viking woman. She didn't fancy the life of a viking wife as her future. She studied secretly, and trained with an axe.

- - - - -

By the time, she returned to the village, she had more questions than answers. Visiting Gothi's hut, she found one of the few bookshelves in the entire village. One of the book spines seemed to call out to her, and she took it down.

Hiding beneath a book on medicinal herbs, she stole it from Gothi's hut. She didn't want to be caught borrowing a childrens' book, or whatever it was that she recalled.

She headed to the Grand Hall to read it, hoping to get lost in the crowd of drinkers, and smoky fireplaces.

- - - - -

She was somewhat relieved to find that it wasn't a collection of childrens' tales. Flicking through the pages, she even found a picture of the gem. Well, it looked roughly right, if drawn crudely in charcoal. Missing the beautiful shifting colours that moved in its' depths. It seemed to ensnare the senses and fascinate.

Astrid read what was guessed about the gem. It told of arguments between Loki and Thor, as if they were siblings. As if they were real. Astrid turned it over in her hand, and wondered if it were simply a beautiful stone that inspired the story. She didn't really believe in Thor or Loki, though she did want to believe in Freya. They were stories to inspire and she took them as such.

However, as she felt a heavy, chubby hand land on her shoulder. Astrid didn't need to turn around to know who was there.

"Greetings, Astrid."

Snotlout's voice grated, patronising and dismissive. He found no interest in books, or reading. But knew the role of a viking woman, and a viking man. She wondered how he'd feel about those roles if they were reversed. If he had to marry to own anything, or be respected.

His smile was indulgent. His confidence misplaced and arrogant. She knew his feelings towards her, and his intentions. Frankly, it made her skin crawl to even imagine him as a husband, anyone's let alone hers...
11111212.title: She uses the gem on Snoutlout.

choices: Behind Sif's eyes, Snotlout still lurks...
Astor pressed the kiss, as Snotlout vanished from Sif's eyes.
Astor remembers, but Sif & everyone else has forgotten Snotlout.
Astor felt the gem cool in his palm, as it fell forgotten to the floor.
111112123.content:
It wasn't intentional, but Astrid immediately felt the change. Even as nothing else in the whole hall seemed to change. Well, nothing else apart from herself, and Snotlout. The stone warmed in her clenched fist, or rather his clenched fist.

She felt stronger, warmer, bigger, and male. While Snotlout was now looking at him beyond the swell of a very impressive and well displayed bust.

- - - - -

Snotlout's eyes widened in shock, and her bust rose, as she drew breath to release a deafening scream. Astrid took the opportunity, to snake one arm around the newest viking wench's waist. Pulling the startled female Snotlout into his lap, she smothered that scream with a kiss.

Snotlout squirmed and struggled in his grasp. But she had no real chance of escaping Astrid's grip.

'Astor' her mind absently corrected, she was Astor now, and the struggling Snotlout, was 'Sif Revna'.

- - - - -

Astor felt the dancing of Sif's bust against his own firm chest. It had an odd effect on him, and Sif's struggling intensified, as Astor's body responded, naturally.

Still unfamiliar with a wench in his lap, Astor was stiffening, and Sif could feel it. Her eyes were glistening with fear, incomprehension, vulnerability and confusion.

Despite his recent familiarity with female flesh, Astor still found his gaze drawn to the supple flesh on display. It was just so soft, so mobile... and so abundant.

Part of Astor knew Sif was bustier than Astrid had been. Triggering a battle between jealousy, and something else.

As the kiss extended, the confusion faded from Sif's eyes. She spoke silently with her gaze.
111112123.title: Astrid looked up to see a beautiful maiden.

choices: Some instinct gripped Astor, and he slipped the gem inside her!?
Astor knew exactly what to do, to tease and please Sif.
Hiccup turned up like a wet blanket to spoil his fun...
The pair left, for more privacy and a midnight walk.
1111121233.content:
As the kiss extended, Astor felt more of the viking eyes falling on him. He spotted how Sif's lashes fluttered, as she clearly fantasized about being taken.

Astor still wasn't ready to speak, as he organised his thoughts. Taking in the framing of her fur trimmed neckline. Astor had her own questions. Why wasn't Sif chilly, those things looked too exposed, to the icy wind, or the chill rain, for that outfit to be practical.

As he felt Sif leaning into the kiss, she was sure that any remaining trace of Snotlout had vanished. He knew Sif was happy to have a brave viking man like Astor choose her. He still wondered about the practicality of her outfit.

Wondering if it came from Astrid's nightmares, or Snotlout's fantasies. He snorted, as he broke apart. Certain that sore shoulders and an aching back lay in Sif's future. Raising a tankard of beer, he chugged it down, until it was empty.

The back of his hand serving to wipe away the remaining foam. It felt odd to discover a beard with that hand, or to see the fascination that Sif had for the simplest of his gestures. She looked obsessed, and fixated, just the way Snotlout had wanted his wife to be... Adoring and obedient.

Some instinct guided him. A firm hand slapping onto Sif's soft, firm rounded rump. The sound seeming to echo around the room. He guided her to keep her on his lap.

She squirmed eagerly, grinding against Astor's stiffening appreciation of her supple form. There was a thumping of tankards, a chorus of approval. Almost like a recognition of his possession of her!

Astor's fingers opened, and he took in the stone. It looked duller now, the internal glow now reduced to a bare flicker.

Sif sensing her man's distraction, guided his hand. Astor almost recoiled in shock, as he realised where Sif was guiding his hand. Her legs spreading subtly in anticipation.

Astrid watched with satisfaction as Hiccia purred and rubbed her head against Toothless' chest, and occasionally licked the underside of his head.

Toothless looked smug, giving some nearby dragons who had landed nearby to see what was happening a look that seemed to say, 'She's mine. All mine, and no one else's. You jealous?'

Astrid smirked as an idea popped into her head, gripping the staff, she said, "Let Hiccia remember her life as Hiccup."

Hiccia's eyes widened, and her slitted pupils looked around, before realizing that her tongue was touching the bottom of Toothless' jaw.

Toothless was confused as to why she stopped, but before he could question it, Hiccia let away, shrieking.

Toothless looked at her concerned, but the sound of the nearby dragons snickering and laughing at him infuriated him, so he roared at them, "What are you looking at?"

Hiccia's eyes widened even more as she realized that she could understand Toothless.

While Toothless was roaring at the dragons, Astrid walked over to the new night fury and asked, "So, how does it feel to be a female night fury, Hiccup? Oops, I meant Hiccia."

Hiccia could only stare in shock at the viking girl.

Astrid continued, "Here's the thing, when you started taming dragons, I was no longer the best of our generation. Loki take it, I wasn't even second, that would be the dragon expert, Fishlegs. But I knew if something happened to you, then I would go back to the top. After I found this magic staff...well, it just seemed like poetic justice to do this to you."

Hiccia's reptilian eyes were brimming with tears.

Astrid noticed this, and continued, "Oh, don't worry. Believe it or not, I actually don't have any bad feelings towards you. So, I made sure that you would enjoy your new life. You always wanted to find new night furies, and now you can continue the species. Just think about it, all the night furies you could want, and they wouldn't exist if not for you. And Toothless, of course."

Astrid smiles, "Originally, I was going to make you forget your human life, and live as my loyal dragon, but now..."

111133313.content:
As she watched Hiccup, she admired him. Truly she did! But, there was something that triggered a jealousy deep inside her. She sat on the Pier, her legs kicking over the side.

- - - - -

It was how much harder she'd had it growing up. The dedication to training, she'd needed to work twice as hard to be taken half as seriously. Even now, some of the village women still dismissed it as a phase. A tomboy-ish streak she had to get out of her system.

She'd built up her muscles, and prowess despite everything. Her thoughts drifted, wondering if she'd had the opportunity to be the chief's son. To have had hiccup's chances, wouldn't she have grown into a more manly figure. Wouldn't she have become a greater champion and defender for the village.

Naturally, the thought of Hiccup growing up with her life also came to mind. Would she apply that fascinating mind to beautifying herself, and enhancing her femininity. Surely someone as slender as Hiccup would flourish as a graceful woman, rather than struggle to be accepted as a man.

- - - - -

Certainly, Astrid didn't expect any answers to these questions. The light caught on the waves, and the white crest. She snorted in amusement, able to assemble a vague face from the different patterns.

As she watched, it seemed to be clearer, more recognisable with every passing moment. The waves continued to roll, and rebound from the pier. But, the face seemed to remain despite the changes.

"Ægir..." she half muttered the name of the Sea god.

The face seemed to smile, answering her single word, as if it were a question. The face winked, before the waves erased any trace of humanity from their ebbing and flowing.

Startled, Astrid planted his hands heavily on the pier, and propelled himself to his feet. The strength was alien, and his arms bulged with muscles. Not the svelte toned arms, she'd had. But, bulging muscles, and true strength.

"Astor Haddock"

Instinctively, she looked around. Somehow, she knew that was his name. That everyone knew him.. HIM!, as the son of the Chief.

That shock was still filtering through his brain, as he spotted Hiccup sashay towards him. She was tall, and curvy. Slender without being weak, but blessed with a bust, and incredible hips, and a rump that shimmied.

Astor was still taking it in, still adjusting when Hicca Hofferson reached him. The kiss she planted on him, was possessive, and demanding. She was claiming her man before the whole village. Well, the ones near the pierside anyway.

1112.content:
A few years had passed since, the infamous battle of Dragon Island. Hiccup was now highly regarded amongst the tribe, although still considered ..mmm.. eccentric for a viking. He was frequently off exploring, finding new distant islands and documenting exciting new breeds of dragon.

Fishlegs would even accompany him on some adventures. He had a fine artistic eye and a deft hand for capturing detail. Snotlout was still a proud member of Berk's dragon riders. His father Spitelout was less impressed, as Snotlout was always Lieutenant to Hiccup's leader. Hell, some days he was even lower, following Astrid's ideas and plans.

This was less of a problem now, he told himself as Astrid was becoming more womanly. She was developing her own curves, and at the same time having to fight the traditional viking views of a woman's place in the tribe. A snarl crossed his face as he thought back to the looks that she cast in Hiccup's direction when she thought nobody was looking.

Stoick was clear in his desire that Hiccup follow him as the next leader. Encouraging him to work with everybody and molding him to become the next chief. Snotlout ground his teeth as the idea, it just wasn't the viking way. A chief should rule by strength... not by ... well, not by weakness and alliances.

Ruff and Tuff were curiously still the same, just older. Snotlout knew that Ruff was a woman, but her constant battling with Tuff. Well, it just never seemed that important. Besides the twins had no drive for leadership, at times he wondered if they ever had an idea that wasn't designed to antagonise their other half.

An idea started to form in Snotlout's brain, how he could solve both of these problems at the same time. If only Hiccup were as female as Astrid....

A woman couldn't be a chief.... that just didn't make sense.

Besides he rode a Monstrous Nightmare, whereas Hiccup had to make do with an injured dragon. Clearly he was the better viking. It all made sense. It was for the good of the tribe, for the good of the village. Hell, maybe over time Hiccup would come to thank Snotlout for her transformation.

Snotlout was also sure that a skinny and weak as Hiccup was as a male would mean that she'd become a beautiful, delicate flower of a woman.

But how to carry out such a plan....
1112.title: A scheme of Snotlout

11122.content:
Snotlout scuttled off to the Berk library, eager to find the way to sideline Hiccup. It galled him, that even the library was there because of Hiccup, as his eyes scanned over the spines of the two dozen books, he thought of abandoning his plan. But it was too good a plan, so he decided to talk to Fishlegs. As a viking he was a good librarian, (a terrible viking insult). Hell, he'd even authored some of those books himself.

After a few false starts he found Fishlegs and Meatlug on the beach on the far side of the island. Meatlug looked to be randomly blasting and eating rocks while his rider took notes. He waved at him, as he circled Hookfang around, looking for somewhere to land.

Snotlout and Fishlegs had never been the best of friends, but Fishlegs had bottomless reserves of goodwill. Snotlout hated that about him, such weakness. But he put a smile on and waved to him as he walked over.

"Working on a new book..." his voice trailed off, as he struggled to find the subject of any interest.

"Yes... I thought you might like this. We're testing the rocks to see what effects they have on Boulder class dragons." he rubbed Meatlug's stubby little ears affectionately.

Snotlout just pretended to listen, adding the occasional grunt and general agreement to keep him talking. Snotlout was happy for the chance to talk about dragons, and Meatlug in particular. After fifteen minutes or so, he almost snapped.

But with a snarl tried to change the subject instead.

"Yeah... yeah... Boulder class are really interesting. But... er... what about Stormfly... I mean what about ... Sharp class dragons."

He hadn't meant to mention Stormfly, but Astrid was a girl he found very interesting. An interest that went entirely unreturned. As a dragon rider he wasn't short of attention.

"I didn't know you were ... oh, Astrid's dragon ... " he gave Snotlout a sly wink.

"Have you tried Chicken... as an alternative to fish... If you want to win her favour, you will need to get on better with Stormfly. But...." he fell silent, and turned his gaze to the sky. It wasn't really a secret, that Astrid craved the eye of Hiccup, even if she were unwilling to admit it most days.

Perhaps the only vikings unaware of the simmering chemistry were Hiccup and Snotlout themselves.

With a sigh, he started talking about the poisonous spines of the Sharp-class dragons.

"Most immobilise their prey, allowing the dragon time to swoop down and capture their prey, or if necessary slow down an enemy and make their escape."

Snotlout grunted, settling on top of a rock in preparation for a long and boring discussion.

"Some spines are even deadly." he added, as if the name Deadly Nadder were unearned.

Fishlegs then started on about how long it took a dragon to replace a thrown spine.

Snotlout's chin slid off his fist as he listened distractedly. With a snort he distracted Fishlegs.

".... you know that Stormfly is a female." he finished saying, looking over to the bored viking, as if explaining a great secret.

"Yes, and Hookfang is male." he bit back his anger. This wasn't working, and it was frustrating. He was just about to insult the heavy viking and leave, when Fishlegs beckoned him closer with a wave of his chunky hand.

"I've not checked recently... but... she might not be female any longer..."

His ears pricked up at this. He didn't really care about dragon sex, but this sounded promising. He'd certainly never looked that closely.

"How would you find out... and why would she not be female any more..." his confusion written on his face.

The look made Fishlegs smile, even if he hid it from Snotlout.

He waved him closer. "Well, a nadder has a gland that can give it's spines the ability to change sex."

Snotlout lifted his head, barely able to contain his excitement, as Fishlegs chattered on about the advantages for breeding cycles and so forth.

"Will this work on other dragons?" Snotlout asked, genuine fascination clear in his voice.
11122.title: Snotlout made a few discreet inquieries of Fish...

111221.content:
Fishlegs is surprised by Snotlout's interest in any dragon apart from his own. But doesn't want to miss the opportunity to talk.

"Well you know how Stormfly is mostly blue with a plain white belly."

Snotlout just nodded along. Meatlug went on to describe the dark spine that would look out of place, even warning the young viking of the danger of plucking it.

Both from the affronted dragon, and the risk from the spine itself especially if he didn't wear gloves.

Already he was planning how to get close to Astrid, naturally using his charm and her fascination with his masculine viking virility. His chest puffed out, already it felt almost too easy.

But he waited another hour or so, listening to Fishlegs witter on endlessly about the other spines on a nadder and the effects they could have on a subject that was struck by them. He didn't want it to be too obvious, if Hiccup became a girl just after he'd asked about the effects of nadder spines. So he kept his interest as best he could occasionally piping in with a half-hearted question.

"Wow... fascinating.... truly fascinating." he said when he heard enough.

Fishlegs didn't pick up on the sarcasm, happy as he was to spread his dragon knowledge to every other flier, even Snotlout.

As Hookfang and Snotlout flew away, he tossed a pebble into Meatlug's gaping maw.

"You know he's not half bad, if you give him a chance, Meatlug."

Snotlout had to restrain himself from acting immediately. It was almost a physical pain to hold himself back from immediate action. It was unviking-like, he told himself. But after flying across the island, Hookfang was in need of a good sleep.

He went to bed and had a good sleep. In his dreams there was even a new figure, a new Hiccup, a female Hiccup... a rival with Astrid for the attention of the new chief in waiting.

He woke to a banging on the door.

Yawning and scratching he went to the door. Without opening he knew who it was. Hiccup.

"Time for the Dawn Patrol." Hiccup's happy voice carried through the thick wood.

"Perhaps a couple of spines through his bedroom window." Astrid suggested half-jokingly... but only half.

As he opened the door the chill air finished the job of waking him up.

Hiccup was already mounting up. Toothless eager to be away. The twins were knocking lumps out of one another as their dragon hovered in the air. On the other side of Hiccup were Astrid on Stormfly and Fishlegs and Meatlug. As ever the gronkle was the most stable of the dragons as they hovered around the viking standing in the doorway.

He took a moment to examine the nadder.

Astrid's control was excellent he hated to admit. But from this angle it was very clear which particular spine that Fishlegs had been describing. Spotting it put a smile on his face.

He leapt to Hookfang's back in a couple of bound, and landed ready to fly.

Hiccup was already leading from the front. They flew in a rough V-formation. With Toothless at the apex. Just to the left and slightly behind went Stormfly. She was followed by the twins on Barf and Belch. Forming a line from Toothless's right was only Meatlug. With a gap for his Monstrous Nightmare to fill.

Once in formation, they circled on the thermals rising higher until they dove in formation across the village, heading for the harbour before doing a complete circuit of the island.

111222.content:
"It's a survival thing." Fishlegs explained. "When there aren't enough females, the competition for mates can become rather rough, and for the losing male." He paused and blushed. "Well... He becomes a she."

Snotlout tried to hide his delight at this news, even as his imagination ran ahead of him he realised that Fishlegs was still talking.

"... Gunna keeps a supply."

"Gunna?"

"Yes, Snotlout." The frustration of the stocky viking was building. He hated being asked questions then ignored.

"I mean, it is a great cure-all despite the rather obvious side effect."

"Yeah.. Yeah. Cure all, .... side effects."

"Shall I say it again?"

Snotlout forced himself to sit down, and pay attention. It wasn't easy, but he forced himself.

"You can apply it as a poultice to even the most severe of wounds, and it will allow the person the chance to recover. The price is that it will put the patient in a deep sleep, and when she wakes, she'll be fully recovered."

"So Gobber..."

Fishlegs shrugged, "Well, Gobber doesn't mind his artificial leg and hand, and prefers to remain a man."

A snort of laughter escaped him, "Can you imagine the size of woman, he'd become." He nudged Snotlout playfully, cautiously, but playfully. He cupped his hands far in front of his own chest, cradling an imaginary bust.

"It all depends on the person's size, and imagination, apparently what dreams come when they're sleeping can change how a patient transforms, it's not down to merely their original size. I mean, if Hiccup needed the cure, he'd... -rather- she'd probably be fine. He's not carrying a lot of weight, that needs to find a new home, and he's a restrained sort."

He stopped, and looked Snotlout over.

"Of course, you'd become a lot bigger, and your anger would ensure you became curvier too."

The heavy punch on Fishlegs arm, stopped his musing immediately.

"Just saying.." his tone took on a slightly whiny edge.

"I mean... I would too..." his voice trailed off, when he saw the scowl settling more firmly on Snotlout's face. His words weren't providing him with any comfort.

"Probably doesn't matter, the way it's made. Well... Once this batch is gone, I doubt anyone will be making any more." Fishlegs' gaze shifted, to Meatlug. He was always cautious when discussing things that happened in the past between vikings and dragons.

1112311.content:
Snotlout was scuffing his way along the beach on the far side of the island. It wasn't much of a beach, made more of pebbles, than sand. But at least he could kick the stones as he muttered to himself about life's unfairness.

He cursed Hiccup for being born the chief's son. He cursed Astrid for being Hiccup's favourite. He cursed Fishlegs for his love of books, and the details of dragon behaviour. He cursed the twins and their damned pranks and jokes. He cursed them all for failing to appreciate him, and Hookfang.

Putting a tremendous effort in he sent one stone flying, off his toe and out to sea. He hopped furiously, as the pain hit his brain. A scream aimed at the sky was only heard by the distant Hookfang, sunbathing on a nearby rock. He went to kick another pebble in his fury. Barely restraining himself in time.

After a moment, his eye was drawn to the odd stone. It was unlike the dark sharp edged pebbles, or the rounder, redder ones. It looked really out of place. Not just on the beach, but unlike any stone he'd seen anywhere. It glowed with an inner light, that danced as he looked at it.

Even picking it up, and examining it more closely. The light was more lively than even quartz or any other precious stone, the few he'd seen Trader Johann display. There wasn't much use for precious stones.

Though he hated the idea, Fishlegs sprang to mind. He hated asking him questions, having to listen to the answers. But with him riding that dull Gronkle, he had to admit Fishlegs knew his rocks. Obviously, he wouldn't trust him with a valuation. But he could trust him with a name or a type of rock.
1112311.title: Snotlout's mystery find...

choices: Landing back at the village, he heads off to find the Wise woman.
As if sensing the danger, Hookfang unseats Snotlout (at a safe height)...
On landing, an unbidden wail, rips from Snotlout's throat, his fear taking him.
11123111.content:
Snotlout kept a loose grip on the stone in his pocket as he walked. It seemed to radiate heat. Realising it's usefulness, he started scanning the beach as he walked back and forth. At times he'd kick at the stones, in frustration. Night flying and flying higher above the clouds was a chilly business, and any relief would be happily welcomed.

Despite his natural superiority, he knew he wasn't regarded as the smartest of the Dragon riders. With Fishlegs' seemingly endless knowledge more annoying than he wanted to admit. He also distrusted Hiccup's intuition with dragons. After half an hour, on the beach, it was getting darker, and he hadn't found any more of those glowing stones.

Pulling it from his pocket he wondered if it could work as a torch at night. Doubting that thought even as it occurred, the stony beach wasn't exactly speckled with glowing stones.

But as he withdrew it, he spotted something worrying. The damned thing had grown, and was encasing his hand in it's stony embrace. He tried shattering it against boulders on the beach. But beyond sending off a few sparks didn't do any damage. Worryingly, also the stone's glow pulsed in time with Snotlout's heart.

Trying nightmare gel, he tried to burn it away. But it burned brightly without weakening it at all. Before Snotlout's eyes, it's flame flickered and failed much quicker than it should have. The reason became clear with the next few beats of Snotlout's heart, as the stone surged up his arm, binding and locking his wrist in one position. Four heartbeats later, it had locked his elbow into a bent pose.

Afraid of being glued to Hookfang, or stuck to his saddle, it took him a moment to realise his danger. As his pulse raced, it seemed like the rush of speed was spent, as the stone advance slowed as it approached his shoulder.

He confronted his isolation, and called Hookfang over. It looked like the glow was distorting his arm, making it look different. An internal yellow glow, a hint of sparkles seemed to light his flesh from within. Mounting Hookfang from the other side, he was soon airborne and headed back to the village.

No longer the hero, with the magic warming stones. Now, he was the invalid, the fool. He cursed himself for her impetuous bravery in risking this unknown danger.

1112311311.content:
"Hey, Snotlout."

Snotlout scowled as he heard his cousin's voice, and grumbled, "What do you want?"

Hiccup got off of Toothless, who immediately started trailing after Hiccup, and asked, "Well, you normally don't come to this area unless you need something."

Snotlout felt his fists tighten, one of which still had the strange stone he found earlier, and snarled, "Gods, do you even hear yourself?"

Hiccup raised an eyebrow in confusion, "Huh?"

Snotlout continued, "You're acting like such a woman!"

Hiccup blinked, "I really don't think I'm acting anything like Astrid or Ruffnut. Are you all r-"

Snotlout snarled, "Not like them, you idiot! You're acting like a traditional viking woman!"

Hiccup opened his mouth to defend womankind, but Snotlout's glare stopped him before he could start.

Pausing to glare at Toothless, he continued, "And that thing acts more like a real viking than you! You are so annoying! I wish you could see what I mean!"

Hiccup's hands rose to his head, and for a moment his hands spasmed in pain. Until a moment later, as she dropped her hands Snotlout could see that Hiccup was truly the woman he'd named her.

https://s-media-cache-ak0.pinimg.com/564...

As Snotlout stood there, still struck by the change, he felt the rock digging into his hands, it's sharp corners drawing blood, as he clenched it in his pocket. Needing to see the damage, he pulled his hand out. The rock pulsed with an internal glow, brighter than before.
