1112311311213.content:
Even as Snotlout watched, Hiccup continued to change. Her figure became more womanly, her hair grew and was braided as a Berk maiden. Her leather riding outfit became a top and skirt, showing a great figure and luscious legs. The was beautiful! She had also shrunk, being even shorter than Astrid. She looked up at Snotlout, "What have you done to me!"

At first, he was too stunned to answer. Then he grinned as an idea crossed his mind. If the Chief's only child was a daughter, she could not become the next chief! Instead, the next chief would be... he grinned and mumbled a wish that Hiccup could not hear.

She started to demand an answer, "Snotlout! Tell me what is going on!"

He grinned, "Woman. Tell me your name and what sex you were born."

Somehow, Hiccup felt intimidated by this huge male. She calmed down and said, "You know that Snotlout. My name is Ingrid and I was born female. Wait! I mean I am the Chief's daughter, Ingrid! I mean, I am a girl.... no!" She could not say anything different! "I don't know what you did Snotlout, but I will find a way to undo it!"

That did worry Snotlout. Hiccup always seemed to find a way out of things. But he could fix that too, "Hmmm... then I wish that this change can never be reversed. Not by me or anybody else. Ever!"

Hiccup/Ingrid gasped, "I don't know what you did, but my father will make you fix this!" And with that, she jumped onto Toothless and flew off. Snotlout grinned and followed her.

A few minutes later, Hiccup/Ingrid spotted her father from the air and headed down.She saw Snotlout following her, but her made no attempt to stop her. As soon as Toothless landed, she ran to her father, and when she got to him, she didn't even realize when she gave him the equivalent of a Viking female's curtsy in show of respect as from female to a male. "Father! I need..."

But Stoick held up his hand to silence her. He looked down at her and said...
1112311311213.title: Tightening his fist, Snotlout mutters his next ...

choices: Astrid's betrothal to Tufffnut is announced
11123113112132.content:
Stoick held up his hand to silence her. He looked down at her a moment then pulled her close, hugging her, then he said, "Perfect timing daughter! We can now announce your betrothal." Just then, Snotlout landed next to Toothless and dismounted. Stoick grinned, "I am glad the two of you were flying together! It is good to have the future groom present too!"

Hiccup/Ingrid stumbled along as Stoick half dragged her toward Snotlout, "Wait dad! You don't understand! I can't marry Snotlout!"

Stoick paused, and kneeling down, held his daughter's shoulders firmly, "Sweetheart. You know I would not do this if it weren't necessary. If only you had been born a boy, perhaps you could have married a girl like Astrid. But you were born a girl. We will need a chief. Since it can't be you, that will be the man you marry. And only your marriage to Snotlout will prevent a civil war here on Berk. I am sorry daughter, but that is the way of women. For the sake of peace on Berk, don't fight me in this."

Hiccup/Ingrid found herself being pulled along again, but now stunned and limp. Now she was confused. Her father and everyone else seemed to thing that she had been born as Ingrid. And there had always been tension between Snotlout's family and her's. Astrid was from that Snotlout's clan, and Hiccup's marriage to her would have united the island. However, now, she was the female, and somehow, because of whatever it was that Snotlout had been holding, she was being forced to marry for the sake of peace. At least for Astrid, they were in love with each other. Unity or not, they would have married. Now Hiccup had to marry a man she not only didn't love, but could barely stand!

The whole time these thoughts were running through Hiccup's head, her father was proclaiming her betrothal to Snotlout. She was in stunned shock as Snotlout took her in his arms and kissed her forcefully and deeply to the cheers of the village. She was numb as she stood there, held close to Snotlout by one of his strong arms, and was congratulated by people of the village.

Stoick announced, "The wedding will be in two weeks, at the start of the Spring festival!"

When the crowds had faded, they walked back toward the dragons together. Snotlout looked down at her, "An early wedding present for you Ingrid. I made sure that Toothless is female too, so that you don't have to worry about riding a boy dragon." He leered at Hiccup/Ingrid, "And just think. At the same time that the farmers will be putting their seed in the ground, I will be planting my seed in you. I think you will look cute with a baby bump."

The thought of what Snotlout was saying almost made Hiccup/Ingrid throw up! What was worse yet, unless she could undo this, there was no way to prevent it. Once the official announcement had been made, it could not be undone. She was powerless to stop the wedding now. And after the wedding.... well, she did not have the strength to resist Snotlout, and there was no such thing in the laws of Berk as a husband raping his wife. Once married, she was almost the same as Snotlout's property in Berk society. All this made her shudder! It was bad enough to be trapped in the body of a female, but to be forced to bear Snotlout's children was unthinkable! She shouted, "I will still find a way to undo this before our wedding day!"

Snotlout grinned, "Not this time Ingrid." And with that, he grabbed her, and lifting her with one arm he forced a long, sloppy kiss on her as the other hand harshly groped her rear.

When he finally released her, she gasped out, "You are a bastard Snotlout! And if you go through with this, I will never love you!"

He laughed, "Who needs love? But I will love having you over and over."

In total disgust, Hiccup/Ingrid ran off. She ran to her favorite hideout in the woods, not stopping until she got there! Once there, all the emotions, which seemed even stronger in this female form, overwhelmed her, and she bawled like a baby. Even when she calmed a little, she could not stop crying. How was this possible? How? She was a man! Or should be at least! How did Snotlout do this to her? How did he not only turn her into this female, but make it that nobody but the two of them remembered her ever being Hiccup? She knew that it had something to do with whatever he held in his hand earlier, but what was it? She had to find out! Then she had to get it from him! Maybe she could undo this! But how?

It was only then that Hiccup/Ingrid realized that there was a gentle arm around her shoulder, and someone was stroking her hair. She looked up to see Astrid and wondered how long she had been there? Astrid spoke softly, "Don't stop crying on my account. We have always been like sisters. I know what you are feeling and I don't blame you." She growled, "It is not fair we women have no choice in who we marry! Not that there are a lot of choices for us on Berk anyhow, but Snotlout!?! You poor thing! I would be crying too!"

Hiccup/Ingrid sniffed the tears away, "Please Astrid! You need to help me! I can't marry him!"

Astrid hugged her, "You know I would do anything for you Ingrid, but what is there to do? It is announced. You are already his." She paused, "You could fly off of course. Go to another island maybe. But that would only guarantee war on Berk."

Hiccup/Ingrid looked up at Astrid with red eyes, "There is a way." She told Astrid of the strange stone that Snotlout had come in possession of. She could not tell about having been male and turned into a female, but she could tell most of the rest."

Astrid shook her head, "Ingrid. My dearest friend. I know how much you loathe Snotlout. But I think your imagination is running away with you. We had this talk before. Your dad has been talking to Spitelout for weeks. Snotlout wouldn't need to wish to marry you. It was almost guaranteed since the day you were born." She sighed, "And it is not going to go well for me either."

Hiccup/Ingrid looked at her concerned, "Why?"

Astrid sighed again, "I heard my parents talking. We both know that the only other males our age on Berk are Fishlegs and Tuffnut. If I had a choice, it would be Fishlegs. At least he would be a caring husband. But my parents were talking to Tuffnut's dad. He pointed out that, if I married Fishlegs, there would be nobody left for Ruffnut to marry. He offered them three sheep and two goats if my father would select Tuffnut as my husband."

Hiccup/Ingrid gasped, "Wow!" Then she realized, "Your parents couldn't possibly turn down an offer like that." There was sympathy in her voice.

Astrid nodded, "Yes. And Fishleg's family could never afford to beat that offer." She shuddered, "And if any man on this island is more perverted than Snotlout, it is Tuffnut. I don't even want to think of what it will be like to be his wife!"

Male or female, Hiccup/Ingrid was always more motivated by the needs of others. She grabbed Astrid's hand, "List to me Astrid! I swear to you that Snotlout has that object! And if we can get it, I know that it is possible for both of us to choose who we will marry and still have peace on Berk! Just trust me! Will you help me...," she hesitated to use the word, but she needed Astrid's help, "... will you help me sister?"

Astrid laughed, "We are like sisters, but you only call me one when you are trying to get me to do something stupid! But why not? It is better to try and fail! I am with you sister!"

Hiccup/Ingrid flinched at being called a sister by Astrid, but it fit, and they hugged each other briefly, then sat down to make plans.
11123113112132.title: Perfect timing daughter! We can now announce yo...

choices: Snotlout threatens to use the stone on Toothless
Snotlout uses the stone on Astrid and Ingrid
111231131121322.content:
It was almost noon by the time they finished talking. It amazed Hiccup/Ingrid at how close of a bond she felt with Astrid now. Without the sexual tension that had always been present between then when she was a male, they truly were like sisters. Very close sisters. She was seeing Astrid through a whole new light now, and her admiration for her had grown immensely. Sure, as Hiccup she had understood that it was harder for Astrid, striving to be regarded equally with the male warriors, but now as Ingrid, she understood how little regard their society had for females in general, and how quickly all that she had worked so hard for could be taken from her. In fact, as Ingrid, if she didn't find a way back to being Hiccup, her freedom would be gone in two weeks. Just being betrothed meant it was greatly diminished.

Astrid said, "I hope we can find this thing. Snotlout is not the brightest torch on the island, but he will know that he needs to protect something like this."

Hiccup/Ingrid agreed, "Yes. But his biggest weakness has always been his over confidence. Let's hope that works in our favor."

As they walked back toward the village, Astrid paused and walked off of the trail a bit to a small but shielded clearing, "You will come to my house for lunch, but let's stop her to pee. I really gotta go!" Without hesitation, Hiccup/Ingrid saw something she never witnessed before. Astrid just squatted down and began to pee! (Underwear hasn't been invented yet.) She saw the puddle of urine forming beneath Astrid's skirt. It was embarrassing! Not so embarrassing that she could overcome the sudden urge to pee herself. This was the first time that she was forced to acknowledge the depth of the change that had been forced onto her. Standing to pee would never be an option as long as she had this female body. Not seeing any other choice, she followed Astrid's example and squatted down. It felt odd as the urine shot out of a hidden place between her legs, but the overall feeling of relief was the same. When she was done, Hiccup/Ingrid followed Astrid's example of tearing off a large leaf and wiping between her legs.

They got to Astrid's place, and Hiccup/Ingrid knew that, as a female, she would be expected to help with the preparation of the meal. Somehow, things like that came to her as if she really had grown up as a female, even though she could not remember learning them. The same went to cleaning up afterward. However, as soon as they were done, Astrid's dad stood up and grabbed Astrid's hand. She seemed to understand what was going on, and immediately tried to resist, but her father was far too strong and easily took her outside.

Once out, he called out with a lout voice, "Everybody! May I have everyone's attention! I have an announcement to make!"

Astrid screamed, "No! I...", but a swift slap shut her up.

He continued, "I am announcing the betrothal of my daughter Astrid to Tuffnut. If the Chief consents, they will marry on the same day as his daughter, at the Spring festival."

All of the fight went out of Astrid. There was no longer any point. She was now officially betrothed to Tuffnut, and nothing she could do would change that now. However, as soon as her father released her, she ran inside. It was the first time Hiccup/Ingrid had seen her cry like that. Now it was her turn to comfort Astrid. She was sorry that they now had this in common. She whispered, "We will find that stone and fix this too! I promise!"
111231131121322.title: Astrid's betrothal to Tufffnut is announced

choices: Convnvince him that he is the one who should become Chief someday
Convince him that Snotlout will betray him too
1112311311213221.content:
Astrid and Hiccup/Ingrid spent the next couple of days flying to remote locations and trying to figure out where Snotlout might be hiding his secret. During that time, Hiccup felt even closer to Astrid than ever. She wished that she was able to tell her the truth about having been male, but all attempts failed. It would have been nice to at least talk to her about having to get used to the constant bounce and motion of her breasts, and the fear of what sex would mean to her now, but all of that was impossible too. Well, they did cry together over the thought of being forced to bear children for Snotlout and Tuffnut, which made both of them sick, but Hiccup could not relate her terror of sex as a female.

Things took a turn for the worse on the third morning when they tried to take off, but both Toothless and Stormfly seemed too weak to fly. As they tried to figure out what was wrong with their dragons, Snotlout showed up, "Are you girls having trouble?"

Immediately Hiccup/Ingrid knew this was Snotlout's doing, "What did you do to our dragons!?!?"

He smiled, "Come over here with me Ingrid, and I will tell you." She reluctantly walked up to him, and he put his arm around her and led her farther from Astrid. Then he stood her in front of him, "For now, I just made them too weak to fly. I am sick of how you have been avoiding me, and how you refuse to act like a proper betrothed when we are around each other. That is going to change! If you don't start behaving like you are happy to be with me and marry me, not only will Toothless never fly again, I will make sure that she is deformed in a very painful way."

She gasped, "You wouldn't! Not even you could be that cruel!" But his silent stare made it clear that he would. She had no choice or Toothless would suffer. She sighed, "Okay Snotlout, you win. I will do as you ask."

He grinned, "Good. Prove it right now. Kiss me. And not a little peck. I want you to kiss me the way you used to kiss Astrid, with passion. And if I don't think it is good enough, the next sound you hear from Toothless will be agony."

Hiccup was totally defeated. She knew that he would do it. She had to brace herself, then she reached up, wrapped her arms around Snotlout, and kissed him with as much passion as she could muster. She tried to pretend that she was still male, and kissing Astrid, but Snotlout's whiskers ruined that. All she could be was the weak female submitting to her lover. Snotlout held her head in place as their tongues intertwined while his other hand groped her rear. When he released her, she was gasping for air!

Snotlout smiled, "That is more like it. Only in the future, you will do that in the market, or the Hall, or wherever, regardless of who is around. And you will not go flying, or anywhere, unless it is with me. Understood girl?" She nodded, "Oh, and no more of your flying gear. From now on, you will wear your betrothal dress. You are a female now, so start acting like one!" As he said this, he unbuttoned her top and kissed her there. She did not try to stop him. When he was done, he groped her rear again and pushed her back toward Astrid and the dragons, then he added, "Oh, and tell Astrid that the same goes for her. Either she submits to Tuffnut, or Stormfly will suffer for her disobedience. Tuffnut and I will expect our women to be with us before the sun is higher than the Hall roof."

Hiccup/Ingrid was crying as she walked back up to Astrid and the dragons. Astrid looked at her, "Ingrid! What did he do to you!" Hiccup/Ingrid composed herself and gave Astrid the bad news. She turned pale, "You don't think he would do it! Do you?" But they both knew that he would.

In silence they went back to their homes and put on the flimsy, feminine dresses. They walked in shame to the market and paused a moment when they saw Snotlout and Tuffnut together there. Of course, they would choose the most public place! Then they each went to their betrothed and kissed them a long time. The whole time, they were pawed and groped from top to bottom.

Snotlout broke the kiss, then loudly said, "Tell me you want to have my babies Ingrid."

"What ya' up to?" Astrid asked. Her tone suspicious, just because it was Snotlout, and his shifty behaviour was clear to her alert senses.

"Nuthin'..." he answered reflexively. Surprising himself that it was true.

His fist played in his pocket as his fingers felt their way over the newly discovered stone.

111231131214.content:
"I wish Astrid didn't feel so intimidated by me." Snotlout muttered as he fingered the stone in his pocket. In that moment, Snotlout's world changed. As if a blinding light went off behind his eyes, Snotlout blinked away the speckles that shot across his vision.

Astrid still looked down at him from atop Stormfly, but the look of suspicion and contempt was gone from her face.

"Snotlout... are you OK?" There was real concern in her voice.

"Of course... why shouldn't I be?" he answered. His voice was a little high and piping, as he spoke. Trying to clear his throat, and whatever was causing that girly tone, Snotlout tried again.

"I'm fine..." But it emerged in the same feminine tone.

It was then that Snotlout realised he was no longer a he. Looking down, his entire outfit had changed. Instead of his coarse jerkin, he got a view of a deep cleavage... His own... Her own cleavage!?

https://66.media.tumblr.com/540139af4a2d...

It was at least protected by armour, but she murmured in disbelief. "I'm a woman..."

Astrid responded, "Of course you're a woman and almost as good a flyer as I am..."

Snotlout wanted to object further. But Astrid seemed to remember her as a woman, and with everything else she didn't want to appear insane.

But, Snotlout's world was rocked. She knew everyone and everything's place in the world, and women didn't belong on dragons. They were for home-making and baby raising. Only the insane like Ruffnut didn't count, and nobody was going to tell Astrid she was a viking woman.

Snotlout shuddered as she looked around. Desperate for someone who didn't see her as a woman... born and bred.

111232.content:
Bored and looking for something else to do, anything else to do. Snotlout left Fishlegs alone, and went off to explore the beach. There wasn't a ship on the horizon, and he found a few nadder spines embedded in a dead tree trunk.

With their precision, he knew they were Astrid's from target practice. He'd heard that nadder spines kept their edge well, and with a simple leather binding, could be fashioned into a simple dagger.

http://vignette4.wikia.nocookie.net/drea...

Unwrapping his wrist guards, he cut them into a more manageable length with the embedded spine. With it still stuck in the wood, it was easy to criss-cross them into handle. It worked well enough for him to make four workable daggers. Two were blue spines, one was a dull golden yellow. The last was scarlet, and didn't look like it came from Stormfly. Although given where it was stuck, and the surrounding spines it had to.

Pulling the red dagger from it's place, he jammed it into his belt beside the other three. A hiss of pain escaped his lips, as he cut the palm of his hand with the last one. It wasn't a deep cut, and the hiss was more in surprise than pain. There was only a trickle of blood, and as he clenched his fist, that helped him focus on the pain.

With his new selection of weapons, he felt he'd achieved something, and headed back to rejoin Fishlegs.

His palm stung, and he worried vaguely about infection, or some nadder defensive toxins. A few questions for Fishlegs, and he'd have any concerns settled. With a snort he recalled how many potions and poultices Gothi kept in her tiny home.

He gave Fishlegs a friendly wave as he climbed the last sand dune, and the viking looked up from his book.

11131.content:
Hiccup was walking the beach, taking some alone time. The bewilderbeast had just been vanquished and everyone in Berk was bending over backwards to do him favours.

Now that's not a bad thing, Hiccup thought to himself. But he did miss the days when he was younger and the villagers would ignore or avoid him. As the chief's scrawny son, he couldn't reassure the villagers that Stoick would hand on power to ... to him.

He didn't want to be weak and ignored, but at the same time he wasn't entirely comfortable with all the attention either. Hell, Astrid knew he liked his personal space, and he knew she kept an eye out for him.

Toothless was always a good companion, never interrupting and suggesting things.

He kicked a stone, it went skidding through the sand, as he walked onwards. The beach was surrounded by cliffs and only accessible by dragon so he knew he'd be safe. At least until Astrid decided he was being self indulgent.

Oddly he felt a bond with Astrid even before they were friends.

It was hard to define.... It wasn't a friendship.... it was just the sense of not fitting in.

He was the chief's son, and also the least viking-like viking in the whole village. He thought back to Stoick's disappointment in his son. Stoick was a good dad, Hiccup knew this deep in his bones, but he felt the pain of his dad's disappointment deeply, as he thought it was true himself.

Now, Astrid.... Well, Astrid was different from him. A smile crossed his face with just the thought of her.

Her family was not powerful or well connected, it didn't hold sway in the village. But Astrid didn't fit in either. As a young viking, she was expected to learn how to keep a house... and a husband...

Viking life was rather more limited for the women of the village. Astrid fought against the pressures of people's expectations, and took to carrying her axe. She practiced with it endlessly, and built her strength, and ability.

She was always going to make a fierce viking warrior.

Hiccup reached the stone, and gave it another kick, shooting it off down the beach another hundred yards. This time though he noticed it give off a pale blue glow.

He chased after it, hoping it wasn't a dragon egg. Although dragon eggs are sturdy, their mother's wouldn't like to see it being kicked.

He reached it, and dropped to his knees. As he picked it up, the glow brightened and strengthened.

Peering deep inside, there were dancing lights, but no sign of a baby dragon. Hiccup's thoughts went back on track.

Idly he wondered if Astrid would have made a better son or he'd have been a better daughter. A random thought just to be dismissed.

The light flared, and painfully too late, Hiccup dropped the stone.

As Hiccup blinked his eyes clear, he looked around his feet, everything was still blurry and imprecise, but there wasn't anything 'glowy' near his feet. But something was different. Hiccup knew it in his bones. He settled back onto his rump, and waited until he could see properly.

http://fc05.deviantart.net/fs71/f/2014/0...

Hiccup swept his errant hair back over his shoulder. Pausing he wondered when it became that long, it was always untidy, but never this long. He couldn't carry off long hair, it just made him look ... well ... girly.

That was the thought, and everything fell into place. Hiccup's hands raced over his body exploring it....

She swallowed nervously, it was undeniable.

Stoick had a daughter.

111313.content:
Hicca races towards Toothless, and leaps onto his back.

"C'mon Buddy." she strokes his neck, and holds on as Toothless kicks off and with a single bound is in the air.

"Back to the village." her voice is softer, and as she rides it hits her.

Toothless hasn't reacted at all to this change. Not even a curious or cautionary sniff.

Toothless flew low and fast weaving between the trees as he went. It was the type of flying that Hiccup enjoyed, but at the moment, she had other things on her mind.

She kept her eyes on the horizon, allowing Toothless to choose their route.

It was only when a voice called out that she looked up.

She had to crane her neck, as she saw the familiar large head of a nadder. It was Stormfly and Astrid.

"Hiccup." the voice repeated.

It wasn't Astrid, that was a deeper voice... a man's voice.

She slowed Toothless, and watched as the rider swooped in closer. The flying was incredible, Hiccup had to admire it.

The wings hid who the rider was, "Astrid..." she asked uncertainly.

Soon the two dragons were flying side by side. He didn't recognise the rider, well that's not quite true. Who it was, was quite clear. There were little features and details to his face that told Hiccup this was Astrid. Not the least of these was the rather annoyed look on his face.

"This is your fault, Hiccup." he snarled. "Or should I say Hicca."

Hiccup just pointed to the ground for them to land.

As they both dismounted, it was clear that Astrid was male, and about a foot taller than Hiccup.

"Hi. I'm Astor. Or at least to everyone else, that's who I've always been." He pushed Hiccup forcefully on the chest, so she fell backwards over a rock.

He stood over the splayed out form of the chief's daughter.

"Everyone thinks this is the way things are. Ruffnut just tried to hit on me." he squirmed and made an disgusted face.

"Sorry, I don't know my own strength." he offered a hand to her.

"As soon as I realised that I was the only one who knew your name, Hicca. Then I knew who had to be behind it. So I came here straight away."

"Huh... What .... What are you talking about?"

Astor smirks, "Don't tell me you've fallen out of love with me."

"What... Wait... you love me..."

"Oh, alright. I can't stand in the way of true love, I'll be happy when you marry Snotlout." Astor's smile beamed wider than ever as he saw the confusion and fear on Hicca's face.

"Wait.... What... Snotlout?... Marry! Snotlout!!..." Hicca squeaked. She started to wobble unsteadily on her feet, Astor put out a hand to keep her on her feet.

Astor pulled her in and calmed her with a kiss. Hicca felt herself melt into the strength of her friend, a friend who meant so much more.

"Better now." he said as he broke the kiss.

Even his deep gruff voice did things to her, that she didn't want to think about. It looked like Astor played as dirtily as Astrid. Hicca needed to regain control.

She gave a polite cough, and pointed her eyes downwards. Astor's gaze followed down to where his hand was wrapped around her clothes. His fist settled nicely between her breasts.

Blushing he let her go, his gaze finding the pebbles very interesting all of a sudden. He punched her on the shoulder.

"You'll have to adjust too." Hicca informed him.

"You can't just go hitting girls.", it was her time to smirk. "Although, I think that Ruff might like that kind of attention."

Hicca fell silent, as for the first time, she genuinely considered Ruffnut as a potential rival. It was all happening so quickly, her joke also brought home the fact that Tuffnut might be interested in her too.

"Yeah... Let's just be a couple." They both said simultaneously. Their thoughts obviously following the same path.

"Just pretend... and just for the moment..."

"Ahem... anyway... Hiccup, what did you do?" Astor finally asked.

She explained that she'd gone for some privacy, and just to get used to the idea of the loss of his father and the return of his mother. She shook her head in disbelief. So much had changed. Blushing she brought the stone up, and her voice fell to a whisper as she mentioned what she'd been thinking when it gave off the flash of light.

Valka gave him a head rub, and jumped off.

She crushed Hicca to her bosom.

Her face was more mixed, as thoughts of Stoick came to mind.

"Your father would have been so proud to give you away."

She takes a sobbing gasp.

"I'm sorry to rush you into this, but the village needs a chief, and this will secure your name and fame. Besides, you've got a good man by your side to act as counsel."

She gave Astor a punch on the arm, the sight of him flinching brought a smile to Hicca's face. The boot was truly on the other foot.

When Valka turned back to Hicca, Astor's face was confused as he silently mouthed questions to his bride-to-be.

"You have Gobber and Myself to help you with the different tasks, Gobber was Stoick's right hand man after all."

Hicca had to nod her agreement.

She straightened her shoulders, "This is your duty as chief. The village needs to heal after Drago's attack, and celebrate your victory."

Her smile returned. "A week of celebrations ending with a wedding!"

The pride was clear in her voice.

Hiccup felt an absence when he woke. His bedroom wasn't just as warm as usual, and he glanced across to confirm. The slab where Toothless usually slept was empty. Just as he was thinking about looking for him, he heard the familiar scrabble of claws and Toothless snuck into the room. As quietly as a large dragon with sharp claws can on a stone floor.

There was a slightly ashamed look on his face, He licked some blood from his scaly lips and settled for the night. Hiccup hoped that he hadn't been eating something he shouldn't. But there weren't many things that a dragon couldn't eat if it wanted to give it a try.

He warmed his stone bed with a steady purple-white flame, before settling down like a cat. With his tail curled around his paws, his snout poked out.

Sometimes Toothless snored, not loudly, when Hiccup discovered this he watched fascinated as little brilliant white flames flickered at his nostrils, like a pilot light.

With his friend back in the room, he quickly fell back to sleep, a deeper sleep.

As they both slept neither was aware of the unfamiliar green flame, that started to flicker around his teeth. Even less were they aware of the odd smoke that was filling the room. It rose up to the ceiling, but soon as the room filled it descended.

Hiccup coughed softly when he first breathed in the smoke, but soon he had a steady rhythm, slow and deep.

1113212112.content:
As the pain intensified, Hiccup fought to loosen the straps on his false leg. It felt counter-intuitive, especially in flight to kick it off. But the relief was instantaneous and incredible. It felt like a phantom limb was now freed, imagining he could feel the breeze racing through his toes.

Knowing that Toothless would be making a harder landing, without his additional help with Toothless' tail. Patting the muscular wing joint, he caught the dragons' attention. Pointing to a clearing, he indicated it as a place to land.

Naturally, Toothless felt the unresponsive tail, and with a quick glance to Hiccup confirmed they'd be landing... as is.

Hiccup didn't want to risk triggering more of that intense pain, so kept his imaginary foot clear of any stirrups, or leather. Half giddy at that idea, keeping a phantom limb from getting tangled up. It took his entire concentration to guide them both down.

Astrid and Stormfly held back, giving them both the space to land.

- - - - -

The landing was too fast, and too steep. Resulting in both dragon and rider, having to tuck into balls, and roll off their excess speed. Hiccup clenched his knees to his chest, as he raced through heather and bracken. He came to a stop and toppled over. Head still spinning, it was hard to tell where Toothless had come down. Though the crashing, and snapping of branches was a good guide.

Struggling upright, Hiccup swayed back and forth, his vision still trying to spin. While the world remained like a tempestuous sea.

She was still fussing with her wings, as she folded them back to her flank, as Astrid skidded from her back. She raced towards Hiccup, as behind her Stormfly took long, loping strides towards, he assumed Toothless.

It was then that he noticed something was wrong. His hair was much longer than last night. His long brown hair was now as long as Ruff or Tuff.

He'd never gone full-viking, as he considered it. The wild unkempt hair was fine for a burly man with full beard, but with his build he decided not to risk looking too .... too well ... feminine.

Toothless joined him at the window his large draconian head pushing him effortlessly to the side. However Toothless still seemed a little off, he even gave Hiccup an unsure sniff, as if uncertain his friend was the same.

The fresh air soon revived the friends.

"Shall we circle the island?" he asked Toothless eager to be away. He bounded around the room in anticipation causing devastation in his wake. But thankfully most of the things were toppled rather than smashed.

A flare of pain shot down Astrid's spine, causing her to double over. Clutching her stomach, she fell to her knees. Those scaly knees raising dust as they hit. Swallowing the cry of pain, she got back to her feet. But, she was bent over. Like an old woman, she couldn't stand up straight. She reached for the support of her bed to help keep her standing.

It was then she felt the weirdest sensation. As her leather kilt started to flat. It felt like her spine was lengthening.... But she wasn't any taller.

Between the growth surges, she reached a tentative hand backwards, to discover that she now possessed a spiny tail. A double-checking glance confirmed it matched her scaly legs and arms for the same matching shades and patterns. It should've reached the floor, it was more than long enough. But it was raised to avoid brushing on the floor.

An errant thought, as she watch. Astrid found it responded to her thoughts, swishing and swaying from side to side. Extending her arms, she found they were now wings. All traces of their humanity vanished. Giving them an experimental flap, she felt herself become lighter. Felt a little lift from them.

Looking down her body, she saw that it had changed without her being aware of the changes. Her underbelly a lighter shade of scales than covered her back and legs.

Another realisation crashed over her as she took a deep breath, a vain attempt to calm her rising fear. She felt her nostrils flare, and they felt far larger than they should. Her hair felt stiffer, and oddly... primed.

Turning her head this way and that, she worked out that her vision was different. Her field of view wider. Then she caught her reflection in the window. She was a beautiful amber coloured nadder. From snout to tail, she was a dragon. No trace of Astrid was left.

1113322323311.content:
Gothi sat in a trance in her hut. Sitting cross-legged in front of the chief, she looked like she could be accidentally squashed if Stoik stretched or rose suddenly. She was less than a quarter his size, and looked fragile as a dry twig.

Stoik knew she still had a resilience in her. As a hooligan, she was powerful, her strengths lying in the arcane arts rather than on the battlefield. Her herb garden had fought off the worst excesses of infection from dragon bite, or infection keeping the tribe whole in the face of a dangerous world.

She was nodding, softly, rhythmically. Either agreeing with Stoik's doubts about Hiccup's credibility as a leader, or just at risk of nodding off in the stifling hut. The air was thick with pungent fumes. Stoik didn't want his son's rule to be brief, and terminal. His naming had been a mercy, almost a prayer for an early death for such a fragile child. But, Hiccup had survived childhood, even if he lacked the size and strength he'd wanted in a son.

- - - - -

Coming to Gothi had been a last resort. He didn't want the tribe led by Spitelout, or his idiot son Snotlout. He huffed angrily imagining how brief that would last as a family. His son had always valued his tinkering inventions, an aid to his lack of strength. His imagination was unviking-like. A problem should be handled head on... and head-butted until it surrendered to a viking's will.

His mind turned to Astrid, who embodied those values. Embracing change when it was needed but not seeking to overthrow the natural order... just .... just because. Stoik found himself fuming, silently wishing Astrid was his. She lived the viking lifestyle, fighting her entire life to be taken seriously. To not simply be married off to some lumbering hulk.

The thought blossomed in his head, since the pair were already such good friends, then perhaps he should do the chiefly thing. Have their bond recognised. If Astrid were swapped with Hiccup, she could have the gender to match her spirit, and Hiccup would be safe to tinker, to experiment and invent. A sly, sneaky womanly way of fighting behind the scenes.

As he rose out of his reverie, Stoik saw Gothi's eyes were open and looking directly into his. She seemed to read his expression, his guilt, his hope, his resolution.

"Are you settled on your course, Chief?" She asked with deliberateness. Her words thin and reedy, but still carrying great weight.

Stoik grunted, and gave a curt nod of his head. His beard rustled as his helmet scraped off the low roof of Gothi's hut. Looking for the damage his helmet had done, he was surprised to look down and see she'd pushed a pot into the centre, resting between them.

111332232331112.content:
Stoik prepared the feast, for the three of them. A whole roast pig was turning slowly over the fire. He probably have to eat most of that himself. It was a sacrifice he was willing to make as chief. Tonight he had to be the Chief, it was the village's future that was being decided.

The defences of Berk were more necessary than ever. With the reputation for dragons both benefitting their reputation, and drawing some unwanted attention too.

Naturally, Hiccup didn't have to travel far to reach the dinner table. Astrid turned up in her freshly polished armour. An awkward stance as he opened the door for her. She was well aware what was going to be discussed tonight. And it terrified her more than riding a dragon into battle.

As her head tilted sideway, Stoik realised she was looking past his bulk, for the reassuring face of his son. He could even tell when she spotted him, as her face lit up with joy.

- - - - -

The plates steamed, and were piled high with meat and potatoes. A simple meal, nourishing, and filling. As Stoik served up 'coffee' a luxury from Trader Johan, the smell was strong and bitter in his nostrils. Unsure how it would taste, but it couldn't be worse than the exchanged looks the two were sharing.

The conversation was short, blushing, and full of stilted pauses. It ended with Astrid explaining she did... She truly did love Hiccup, but that she wasn't ready for... for that... for kids, and all that! Hiccup's hand reached across the table to comfort her. Patting her extended hand, with his own.

Hiccup wasn't spared any blushes, as Stoik turned his attention to his son. Who looked ready to ride Toothless, and race the dawn away from this situation. He waved off the idea of becoming chief, assuring Stoik that he was still young and vibrant. That he wasn't considering taking over as chief any time soon.

Stoik caught the exchanged nod, short but shared. He knew they'd agreed their position before the meal. Hiding his proud smile in the dense beard, he knew exactly how a tight family faced challenges, and fought battles before they were engaged. This was a great sign for their future.

Patting them on the shoulders, he made great play of accepting their resolution. The beefy pats meant their drinks were spilled.

He refilled their mugs, with a little addition. Offering it back to the fiancés, with a word or two.

"Should things change, you could reassess the situation." he said. The meal was finished off with the coffee. Leaving everyone restless, and energised.

1113322323311121.content:
Neither of them stirred, as Stoik lifted them up, putting them both in Hiccup's bed. He left them both clothed and atop the sheets, a growling chuckle coming from him as he left the room. A last backwards glance, saw them cuddling together, and mumbling soft nothings to each other.

Returning to his own bed, he felt the energy of the coffee leave his system. Stoik slept and dreamed of his new son, and future chief, and the daughter he'd be welcoming into the family, and soon be the mother to his grand children.

- - - - -

He slept through the night, the Sun sneaking in Hiccup's bedroom window first, it fell into his eyes. Blinking against the intruder, and slapping an arm across his face, he tried to blot out the Sun. The murmurring moan, was oddly pitched. As Hiccup struggled to stay asleep a number of sensations demanded his attention.

The memories of the previous night demanded he answer the questions. Stretching, and yawning he tried to fall back asleep. One arm brushed against a body that wasn't his own.

- - - - -

Instantly, he was awake, and sat bolt upright. In confusion he looked down on his own body, a soft snore coming from him, as his scrawny chest rose and fell. It felt like a weird dream, but a touch too real. He knew he was in trouble once he realised he was in Astrid's body.

Her chest rose and fell, in quiet rapid panic. The leather skirt, and thick woolen leggings were absolutely hers too. Snatching at the hair batting against her face, Hiccup confirmed he...?... she was now blonde.

It wasn't the way he'd wanted to spend the night together. But Hiccup knew exactly who he needed to talk to... and she...? ... he was lightly snoring away in front of her.

Gently rocking his shoulder back and forth. A hand ready to stifle him in case he screamed. Hiccup woke Astrid.

His eyes opened blearily, staring in blank confusion into her own face, smiling uncertainly, and mutely begging for her silence.

His reaction was the same as her own. Astrid sat upright, eyes wide in horror, his mouth working silently, as questions bubbled to the surface, each replaced by the next before it could be spoken.

"I'm you..."

"yeah... and I'm you..."

The young couple spoke over each other. The sound of the rocks in an avalanche announced the presence of sleeping Stoik in the next room.

"What's.... What's going on?" Hiccup asked, in a voice that was feminine, and tending towards panic. Astrid patted her hand and muttered meaningless soothing noises, as if calming a nervous pet.

They snuck out of the house, Astrid leading the way, and pulling Hiccup along by the hand. At least that's how it would have seemed to an observer. Astrid froze as she hit the familiar floorboard, it's creaking moan of protest announcing them. Briefly, she froze, before continuing. Hiccup was slower, and struggling with his artificial leg. But they made it outside, away from the guilty bedroom they'd shared, and the nightmare that had swapped their bodies.

In the shadows between two houses, Astrid challenged Hiccup, pushing her with one hand awkwardly landing on her chest. Hiccup recoiled, more in shock than from the force. It was having a bust, and having it pushed that disturbed her more than the anger.

He felt the same distress.

"I didn't do this..." she hissed angrily. "And I'm guessing you didn't either..."

Crossing his arms, Astrid's expression was uncomfortable. The absence of her familiar bust, just meant the crossed arms felt different.

choices: With a yawn, Hiccup returned to bed.
Too excited to sleep, Hiccup had to take notes.
Not much later, there's a knocking at the door.
He glanced over jealously to the curled-up, and still sleeping Toothless.
11133223233133.content:
It was a dark, damp winter morning, and Hiccup was glad to be back from his night patrol. With Toothless beneath him to keep him warm, it was less of a burden. But he was still soaked from head to toe, and the chill of the wind was unforgiving. Happily he headed into his hut, stripped off his wet clothes, towelled himself dry, and curled up in bed beneath several layers of furs. He was snoring almost instantly.

- - - - -

It felt like he'd barely closed his eyes before the door crashed open, and an excited Fishlegs charged in. Unfortunately, the low dawn Sun entered behind him, and fell right into Hiccup's eyes. In reality, he'd slept for an hour or two, but he was still exhausted, and his muscles ached.

"Hiccup... Hiccup... Hiccup..." He bellowed excitedly, blowing away the last remnants of sleep, if not tiredness. He was holding a stone proudly in front of himself. An awkwardly shaped stone, and it took his dopey brain a few seconds to work out it wasn't a stone.

There were times, when Hiccup was glad he had his own hut now. After his adventures on the edge the return to Berk had felt a little stifling. Living under his father's roof, a little childish. But, right at this moment, he'd have given anything to have an enraged Stoik throw him bodily back out, and slam the door behind him.

"Just tell me, it's not from a Changewing, this time..." He mumbled the warning.

Getting up he dressed, and confirmed the egg was still warm.

"It was abandoned on the beach." Fishlegs explained patiently. "It would've died if I had come along."

The doubt was still written on Hiccup's face, "I watched for ages from the long grass, no dragon mother would leave her eggs for that long, or that vulnerable."

Hiccup wanted to agree, but couldn't bring himself to say that. The memories of the changewing assault still gave him nightmares, but his dragon lore was deep. Making a small hollow in the fire, he nestled the egg into the warm embers on the edge of the fire.

Double-checking the patterning, he made sure it wasn't a changewing egg himself. As he walked around the fire, he examined it from every side. It was an unknown dragon, and he could understand Fishlegs' excitement. Even if he hated the timing.

- - - - -

The penny was dropping about then for Fishlegs, as he noticed the pile of rumpled clothes, still steaming off the worst of the torrential rain. Hiccup saw the dawning in his eyes, as he recalled who'd been on night patrol.

"Sorry, sorry, sorry..." he was apologising with an uncomfortable half-bow. Hiccup hated this... When his friends treated him, like a chief in waiting.

"We'll leave it there, just now, and keep an eye on it's progress." Hiccup told him.

His curiosity was clear, his thirst for answers had him licking his lips with questions, with objections. If he left the egg here, it might hatch in Hiccup's company, reveal its' nature to him first, rather than himself. But he was still mortified by his entrance.

With another awkward bow, he backed out of the home, and closed the door behind himself.

111332232331331.content:
Curiosity battled exhaustion briefly, with exhaustion winning. Hiccup returned to his bed, keeping an eye on the egg, he knew it might hatch at any point... but that might mean hours from now, too.

The warmth swept him back to sleep, and soon he was snoring again. The rest of the tribe were wise enough to keep away from the hut of someone who'd just completed a night patrol. Besides, the weather worsened, as rain pelted down, and the wind whistled between the various homes of the village.

Only a few vikings were up and about. Fishlegs had retreated with Meatlug to the village forge. They worked their creating gronkle iron for Gobber to forge. The heat of the furnace, and the physical labour meant it was snug and warm. Even the breezes that entered the forge served to make it burn hotter.

- - - - -

Back across the village, Hiccup was fast asleep, when the top of the egg cracked. Instead of a dragon beak emerging, a sinuous spire of smoke emerged from it. Hiccup snuffled and snorted in protest, as the smoke reached his nostrils. The smell was quite strong, but his tiredness was deeper.

As the cloud thickened inside Hiccup's hut, he took it deeper into his lungs. Despite the smell, it worked as a soporific, keeping Hiccup asleep as it's true purpose was revealed.

As Hiccup slept on, oblivious to the changes to her body. Even her soft snoring sounded different. The door opened quickly letting a sliver of moonlight in, before closing just as rapidly behind the new arrival.

A careful sniff of the air, through her improvised mask satisfied her that it was free enough of the various drugs. Just to be cautious, she opened a couple of the small windows, allowing a draught to clear the last traces from the air.

The sleeper moaned softly in her sleep, but didn't.... couldn't rouse. It was an odd experience, as she peeled back the furs. The body was familiar, and looked out of place in those clothes.

Slipping down her mask, Heather looked down on her double. She whimpered as she lay there, vulnerable. Hiccup wasn't woken as Heather stripped off his familiar clothes. She did it with a degree of disappointment, finding Hiccup in his bed, was different in her head. But with a simple detached air, she stripped her naked.

It was just as odd to dress her in her old clothes. It did give her a new respect for herself, just seeing herself from the outside, rather than in a mirror gave her a confidence boost.

The first part of the plan was done.

The cave held a small padlocked trunk, deep inside.

"Sleep well?" A voice asked, startling Hiccup into whirling around.
11133223233211.content:
Hiccup was out storm riding. Many dragons preferred the calmer weather. Gronkles and other stone class dragons were too ungainly for drifting from one gust to the next. Other dragons were too fragile, the long wings and heavy bodies meaning they could be caught in baffling, battling gusts, ending up with torn tendons. But, Toothless had always looked out the window of the hut with a curious fascination whenever a storm hit Berk. It didn't matter if it were a gusty wintery, blizzard, or the worst torrential rain.

Snuggling up against the warmth of Toothless, he'd sit by the window and watch the clouds speed across the sky. Naturally, he was protective of his dragon, as Toothless was protective of his rider. Their trust and respect was mutual.

The wind gusted around Hiccup finding gaps in his rough viking clothes, and even the most intriguing clouds would still chill the blood quickly. Walking from the window he put the shutters across the window, and headed back to his bed.

- - - - -

Hiccup found him still there, when he woke, his broad muzzle working the latch up, so he could watch the wind and clouds. His nostrils flared and shrank as he drank in the freshest air. Realisation dawned on Hiccup, that Toothless was as fascinated by the storm as he was...

Scratching and petting his dragon, he coaxed out Toothless' desire to fly in this weather. It was wild and untamed, a true challenge to any dragon, or dragon and rider. Nodding to himself, resolved to take to the air. Hiccup dug out his waxed cloak, it stank of fish oil, but was the most waterproof item he had.

Toothless was nodding his heavy head enthusiastically as Hiccup readied himself for the worst weather yet.

"You're sure?" he asked, as he reached the door.

The reply needed no interpretation, as Toothless ducked his head, and used it to drive Hiccup from his home.

- - - - -

The wind almost took him from his foot, as he braced against the general direction of the wind. The gusts were the real danger. As Toothless emerged, his bulk blocked some of the wind, turning it into tumultous eddies. Hiccup closed the door behind him. The night fury's head was already low, inviting him to mount up.

The wisdom of this was insane. In fact, it was positively foolhardy. Hiccup mentally checked off the reasons for going alone. The twins didn't need much consideration, before they were dismissed. Fishlegs was too protective of Meatlug, and probably couldn't manage to take off. Snotlout wouldn't be the best company, and Hookfang's broad wings would mean he lacked the agility that'd be needed. His head bobbled back and forth as he considered taking Astrid along.

He wanted some company, and didn't want to just vanish. Belatedly realising he'd not left a note. Oddly half-accidental, half-intentional. He didn't want to confess to this madness.

Deciding to ask her along on the next trip... if there was one, Hiccup mounted up.

- - - - -

Without any commands, Toothless manoeuvred himself to face into the wind. Opening his wings, he took to the air. It was the wierdest take-off Hiccup had ever experienced. He was literally flying backwards, as they climbed higher and higher.

The ground vanished into a grey haze, before everything became a vague outline. His meagre human senses inadequate for these conditions. It took all his concentration to control the Toothless' tail. He trusted the dragon to keep them both safe.

It was the wildest, most untamed flight he'd ever experienced. Reminding him, of that first test flight, where he'd taken Astrid up. But, this was driven by sheer passion. Toothless effortlessly shifting from one gust to the next. Hiccup's stomach clenched as he felt them rise and fall so rapidly, and so blindly, he could do little more than cling on. Half expecting to smash into the ground at the pit of any descent.

- - - - -

With a few familiar motions, he encouraged Toothless to go higher. The night fury took great pleasure in choosing a tempestuous rise. But, Hiccup was rewarded, when they suddenly broke through the clouds.

The view was unlike anything he'd ever imagined. The peak of the mountains appearing like islands in an ocean of clouds. He found tears coming to his eyes, the breathtaking beauty was beyond belief. This was something he had to share with Astrid!

Looking down, he saw how the clouds squalled beneath them. A throaty chuckle from Toothless confirmed he was sharing the same thought. This beauty came with a price, a risk endured for this view.

https://i.pinimg.com/564x/bb/ad/6c/bbad6...

It wasn't a vista he was ready to surrender yet! From the ground so terrible, and depressing, the contrast was incredible.

- - - - -

Reminding himself it was a second test flight, and despite Toothless' enthusiasm there was danger in those clouds. He stroked Toothless' warm neck, reaching to scratch beneath his chin, and behind his ears. He deserved the reward.

They headed back down, their vision turning grey once again, as the clouds swallowed them. Hiccup trusted the Dragons' other senses. That echo vision seemed to work well in the cave, so perhaps it guided him here. He was sure Toothless was more atuned to the wind too. Able to read the subtlest changes, and lean into them.

However, it was still a savage ride. The storm pummeled them and drove them off course. Navigation was impossible for Hiccup, and he doubted he'd get his bearings until the storm cleared. The flight was far longer, far too long, and Hiccup realised they'd been blown away from Berk, and they were merely seeking somewhere to land.

- - - - -

Chilled to the bone, and soaked through, every gust was now agony. He was quivering in his saddle, as Toothless sniffed and snuffled. His head moving alertly, the scent of something in his nostrils. "Thata boy." Hiccup chittered through chattering teeth.

It was one of the quickest, sharpest, roughest landings he'd ever had. Hiccup didn't recognise where they were... Hardly surprising since he couldn't see further than a few feet in any direction.

"We need some cover..."

Toothless led the way, finding a cave for their shelter. Even a dragon needed to rest after such an exhausting feral flight. Curling protectively around him, Hiccup finally felt some heat seep into his bones, and his clothes turning from icy, to merely wet. He fell asleep as they started to steam, warmed by the dragon's body heat.

- - - - -

On waking, Hiccup cursed his lack of preparations. He'd not packed any food, and his stomach rumbled. Toothless too, was looking famished. He knew Toothless could manage to feed himself, hunting small creatures and fish. He could 'hunt' for something edible himself, berries, nuts and mushrooms were plentiful on the archipelago.

It was like night and day. The sky was pale blue, crystal clear, and free from any clouds.

111332232332114.content:
Hiccup whirled around to face the source of the voice. It was a bent and withered woman, immediately he was reminded of Gothi. There was something aetherial about her speech, and face. She spoke with a distant voice, and her gaze seemed to struggle to focus on Hiccup. Although, she were looking at him, she seemed to see through him at times.

"You have a very playful dragon." she observed, as if dragons and viking being friends were accepted anywhere beyond Berk. But, her smile was wide and genuine. Hiccup could hear Toothless splashing nearby. More playing with the water than hunting the fish in it.

"Sorry... I didn't mean to intrude." Hiccup apologised, reach a hand out to shake hers.

When she didn't react, Hiccup turned it into a clothes smoothing gesture. "Frankly, I'm a little lost..."

Furtively, he confessed, "I don't think we've ever landed here before. Certainly, I'd remember meeting you."

"Yes... Yes..." she answered, gripped again by that distant look, "You shall."

As her eyes refocused on Hiccup, "We?"

"Toothless and myself..." he assured her. Not wanting her to feel outnumbered, or outflanked.

"I took off on my own, my friends...." his voice tailed off, unsure how to finish. Not wanting to sound like he'd abandoned them, or leave him feeling too exposed.

She traced a circle on the cave floor. Stabbing the centre of it with her staff, a fire burst into life. A little startled, he thought of his dragon blade. Sure that this had to be similar, but it was still surprising, and an insight into how he must look to others unfamiliar with his mechanic curiosity.

"Thank you, it is still cold here without Toothless."

Looking the fire over, he studied it, "Astrid is usually the first to complain of the cold...." He babbled. "She's my friend."

"More than a friend." The woman observed. "Well, you speak of her softly, and with respect in your voice."

"I just wish I understood her better." Hiccup half-muttered, before blushing. "She is special." He confessed.

"You shall..." she answered in her airy tone.

Without further words, she looked up and away from Hiccup. A disturbing look, since she was looking at the cave roof, as if reading the weather in the sky.

As she walked away, she spoke again. Without looking back she addressed Hiccup.

"Your dragon is refreshed and rested..."

She was heading towards an odd building. It was a jarring mix of impressively large, and vaguely ramshackle. With an air of disrepair, but love too. The surrounding terrain turning into an impromptu herb garden at some unknown point.

11133223233221.content:
Ingrid lived an isolated life. Finding peace and solitude on her own little island. She felt exiled there by the shame of her youth. The fish in the nearby waters were plentiful, and the forest dense enough for her to build her own cabin, converting her former ship to form the roof.

She was spurned by Stoik the Vast, a viking she'd lusted after in her youth. One who she'd opened herself up to, only for Valka to swoop in and steal her man. Her love, her passion, her conviction was known by the whole village, so she was shamed by his betrayal.

Fleeing the village, she sailed off, escaping her shaming by their chief. It had been a lonely life, without much of a beach, even visits from Trader Johan, were brief and vanishingly rare.

It was by chance that she found the chest, while exploring her attol. Possibly it had been buried in the past, but the weather had revealed it's lid, and the thick brass bands, that showed it's contents were valued, by someone.

- - - - -

Taking it back to her hut, she worried the lock loose with determination, to discover a selection of books, a collection of small vials, and a crystal ball. The instruments of dark magic. She wasn't interested in that.

However, her own library was quite brief, and curiosity drove her to read the various books, on the long winter nights.

Ingrid came to understand the uses of the dark magic. Even the reasons for fearing it, and there were plenty. However, she also found that certain enchantments didn't seem of feel dark, well, not inherently, if they were used for dark purposes...

Shaking her head, she dismissed that train of thought.

- - - - -

Using the glass, she kept a distant eye on her village. Getting wildly drunk with joy, when Valka was taken and killed by dragons. Waking with a migraine, she considered returning to comfort and console her man.

However, his passion still burned deeply for her despite her absence. Even fanning the flames higher to know she wasn't there to grow old, and wither, or turn into a crone, or a nag. The sight of his sorrow started to turn her heart black.

She watched as the child who should've been hers grew into a shameful mockery of a man. It was cold comfort that their imaginary son would've been strong and hearty.

Between one viewing and the next, she was enraged to discover he'd gone from a joke... a Hiccup, to a hero. A man whose advice was sought, who was rich with friends, and had a maiden by his side ready to fight and defend him.

1141112111.content:
He was writing down the details in his notebook. The one he kept for personal study. He had another that he shared with Fishlegs, and any other rider who was interested. However, he'd learnt that he couldn't entirely trust the judgement of some of his fellow riders.

Twisting the lenses this way and that, he watched the images change on the wall. He'd noted down all the preparations needed for the initial transformation. But, he was still hunting for the restoration.

He mused on how Toothless would be if he took the restorative, and if he'd see that as a punishment. The idea of flying under his own power was intoxicating. His wings were fine for gliding, but they only had a limited range. With a proper draconian body, he could fly at Toothless' side. Compete personally against Stormfly, Hookfang and not merely as a rider.

- - - - -

Finally, he found the various components to transform a dragon back into a man. Noting it all down, he shuddered to think what would happen, if Ruff or Tuff found these notes. They were destructive enough, as human. He wondered what species they'd even prefer.

Scratching Toothless, appreciatively under the chin. The dragon gave a throaty chuckle and allowed the smoulder fire to fade, and the projected images to vanish. Tucking it back into its' hiding place. Hiccup carefully assembled the ingredients, prepared the fire and started to brew up the two potions.

The marble pot was green and held the one to transform him into a Night Fury. A loose scale plucked from his tail, where it had worked loose was added last.

The potion for his restoration was very similar, but with a cutting of his hair to restore him to human form.

- - - - -

As, the brews bubbled away, Hiccup wrote out a note, warning anyone against interfering with the potion. He labelled it as dangerous, experimental and explosive. He certainly didn't want to let any of his friends know what it truly was...

Covering the restorative in the grey marble pot, he covered it with a lid. It didn't need to be hot, but he didn't want it contaminated either.

Feeling he'd done everything in advance. His eyes were drawn to the green pot. If he drank that, he could be Astrid's night fury. She could have the ability to go as fast as he could on Toothless. He wondered what it'd feel like, to have a tail, to have wings, to sense the shifting breeze.

Despite it's allure, there was still a doubt at the back of his mind a hesitatation at the risk.

Making a last delay, he moved the grey pot out of the way. He didn't want it spilled accidentally. Even the pot seemed to be calling out to him. The scent tempting him to take a swallow, and experience flight as never before.

It tasted foul, like he'd never imagined. But, he choked it down. It warmed his throat as it went down. The heat spreading to his arms and legs with undue haste. He looked Toothless in the eye, as a dizzy spell hit him.

Sinking to his knees, he toppled onto his hands and knees. It felt right, this new posture.
1141112111.title: Hiccup becomes a female Dragon!

1141112111111.content:
Hiccup felt his bones shift beneath his skin. Skin that was darkening as it rose up his body. His whole body now draconian below the neck. He could feel the tail swish over the cobbled floor. Unfamiliar muscles flexed, and he felt the draught from his wings wash over his body.

His eyes turned moist, as he blinked away a wave of pain that washed over him. His cheek bones cracked beneath his face. His jaw shattered as it widened, knitting together far wider apart. Screwing his eyes shut, as the changes rose up his face. He could feel the loss of sensitivity, of movement. In their place, his senses shifted.

Breath huffed in and out his nostrils. The scents now sharper, more precise. He could smell the embers of the fire, even the last meal that had been cooked there. His hearing was more focused. Earflaps felt more mobile, able to move to better hear from any particular direction.

As he cautiously opened his eyes, he could see farther. The heat of the fire was blurred, Even the walls seemed to shimmer, as he looked at them. Some instinct informed him this was heat he was seeing. The residue of previous visitors.

Even as the thought occurred. He turned his heavy head to the pots that had held the potions. Their level was clear, without looking inside.

Rolling his head, he flexed the tension from his spine. His whole body was far more agile, lithe than before.

- - - - -

Then it hit him... The insight striking like a revelation. Beyond questioning, beyond doubt. Hiccup knew he was a female. A female night fury, snuffling she drank in her aroma. She picked up Toothless' too.

115.content:
Hiccup was passing through the village, his mind was wandering and he bumped into the village wise woman. With a crash they both fell to the ground. Apologising profusely he jumped to his feet and helped her up.

He helped Gunna back to her feet. Before he could stop himself he was offering to help her back to her home.

The young viking usually shunned it, with rumours and stories that delighted and scared them as children. Of course, he was too old and worldly for such things. But he still cast a mindful glance backwards, wanting to be sure that at least one person could see where he was going, should she turn him into a snake or some such thing.

She barely talked, and only leaned lightly on the young viking. Hiccup was happy with silence himself, and perked up when Mildew looked to be walking towards him ... probably with another complaint about dragon damage ... When he saw Gunna, he changed his direction and broke eye contact.

"Won't you at least come in for a 'thank you' drink." she offered when they reached her front door.

He felt a bit silly for being so quiet. "Are you sure, you're all right?" he asked intending to turn down the drink. After all, Toothless needed a good flight and his father would have a job for him .... or ..... or Gobber could use his help...

As if sensing his unease, she skewered him with a sharp gaze, making him even more nervous.

"I can answer your questions..... all your questions." before she vanished inside her home. But the door remained open behind her.

"Son of Stoic the Vast, you have made a deal with me. By giving me your Blood you have sealed it. In this case, you will change to bear what was said here. I do not tell lies." She said in a haunting tone.

Hiccup wouldn't believe it. He knew that she was a hag. Why does everything go wrong in his life? How will he explain it? As he grabbed the door handle again a shock went through his body.

He crumbled to the ground. He had to steady himself. That was weird he thought to. That had never happened before. As he reached for the door handle it opened up to the outside. He looked back. Hiccup followed Gunna into her home. The smoky air obscured virtually every detail, in the main room. But as he stood in the doorway, fires burst into life around the room. Even Hiccup knew what that was for. As she slowly sat down she pointed to the chair on the opposite side of the table. Dutifully if a little perplexed, he sat down.

"Questions?" he asked, half joking. But unsure himself whether Gunna was going reveal anything.

He looked into the ball, as she wove her hands around, "paths not taken. Futures unknown..." her voice was weaker and more distant than usual. She was being even creepier than Hiccup liked, and he had a fondness for the unusual.

12112.content:
Astrid was wandering through Berk, not paying attention to where she was going. She was feeling very frustrated, having just failed to pull off her latest stunt. It had left Stormfly with a badly injured wing, and herself with badly injured sense of dignity.

She'd broken into floods of tears, a rare display for her. But the idea that her pride had caused Stormfly pain just tore at her heart. Until Fishlegs and Hiccup arrived on the scene, she just watched horrified, as her dragon's wings, just flailed, painfully. Hiccup wanted to comfort Astrid, but was himself pulled towards the Deadly Nadder. Fishlegs examined the injured wing, tentatively running his fingers over the membrane, and the bones beneath.

"She'll be fine... But she needs to rest and recover."

Astrid promised her dragon would eat only the finest chicken until she was sky-worthy again. She found herself clamped onto Hiccup's shoulder, as the pain and fear left her body.

Hiccup wanted to be sure that she was okay, but Astrid wanted to be alone. Hiccup was used to Astrid, and kissing her gently on the cheek let her wander away. He called out re-assurances to her as she left. Dearly she wanted to go with Stormfly, but wasn't sure she could handle the rising guilt, or the endless questions from Fishlegs.

Even stomping along the beach angrily was frustrating. The sand just absorbed her angry footsteps, and the advancing waves, just washed her prints away.

The night was falling before she realised how much time had passed. It had given her a lot of time to think. She had come to some very uncomfortable conclusions.

That deep down, that she felt as a woman... somehow unworthy of being with Hiccup. She knew that her place in viking society was not as high as the boys. They were expected to become warriors, to fight for the clan... to fight for the village ... to fight for his partner.

She thought of her only female companion. Ruffnut. Tolerated only for her stupidity, and the fact that she was too similar to her brother. Her entire life had been a battle to be taken seriously. And now.... And now, it had caused Stormfly ... she sobbed, as she considered how much worst it could have been. Her arrogance... her pride... her insecurity...

If only she didn't feel the need to prove herself... everyday ... every time ... if only she'd been born a boy. She could be a friend to Hiccup, an equal...

She was disturbed from her thoughts, by a small cough.

Looking around, then down... she spotted Gunna. Berk's wise woman.

"Feeling a little distracted are we?"

"Feeling a little out of place..."

From behind her back she pulled out a scarlet stone. It glowed with an internal light, sparkling and teasing.

A trickle of salt water entered his mouth, waking him. Arvid woke spluttering, with the back of his hand he wiped away the dribble of saliva coming from the corner of his mouth. He felt a little disoriented as he looked around.

He ran his hand over his chin, once more. Just to confirm.

Yup... he needed a shave. Beneath his breastplate, he discovered he was male. That he was... he guessed Hiccup's brother.

He wasn't sure how he knew that... but it felt right. A fact that couldn't be doubted.

Gunna...

He cast about for the wise woman, but she was nowhere to be seen. The chill of the sea was seeping into his leggings, so Arvid got to his feet. Or rather he would have, but once he was kneeling he found the scarlet stone was still beneath his hand. It was quieter, and he found himself reluctant to let it go.

There was still activity deep inside. He finished standing up, and looked around for the simplest way back to the village. Idly he cast the stone back and forth between his hands.

The image of Hiccup came to mind.

http://40.media.tumblr.com/b7b64047d385c...

He could settle into being the best viking he could be. And there was also the fact that Snotlout wouldn't be hitting on him.

Arvid was Hiccup's younger brother. But Arvid was happy with that, she...-damn it- he'd have to get used to the damned pronouns. He trusted Hiccup.

As he caught the stone in his other hand, Hiccup came to mind again.

http://41.media.tumblr.com/17c8e1e05a55f...

1213.content:
Astrid was petting Stormfly and feeding her some chicken. The Deadly Nadder doted on her rider, but sensed that Astrid was upset she cooed, and purred encouragingly.

But Astrid sat down on a rock, she was clearly preoccupied, as she played with the chicken in her hands.

"I'm not the only female dragon rider..." she said quietly aloud, as if it needed clarifying.

But Ruff was like her brother, somehow you just didn't count her a girl. She knew it was unfair, but she just didn't feel like the rest of the riders accepted her as freely as the others.

Well, she knew that Hiccup liked and appreciated her, and her skills. But Fishlegs who would never say anything was quite old-fashioned in his upbringing, would cast glances in her direction. And as for Snotlout, he was as sexist as his father. Both had very definite ideas for the position of viking women in the village, and it wasn't on the back of a dragon.

She grew frustrated, and tossed the chicken in the air. Stormfly, snatched without effort.

But Stormfly wasn't satisfied, and maybe a little angry with her rider. With a quick flick of her tail, a spine shot loose, and grazed Astrid on the arm. Before sticking in the wood behind her.

"Stormfly. Bad girl." Astrid snapped, her hand covering the wound, until she could bind it.

12131.content:
Astrid bound her upper arm, occasionally wincing as she tightened it. Her mind was on Stormfly... It wasn't like her to miss... or make a mistake.

The dragon didn't even seem especially sorry for injuring her rider, and that just wasn't like her.

Astrid felt a little woozy, as she headed out. She decided not to ride her nadder, and rather enjoyed the puppyish way that she followed. Her stomping pace filled with joy. Nobody knew Stormfly better than her, but she had to confess that there were two others in the village that she'd defer to on general dragon matters. It certainly wasn't Ruff and Tuff.

Both Fishlegs and Hiccup were curious and caring regarding their dragons... and well... everybody's dragon. It struck her as ironic that she was the girl,... and more of a viking warrior than either of them. She tried to picture the boys as if they'd been born girls, and shuddered at the image she conjured up.

Now she could more easily image herself as a boy. Her arms muscular and powerful, rather than just toned and defined. She headed onwards... her imagination cheering her as she went.

121311.content:
She rapped on the heavy door, "Come on Hiccup... It's time for morning patrol." she gruffly barked through the door. Her voice felt gravelly and harsh. She took a swallow from her flask, and tried again. "Come'on Hiccup... Don't you want to feel the wind in your hair?"

Her voice was still huskier than it used to be, and she tentatively rubbed her throat. It was odd, as it didn't feel sore, or dry.

Her train of thought was derailed, as the door crashed open, and out sprang Toothless, with Hiccup already in the saddle.

"Okay bye, Dad." he called over his shoulder, as Toothless, hunched on his powerful legs. With a single bound, and a powerful sweep of his wings, he was airborne.

"Come on, Astrid, Catch up... Don't you want to feel the wind in your gorgeous hair -hmm- in your hair."

Her cheeks flushed at the compliment, as she leaped onto Stormfly's back. Spreading her wings wide, she took two long strutting steps, and joined them in the air.

As Toothless, lead the way forward, sensing the air currents, and flying with a natural grace. Stormfly, could match toothless for speed... well almost, and for agility it was also close. Keeping her body close to Stormfly's neck, she enjoyed the warmth of her scaly body, and whispering encouragement to go faster.

It was then that she noticed something. She wasn't as comfortable in the saddle as normal, and the cold didn't feel as bitingly sharp either.

Reaching down, she tried to adjust herself, to free whatever piece of armour or fold of cloth was causing her discomfort. As she did she pulled her nadder back, now was not the moment to overtake, she told herself.

However, it was herself that almost gave the game away, as a startled, shriek burst from her throat. She snatched her hand back. Hiccup must have heard something, as he turned around, a question on his face.

But Astrid waved it away.

She looked at her hand accusing it of betrayal. But squirming in the saddle, didn't settle her fears... her concerns...

More cautiously, she tried again.

Again she snatched her hand back.

She had... she had boy bits.

Clamping her teeth together she stifled a whimper of fear. Looking once more to ensure Hiccup wasn't looking, she peeked down her top.

The girls were still there. She felt a sigh come, as her tension eased. As a viking, she worked out, and was glad not to be as .... top-heavy as some of the village women. She wanted, and fought to keep a more stream-lined, body, a body fighting fit. Although she did enjoy the furtive glances that Hiccup cast her way, when he thought she wasn't looking.

Before her eyes, she felt a tightness in her chest, and before her eyes. The girls, Her girls, shrank, pulling tighter in.

There was no denying it she was losing her gender. She was becoming a male viking.
