12133.content:
Plucking the dragon's spine from the wall, she wondered what had triggered Stormfly's outburst. She was always friendly, and especially to herself. Even during training, she knew that Stormfly was being very careful with her aim.

There was a rough knocking at her door. The rhythm told her it was Snotlout, he rather fancied himself as a ladies man. Astrid thought that he was rather repellent, and tried to avoid being alone with him. She just didn't have Hiccup's tolerance for Snotlout's behaviour. With a sigh she got to her feet. She didn't like his manner, but it was clear where it came from. His father was one of the worst vikings, Spitelout. Not that he was bad, but he was very traditional.

She shuddered to think of what village life would be like if he were 'The Chief', she'd certainly never be allowed to ride Stormfly ever again, and probably find herself betrothed to Snotlout.

With a tremble she directed her thoughts into more pleasant avenues.

She opened the door, and saw Snotlout standing there. It was then that she spotted she still held the spine in her hand, one end glistened, and it wasn't the crimson of her blood. She snapped her eyes back to Snotlout.

121331.content:
When he saw the dragon spine in her hand. He thrust the flowers onto Astrid, not caring that he was forcing it at her without looking. He snatched the spine from her hand, as the flowers fell to the ground.

"So do you make daggers, with these." he asked sensing a business opportunity.

It took him a moment to see that in his haste he had cut his own hand. Opening his hand a few drops of blood dripped to the ground.

What neither of the young vikings noticed was the dark liquid that covered the edges.

Astrid was loathe to examine Stormfly's spines too closely. Somehow, it just felt a little bit of an invasion of privacy. But she assumed they were coated with something to sting, a victim or numb them, perhaps make them sleepy.

Fishlegs had some even more wild theories about the spines of a nadder. However, Astrid vetoed any experiments, and when Hiccup backed her up, he merely put on a disappointed face.

Snotlout lifted his eyes from his bloody palm, and came back to himself. As he looked at the posy of flowers on the ground, he saw that his moment was gone.

"Er.... do you want to practice manoeuvres with me and Hookfang." his toe digging a small hole in the dirt, as he twisted it back and forth.

"Yeah good idea." she answered.

Immediately he perked up.

"You round up the twins and I'll get Fishlegs, and Hiccup."

She didn't see the sudden change in expression and the disappointment the flashed across his face. She loved to fly, and hoped that it would take her mind off the tingling in her arm where Stormfly's spine had cut her.

Snotlout tucked the dragon spine into his belt, planning on wrapping it in a leather thong to make a handle. He might even present it to Astrid if it turned out well. He swung up onto Hookfang's neck, glad for the warmth in cold. At this time of day, the twins usually were by the shore trying to teach Barf and Belch new tricks.

Idly he rubbed his hand, hoping that the cold air would dull the tingling ache from his recent injury. Already he spotted Astrid head off towards Hiccup's home atop her dragon.

1213312.content:
As the pair practised their formation flying, Astrid noticed that Snotlout was flying differently. She clung tightly to Stormfly's neck to be as streamlined, as fast as possible. The Monstrous Nightmare was capable of immense power, but lacked the burst of speed that a Nadder could manage.

Stormfly's warmth helped her battle the windchill, as their pace increased. A chase naturally developing, as Snotlout pulled ahead. But it was Stormfly's smaller size and tighter turns, that allowed her to whip around the trees faster to take the lead. There was a draconian snort, a human growl, but she had the lead.

She felt as well as heard the beating of Hookfang's wings. Releasing her grip with one hand she pointed to a clearing, the wind mangled Snotlout's reply, but enough of the tone came through that his understanding was clear.

Landing in the clearing, the pair felt closer, as they both landed within moments of one another.

As if the dismount marked the end of their competion, they both jumped from their saddles. A darting look exchanged to see who was the first to hit the ground.

But, Astrid's cry of victory was stifled, as she looked... and then stared at Snotlout. As if waiting for the illusion to disappear. She caught the same look on his face, as she felt herself. Snotlout's look wasn't lewd, it was fascinated... horrified and fascinated.

"You.... You're...." they spoke over one another, their arms extended, pointing to each other.

Astrid pointing at the cleavage, visible beneath Snotlout's top. The swelling bust impressive in it's size and firmness. A glance southwards confirmed Snotlout was fully female.

But she also felt the same discomfort herself. Her arm falling limply to her side, Astrid's top feeling tighter, not fuller, not curvier, but more muscular, her shoulders broader, her arms beefier. Beneath her skirt... or was it more of a kilt now... She shuddered not wanting to think about that.... or what it meant.

121332.content:
"Can I have that spine?... oh, Hi... Are you okay?"

Snotlout was babbling excitedly, like Fishlegs was on the discovery of a new dragon. At that idea, her heart leapt, then faltered this was Snotlout, and her eyes narrowed.

"The spine... What do you want it for?" her voice suspicious.

"I have a new business, I can cut you in." the last part coming out as a grumble.

"Go on..." Astrid invited, trying to rouse some hope or enthusiasm.

His eyes darted for anyone listening in on his 'brilliant' idea.

"Daggers!" he said proudly. "I bind half in leather thongs, and there you go! The spines are lighter than Gronkle Iron, and almost as sharp."

He plucked one from his belt, holding it out swelling with his achievement.

"But they'll go dull."

"Repeat business." Snotlout's enthusiasm unblunted.

Keeping her sigh, low, she passed the spine over. It would be the quickest way to get rid of him.

His open palm was criss-crossed in red. Some still leaking traces of blood.

"Still learning?"

"Just perfecting my technique." He said defensively. When he spotted Astrid's injury he gave a dismissive snort. "An injury from your own dragon..." he said tutting his disappointment.

Instinctively, Astrid turned the cut away protectively. However, Snotlout reached out and grabbed her arm.

Their blood mingled and mixed, and for just a moment the pair swayed. Their vision swam, as their eyes watered.

Blinking and embarrassed, Astrid snatched her arm back. As she blinked enough to see herself a mixed look of horror, confusion and fear fighting for expression. Looking down, she spotted the hairy jerkin, and the meaty arms a sparse covering of black hairs on her... She swallowed... on his arms.

Looking back to her former body, she saw Astrid transfixed by her own bust, her arms rising inexorably to confirm it's reality.

"Hey." he barked. Louder, and harsher than he intended. But it had the desired effect, as Astrid's arms sprang back as if stung. Her heavy breathing, making her chest shift noticably, and Astrid saw her former lips tremble, on the verge of losing all control.

"This could be fun." It was a wicked, wild thought for Astrid, battling against her own alien body.

Looking Snotlout straight in her face, she pouted and blew her a kiss.

Snotlout flailed her arms wildly blocking even the sight, as her feet shuffled backwards. Her heels crashed against the front step, and she fell splay legged back inside her home.

Astrid invited Snotlout to sit down. She'd prepared a meal for them both, and had a speech planned. Astrid knew that Snotlout couldn't accept Hiccup as his chief. He'd been raised to dismiss the scrawny viking. Hell, even his name was a joke at his expense.

1213323242.content:
As Snotlout spooned the soup into his mouth, he was exaggeratedly enjoying it. Smacking his lips, and smiling his approval. He wanted Astrid on his side, when he was Chief. She scared him, not that he would ever, could ever admit that. But there was a fire behind those eyes, that scared him. A passion and dedication that he envied and feared.

Astrid was unconvinced by his theatrics, but was happy to play along. She spooned more soup into his mouth, until he was wolfing it down faster than he could manage, a cough brought a speck up. Sending a little trickling down his chin, when he spluttered.

He couldn't believe it, when Astrid swiped up the trail of hot soup with her finger, and offered it to him. Eagerly, he engulfed it, his lips forming a tight seal around her outstretched finger, as he sucked it clean.

Astrid's eyelashes batted wildly, but she was lost in the sensations evoked. However, she was also keeping a sly look at Snotlout's chest... Or perhaps, bust would be a better term.

His thick fur cloak, was bulging. Beneath that, his shirt was pushed forward by a couple of sizeable breasts that hadn't been there before. A brief hiss of empathy burst from Astrid's lips, as she imagined carrying such fulsome breasts herself. A glance down her own bust, and she was glad for their more modest dimensions. Enough to please, but not so much they define how she was seen.

Thankfully, the other herbs kept Snotlout oblivious to these changes. Only their revelation, would snap him out of the dopey haze, as his body was in flux.

She imagined that being triggered by Spitelout, her father. There was no way, he was going to react calmly to his son becoming a busty, lusty young viking woman.

- - - - -

As Snotlout, shifted uncomfortably in his seat. Astrid tried to estimate how much his rump had swelled. It was hard to tell, but it was certainly into womanly territory now. Smooshing and shifting, as Snotlout shifted.

An opportune look confirmed that Snotlout was now undeniably female. The brief flash of camel toe, clear through the fabric of his, or rather her pants.

His hand was slimmer, too. Snotlout's crooning compliment now delivered with an almost girlish trill. Astrid wasn't sure whether it was amusing, or disgusting.

As Snotlout leaned in for a kiss, she decided.

Her hand slipped between them, blocking lips that were now full and pouting.

"Too far, Snotlout." She told her simply.

With her free hand, she pointed to the door. Effortlessly ejecting Snotlout into the night. Destined to wander the night in a haze, and struggle with unclear memories of the night. Mysterious plots, mingling with fantasy, and reality to confuse the newest female viking in Berk.

Astrid snorted in amusement, as Snotlout walked away. Her hips moving seductively by purest instinct, an natural urge she'd never be able to curb.

With one potential rival for the Chief out of the running, she'd confirmed the potion worked. She'd be sadder to lose Hiccup, but the village needed a strong leader, and that was her.
1213323242.title: As Snotlout spoke he started to change....

choices: "Loki... Loki... Loki." The twins chanted their favourite theory.
"It could be a punishment." Astrid mused.
"I have no son." Wailed Spitelout. Looking his daughter over...
Gothi arrived last, hobbling in with her oversized walking staff.
12133232423.content:
Astrid practised her surprised face in the mirror, not wanting to reveal her fore knowledge of Snotlout's change. She was also planning on adding the final metaphoric dagger in her spine.

Well, the inspiration came, when she spotted that he'd left behind more than his manhood. She'd left behind his mace.

Astrid mused on the evidence, and decided to use it to further obscure Snotlout's night. She still hadn't heard the scream of Snotlout's discovery.

It was a cumbersome and blunt weapon, just like it's wielder. Sneaking it off into the night, Astrid took it to the statue of Thor. If Snotlout remembered her from the meal, she could plant the idea that he'd left as a young viking man, and headed out to gather his thoughts.

- - - - -

As she walked, she mused on which God to use to deflect any blame. She considered Thor being unhappy and punishing him. Freya rewarding him with a promise she'd serve as a Valkyrie once she passed. But, the chance to throw the twins an excuse for chaos also amused her. Loki was a prime suspect for things like this.

Keeping to the path, so she didn't leave any foot prints in the grass. She approached the heavy stone statue with a degree of fear. It was one thing to not really believe, but it felt more dangerous to actually blame one for her actions.

She left the mace at Thor's feet. Heading back home, she scuffed her feet through the gravel, hiding even that trace of her arrival and departure from sight.

- - - - -

Once she was back on the more heavily used parts of the path, she started to walk normally. She was aware she might be observed. Her senses were alert, but it was impossible to look in every direction. Besides, a gliding dragon was almost silent and stealthy.

Astrid considered widening her plans. There was something comforting and familiar with staying a woman. Even to gain masculinity and become chief felt unsettling. If she made it so that Thor appeared angry with the men, especially the male dragon riders, she could argue that she was a natural choice.

The idea of turning Tuff into Ruff's sister, made her shudder. But, smiling to herself, was quite sure that Tuff would be the least bothered by her upcoming regendering. Astrid considered keeping Fishlegs male in case she needed to put the blame on a human agent.

Certainly Hiccup would be part of this divine intervention. She could claim leadership of the tribe without undermining him in some way.

- - - - -

There was a roar of anger, followed by a shriek. The roar was a man's, and the shriek a woman's. Knowing her plan was developing, she picked up her pace. Heading towards the screaming, that had descended into an argument, raised voices and accusations.

The lights spilling from windows as they opened flickered, but announced that the whole village was about to discover the new Snotlout!

- - - - -

Astrid was glad not to be the first to arrive. A ring of spectators already encircling the distraught Snotlout. She was doing her chant, as it that were proof of her identity. "Snotlout, Snotlout...Oy, Oy, Oy..." But, it sounded terrible in her new voice, and shook with her chest sobbing.

Hiccup was in the front row, and had spotted some detail that convinced him that somehow, this was Snotlout, despite her new bust, and butt.

Ruff and Tuff, were already crashing their helmets together and thanking Loki. Fishlegs was flicking through his dragon book looking for some kind of flame or draconian weapon that would do this...

"Who's this?!" she asked angrily, as she worked her way to the front row.

"Astrid!" she wailed, reaching out for Astrid's shoulder armour. She recoiled convincingly, "Who are you?" she demanded folding her arms firmly.

She looked dismayed that Astrid couldn't recognise her as Snotlout.

"I think it is Snotlout." Hiccup said, pointing to a familiar scar, even if it was on an unfamiliar arm.

Seeing and hearing Hiccup's reasoning, Fishlegs was quick to join in. Absently pawing at the new woman who battled her shocked and muddled memories to have her original identity recognised.

"This one... I know this one." Fishlegs indicating a scar on her neck. Apparently, unaware that she was missing the view of cleavage he'd flashed by shifting Snotlout's fur jerkin.

However, it was a chill evening, and the icy air didn't miss the opportunity. Snotlout's eyes going wide, as she felt her nips harden, diamond sharp at the intruding weather. Cheeks blushing crimson, she snatched her jerkin back, and covered up the exposed flesh.

This only made Fishlegs babble, blush and back away. Astrid listened as questions flew Snotlout's direction. Thankfully, her name didn't come up. She admitted only to confusion, and not knowing what had happened, or when it had happened.

1223.content:
Astrid was looking out over the sea. Stormfly had found her a spot that none of the others had discovered. It was just below the snowline, with a view over Berk that glittered red in the light of the fading sun. Stormfly was snoozing behind a pine tree leaving her to explore the rest of the ledge.

It didn't take her long there was only room for a couple of dozen trees and a few boulders. She was cursing her fate. That as a viking female she would be expected to marry and give up her freedom to be somebody's wife. She looked over to her dragon, his eyes flickered fitfully behind his lids as he slept a puff of smoke escaping his nostrils as he dreamed.

"It just isn't fair." she muttered aloud.

She liked Hiccup, at least she thought she did. Whenever she thought of him she felt confused and angry and happy, and more confused.

But surely it would be fairer if she were the Chief's son, and Hiccup was the girl. She trained harder and everybody knew it. Hiccup well, he was just Hiccup... smart and all... but, without his luck with Toothless, he'd still be a joke in the village and an embarrassment to Stoick.

Now there was a viking, he lead from the front, and nobody questioned his order.

That was all fine and dandy. But the world is the way it is. She jumped for a branch and started to do chin-up exercises. Quickly she reached ten before being satisfied with her progress. It was the branch that failed first, with a loud -crack- it snapped and she fell to the ground.

She rubbed her behind, glad that none of the other trainers were there to observe this particular moment. The vibration woke Stormfly and he looked over at his rider, his mouth forming a snarl, or perhaps a smile. A lick of flame cleaned his teeth and satisfied that she wasn't in 'real' danger he closed his eye dismissively.

12312.content:
Astrid felt like she couldn't breathe, her nostrils were filled with enough mucus to choke a dragon...and it didn't help that her head was absolutely killing her. She should have known she was coming down with something. Nothing for it now, she'd have to go see Gothi, the Elder for the village of Berk as well as the primary healer, and get some kind of medicine. Getting out of bed was absolute miserable, at least she wouldn't have walk all the way to Gothi's little hut. That was one of the best things about making peace with the dragons, they made travel so much easier.

The teen gingerly climbed off of Stormfly's back, cradling her head. Astrid staggered up the stairs that led to the top of the raised platform that Gothi's house sat on, the highest point in the village besides the Great Hall. The going was not easy, not when she was feeling so light-headed that she felt like passing out, and not to mention the fact that she was starting to feel the first chills of a fever. Astrid finally reached the top and sat down for a moment, panting and out of breath, before staggering up and knocking on Gothi's door.

The old woman opened the door and quickly motioned her in upon seeing the miserable state that Astrid was in. After a few moments of poking and prodding and general examination the healer limped off to a corner of her house and started rummaging and searching through the collection of bottles on the shelves. Gothi returned and wordlessly handed Astrid a bottle and written note instructing Astrid to drink the whole bottle as soon as she got home and to check back with Gothi or one of the other healers in a few days. "Thanks," Astrid mumbled, though due to her cold came out more as "dhunks", before gingerly heading back down the stairs and mounting Stormfly to head home.

She eagerly downed the bottle of medicine, an absolutely foul concoction barely fit for human consumption...though she did feel a little better all ready. It no longer felt like Thor himself was beating on her skull with Mjolnir and the headache had faded to a dull throb and she could breathe at least a little bit now. Mostly though, Astrid felt drowsy and made her way back to her room to lie down. Most of the day had passed by the time she returned to wakefulness. She was surprised at how good she felt, there was still a few lingering symptoms from her cold, but none that would prove too bothersome, and the worst was definitely over with. Astrid was suddenly brought to her senses by a very loud grumble from her stomach and realized she hadn't eaten anything all day except for Gothi's little bottle of medicine. She should feel this hungry though should she? Astrid couldn't put it off any longer, she rushed to the Hofferson's pantry and assembled as much food as she could carry and then some. She finished her first round, but still felt absolutely famished, by the time she felt satisfied she was sure she had eaten more food than she had ever had in her whole life. And now Astrid was feeling tired again.

The teen returned to her room and got undressed before crawling into bed again. By the time she woke again it was morning and all of the symptoms from her illness were completely gone. Astrid quickly got out of bed to start the day, Hiccup and the others would want to know that she was all right after missing whatever they had been doing for Dragon Training yesterday. As she wrapped her breasts in their bindings she couldn't help but notice that the cloth didn't seem to go as far as it normally did before she had to pin it in place...and not only that, her shirt felt much tighter on her than it normally should once she had gotten it on...her tights and skirt felt like they had gotten a little smaller as well. What was going on? Was she starting to go through a growth spurt or something...it honestly wouldn't be too out of place, she was only 15 afterall and still had plenty of growing left to do.

124111.content:
Astrid was working on her new trick to impress the other riders... well it was really Hiccup's attention that she wanted. She lead Stormfly into a downward spiral. She pulled her dragon in tighter and tighter, each circle increasing her speed. With a signal, Stormfly tucked her head and started to perform a loop.

A loop with her rider on the outside. Astrid was ready, her feet secure in the stirrups as she floated and the shot out of her saddle. The whoop that burst from her lips was just the encouragement her dragon sought. She shot between the chalk columns, the waves just a few feet beneath her flapping hair.

As she reached the peak again, she dropped back into the saddle. She settled back into the saddle.

Well, that was the plan. What Astrid hadn't realised was much further back. As she dropped from her straining legs, she wasn't above the saddle. She gave a yelp as she impaled herself on one of Stormfly's needle sharp spines.

It was painful, but she shot back up. She whimpered as she pulled it out. Dropping the offending spine into the sea. She leaned forward to stroke her dragon's neck, assure her it was her riders mistake.

Her frustration faded to embarrassment when she realised this wasn't an injury that she'd be able to hide well. She let out a rare but sincere curse when she thought about how her friends would react. She was going to be the butt of so many jokes, and crude attempts at sympathy.

Fishlegs would blush and probably ask some thoughtless question about Nadder spines.

Ruffnut wouldn't have any empathy fellow feminine sympathy, she was cut from the same cloth as Tuff.

Snotlout would make crass jokes and worse offers of helpful hands.

She scowled, Hiccup would be the worst, his sincerity and genuine warmth was oddly the hardest to take.

Stormfly flared her wings and landed gently on the beach. That didn't stop the hiss blasting between Astrid's teeth as her legs flexed on landing.

With a start Astrid woke, something was different... very different.
"Are you okay?" she groaned at the familiar voice, "That looks painful..."
1241112.content:
As Astrid dismounts tremors run through her muscles, falling to her knees, her vision goes dark. The last thing she sees is Stormfly curling around her trembling body forming a warming protective shield.

Her dreams are filled with thoughts of flight, and the freedom it brings. Freeing her from her responsibilities around the village. As a young woman of Berk, she is expected to marry and bear children. To defend her hearth and home, but leave the real fighting to the men.

Even her dreams she snorts at the idea of leaving her defence in the hands of Fishlegs and Tuffnut. The idea of her having to settle down, even if it is Hiccup that everybody sees as her natural mate.

She tosses uncomfortably, as she shakes off those thoughts, both enticing and scary.

If she were a dragon, she'd never have to make these kind of compromises. If she could just fly like Stormfly, the world would be perfect. Her dreams faded into incoherence and she dozed deeply, a rasping snore signaling her rest. A snore that no viking ever managed to convince her was real.

12411121.content:
With Stormfly keeping her warm, Astrid slept well into the afternoon. But as the Sun shot through the clouds, and the wing that was extended over her head. She gave a yawn and stretched. Sensing the movement, Stormfly uncurled, and was soon tilting her head to one side and then the other.

Both were unaware of how their movements mirrored each other.

Astrid scratched her ride under the chin with a promise of chicken when they reached home. Stormfly gave her an affectionate head-bump like an oversized cat. The best dragon... Astrid said half-aloud, as the ran a hand over her fine crest of head spines.

Astrid tried to guess the time by looking at the Sun. But as she covered her eyes, she spotted a membrane between her fingers. It was a vivid blue with splotches of greeny-yellow.

She'd have to fly fast to get home for dinner, and she appreciated the Nadder's speed. Only just slower than Toothless. It was half an hour of a flight, she had wanted the privacy to practice her new trick.

She stopped.

Since she'd woken she'd not felt any pain, relief welled up. She'd not have to hide her injury, and face the jokes of her friends. Bending to touch her toes, she shifted and stretched, twisting her waist one way then the opposite.

Something was off. She slipped her big comfy boots off. Her feet were cold, and as she ran her hands over them, her skin also felt harder and drier.

The light revealed a worrying colour as well. Her feet had the same hint of splotchy blue colouring, extending to just below her knees.

She slid the boots back on, and jumped into Stormfly's saddle. Seconds later, with a push from her powerful legs, and a languid flap of her wings, Stormfly was airborne.

Astrid had questions, and was worried about the shade of blue. More worried than she liked to consider.

However, there weren't any answers here. She'd need to find help from somewhere.

124111213.content:
Stormfly lands her just at her front door. Landing with a delicate ease that wasn't always obvious from the deadly nadder. A rather stubby and squat dragon. Only the rock types were more dumpier, and they were slower.

"Hi, Astrid." Hiccup greeted her cheerfully, racing up as Stormfly folded her wings to her side. She gave Hiccup a measuring stare, matched by Astrid.

He was out of breath, having spotted and chased the dragon, on spotting them from the ground. She wanted to confide in him, to share her worries. But... "Sorry, Hiccup. I can't fly a patrol, right now." she said dismounting.

A hiss of pain slipping unwanted from her lips, as she landed.

Hiding her hands, she headed for the front door. Not seeing Hiccup's pained face, as he watched powerless to help. Any thoughts of making her fly.... Hell!, if she didn't want to fly she must be ill.

"Astrid." he asked, ready to offer any help as she reached the door. Her death glare turned the words to ash in his mouth.

"Okay... I'll see you later." he finished weakly walking away.

Stormfly took to the air, as Astrid trudged deeper inside. Examining her hands she found the webbing thicker, darker, and spreading between her fingers. Happily kickig her boots off, she examined her feet.

The toes looked wrong. They needed cutting she thought idly, hell even her legs were starting to look blotchy in the half-light of her hut.

The aches drove her to her bed. She really needed to lie down. Every bone felt sore, every muscle stressed. Carefully peeling off her clothes, she found that same webbing seeming to spread. It was forming at her elbow, joining her fore arm and upper arm with elastic flesh. Her clothes obviously making that growth tight and painful, she tossed her top aside.

Yawning, she stretched, and saw that same membrane stretching between her elbow and torso. All looked the same shade of speckled blue that she associated with Stormfly. Listening carefully, she thought she could hear the familiar leathery flap of her wings circling above her home.

As if she were performing her own protective patrol. She only just made to her bed before collapsing there, and falling into a deep sleep.

12411212.content:
Trader Johann had come to Berk once again. It was hard to miss the enthusiastic exclamations of Bucket whenever Johann showed up. Astrid couldn't help but wonder what sorts of things the trader had brought to Berk this time. While weapons were always a good thing and of extra fine quality when they came from Johann, she already had plenty of them...there had to be something equally as useful as a good weapon.

Astrid strode down the docks to Johann's ship, browsing through the man's wares. Unfortunately, she wasn't seeing anything too interesting or out of the ordinary for Johann, sure there were plenty of interesting things, but none that really fit with what she wanted...Hiccup and Fishlegs would probably be all over the books or the things like ink for writing.
"Ah, Miss Hofferson," Johann called happily and making Astrid jump in surprise, "One of my most loyal customers. Anything catching your fancy or anything that I can interest you in?"

"Hey, Johann," Astrid greeted the trader, "And yeah, you certainly can. I was wondering what you might recommend," noticing the man about to speak she quickly held up a hand and cut him off, "That isn't a weapon...I mean they're always nice, or useful, but I have plenty of them all ready. So knowing what I'm like, what else might you have that I'd be interested in?" Johann seemed to pause in deep thought for a moment before beckoning her toward the hold. He began rummaging through several chests and other various containers before finally letting out a cry of success and returning to her with a small bottle.

"Ordinarily I don't put much stock in items claimed to be of a magical nature, but this is something I received from a very reliable source. I'd trust that it does what it's supposed to. This here, is a potion meant to increase the strength, endurance, and durability of the one who drinks it. I was guaranteed that whoever drinks this would become the most physically powerful man, or woman, alive," Johann declared happily. Astrid only looked at the bottle in suspicion, could he really be serious about all of this? You could definitely trust Johann not to cheat you and rob you blind, but his stories weren't always the most reliable. She had no doubt that the contents of the bottle he was offering were something worth a lot of money, but she wasn't sure she believed that it was a magic potion.

If it were true though, that would definitely be something that she was interested in. Especially now with all of the boys hitting their growth spurts, she was still the strongest and the most capable overall of the group, but they'd been catching up to her and she was afraid that they'd only surpass her as time went on....even Hiccup of all people had finally started to not only grow taller, but also to bulk up slightly, he was still a skinny twig, but he was getting there. This potion would give her the edge she needed to stay at the top if it actually worked. She decided she'd take the gamble and buy the potion. If it didn't work she could always tell Johann and he'd either get her money back or get her an item of roughly equal value. "I'll take it," Astrid declared firmly, digging around in her money sack for the amount that Johann specified.

After climbing out of the hold of the ship she rushed back to her home, she didn't want to drink the potion out in public and attract anyone's attention. She wasn't afraid of questions they'd ask, but she did worry that they might try to take it from her and use it themselves. Once inside the safety of the Hofferson clan's home and making sure that no one else from her family was around, Astrid gently pulled the stopper out of the bottle and downed the whole thing. It didn't taste awful, but it wasn't necessarily an experience she enjoyed either.....Astrid suddenly felt rather woozy and like she was going to faint, she could only assume that it was the potion taking hold....it was that or she'd accidentally poisoned herself. She staggered to her bed and crawled into it before her eyelids began to close and she passed out into a deep sleep.

choices: The mystery dragon smirked, and flew towards the village of Berk.

12423111.content:
Astrid was walking back across the fields. A chicken tucked under her arm ready as a reward for Stormfly. Astrid smiled to herself, thinking how intelligent her dragon was. Every inch as sharp as a Night fury, even if she didn't have his turn of speed. Besides, she was her dragon.

Astrid didn't spot the dragon swooping down on her. It's low flying glide leaving both sets of wings rigid, it moved silently. The only hint of its approach was the frightened behaviour of the cows placidly chewing the grass in the field. Even now, the cows were used to dragons, taking on the blasé calm of their humans.

She found herself engulfed in a cocoon of sickly yellow green flames. They swirled around her, clinging to her clothes and body with a delight that said they were more than simple flames. As Astrid curled up, and made herself as small as possible, she felt the warmth wash over her body.

But, it was only warmth, as she wasn't consumed by the fire. Instead, her clothes were turned to cinders, and she dropped the chicken in shock to find herself naked in the open field.

The dragon flashed past, ruffling her hair as it flew by. Taking it in, she spotted the twin wings one set behind the other, giving it incredible agility. She groaned at the observation she knew came from Fishlegs relentless observations. It turned in the air, as if spinning around some invisible pole. This time, she got to see the gout of yellow green flames erupt from it's gaping mouth.

To have already experienced it's fire wasn't any comfort, as the flames clung to her again.

This time however, her flesh shifted beneath their assault. Astrid watched horrified as her hands curled into useless lumps, her feet chilled by the dewy grass turned into similar lumps. In her panic it took her a few moments to recognise them as hooves. Cow hooves!

The third blast made the changes even clearer as her arms and legs distorted becoming the familiar splotchy legs of cows. The frazzled chicken raced, clucking for the edge of the field, and the camouflage of the dry stone walls.

Astrid was stuck, collapsed onto all fours. Her new limbs useless for helping her evade the next storm of flames. This one destroying the last vestiges of humanity. Astrid felt her bust swell, her breasts merging into one larger udder. The sensation of her tail bursting out was disturbing, but just as bad was the pain ripping through her skull, as it transformed into a dull bovine one, with cute stubby horns. A look backwards down her cow body showed no trace of humanity remained, nothing remained of Astrid.

Well, that detail she missed was her human eyes glistened with tears in an otherwise entirely bovine body.
12423111.title: A new type of Dragon

choices: Stormfly woke to her own changes....
Astrid's eyes remained the same human eyes, piercing blue, and soulful.
From the distance, the chase was spotted... and investigated.
Fishlegs spotted the unknown dragon, and drove Meatlug to pursue it.
124232.content:
Horses were rare on Berk, being a smallish island, there wasn't much need for their speed. But a small wild herd did roam through the forests. It took and agile dragon, and a clever rider to slalom through the trees chasing them.

With Stormfly's squat body, she was quite well suited for the tight turns and limited space between the trees. Astrid clung low to Stormfly's neck, the wind whipping through her hair, and down her back. She guided her dragon with the subtlest of hand and foot signals.

Her attention locked solely on the only stallion, the alpha to a pack of mares. The heirarchy intriguing Astrid, she'd observed them each battling for the favour of the leader.

Gliding silently above her, an unseen dragon dipped beneath the cloud layer, he observed the behaviour of the dragon and her rider. His paired wings, gently, effortlessly keeping it level. Tucking those wings back he descended.

Astrid was still oblivious until she heard the rush of flames just a few feet behind her. The jerk of her head, or her startled jerk were enough to distract Stormfly. Her wings started clipping the branches sending her tumbling down to crash and roll into the ground.

A tree trunk stopped Astrid, her head cracking on impact gave her double vision. As the mysterious dragon approached, almost feline in its' delight, and grace.

This time she was engulfed in flame. Awash with blue-green flames, she screamed, the noise lost in the blast of flames. Her shrill cry continuing even as the flames licked over her body. Licked over it, but left it unconsumed. It turned her clothes to cinders, and those cinders to breeze-blown nothing.

Once her body was revealed, she clutched her hands to hide her nakedness. But, now her body was rippling, and shifting. The arm across her bust was changing, her hand already curled into a fist, turned into a solid lump, incapable of opening. She felt a pain in her joints, as if they were bending in ways that were unnatural.

Astrid squirmed in her seat, a root was making the ground painfully uncomfortable. As she rose to her knees, she discovered it wasn't a root. A wandering hand discovered a tail, long flowing hair spouted from the base of her spine.

She barely noticed the flames continuing to flood around her, as she felt her rump get wider and fuller. Holding out her arm, she recognised it as a horse's foreleg, and hoof! Even as the confusion washed over her, she was driven onto all fours. Standing as best she could, Astrid found it impossible to straighten beyond that.

Even the idea of standing on two legs, on her hind legs felt wrong. She wasn't surprised to see hooves at the end of her legs too. A soft downy coat of fur now covered her from head to toe. Gritting her teeth against the pain she clenched her eyes shut, as the pain in her neck mounted higher and higher. The cracking bones impossible to miss. Her ears flicked, flinched at the sound.

Once the pain faded, Astrid risked opening her eyes. She took in her new body, she was a horse from nose to tail. Her sense of smell heightened, her vision duller. But one thing was certain, she could scent a dragon nearby. The safety of a herd was further away. Instinct gripped her, and in a flurry of hoof beats, she raced towards safety.
124232.title: Mare

choices: Astrid tried to lead them both back to the village, the village and Gothi!
Astrid's eyes boggle, as she spots Stormfly is now a colt... a male!

1242325.content:
As the fear left her veins, the scent of dragon now cleared from the air. Astrid was aware how strong her new equine instincts were. She'd have to fight to keep herself, her intellect in charge of this body. As a mare, she was terrified of everything.

Looking around she found herself in the middle of the herd. She felt thirsty and found herself walking towards the nearby river. It was intuitive, not needing conscious thought. Lowering her head to the water, she drank gratefully from the icy stream.

https://i.pinimg.com/originals/3e/3b/4f/...
Astrid

Catching her reflection in the water, she saw the same familiar piercing blue eyes, almost lost in that equine head. Still hauntingly human, and perhaps holding a way back. Or a trace of her humanity for recognition.

It would take a close inspection for anyone to notice that particular detail, Astrid admitted with some sadness.

Looking to the side, another horse tentatively approached the waters edge. Her new vision added to her fear, as Astrid was more aware of her surroundings. The field of view, so all encompassing. She barely had to move her head to see her broad flank, and equine rump. That thought sending her tail swishing in agitation.

The neighbouring horse snuffled at the water. Catching her attention, then Astrid spotted another detail. The golden eyes, were familiar.

There was a hint of a predator behind them. She recognised a transformed Stormfly, as her former mount recognised her rider. They nickered as the shared break-through, nuzzling, and rubbing their long, powerful necks together they shared their new common scent.

choices: Ruffnut stumbles out to join her sister.
"Okay tell me what happened?" Astrid asked.
1253.content:
Astrid was out with Stormfly, working on her flying. She knew that they'd never be as fast as a Nightfury, but that didn't mean they had to stop competing. Besides, she knew how Snotlout would be, if he ever thought he was the better flier.

She snorted at the idea, it was the kind of thing he'd claim, but all the riders were used to his bluster.

She clung tight to the dragon's neck as they whistled over the sea. Inches above the water, she glanced backwards spotting the wake they made as they went.

She spotted something from the corner of her eye. In a flash, Ruffnut burst from the treeline, "Astrid... Astrid... help!"

She waved wildly until, Astrid circled around and landed.

Astrid had never heard Ruff call for help before or Tuff, although it was more down to stupidity....

But it was enough to pique her interest. She stroked Stormfly's neck and jumped down.

Ruff was breathing heavily, and her cleavage was drawing even Astrid's eyes as it peeked between the loose strings of her top.

"What's up, Ruff?"

"That's the problem...." she answered panic filling her voice.

"I'm not Ruff... I'm Tuffnut."

"I'm not playing this game, Ruff." Astrid snapped angrily. She felt a degree of jealousy over Ruff's freedom. As a tomboy, Astrid had to fight for her place in the village. To not just be seen as a future bride... a wife for the next chief. Whereas Ruff and Tuff, were just Ruff and Tuff.

Ruff punched her on the arm.

"Stop it." she snapped again. She made an effort not to rub her upper arm. It had hurt more than she liked.

"Gah... I even punch like a girl now." she pouted.

"Tuff...." Astrid finally whispered, not sure what the joke was.

"Yeah.... That's what I've been saying...." she gestured to her own body.

"I mean who else would look like this..."

choices: Astrid steps between them. "Girls!" she snaps.
"Okay, One of you ... just ... just tell me what happened, please."
A shadow of a dragon passed over the small party.
12531.content:
"Running away.... just like a girl." Ruff cackled as she spotted her new sister.

"Oh Hey, Astrid. Don't you think Tuff's more girly, than I am?" she snorted as she asked. Cupping her hands in front of her chest, except further out.

Tuff felt the sting of the insult, and hunched her shoulders.

"You were always a girl, so you're more of a girl than me."

"Just wait, you'll be the one that Snotlout fancies... "

Astrid head was already aching, the twins were difficult enough, but now they were even more combative.

"You can run... and you did... and it was all girly.... but you can't hide." Ruff elbowed her sister.

"Uh Uh... I'm no girl." Tuff snapped back. Her voice was a little too high... a little too shrill to convince anyone.

"Astrid you hold her down, and I'll braid her hair properly."

She elbowed Tuff again, "Ask nicely and I'll add in some flowers."

"Sure you're not a girl." she smirked. Before wrapping her hands around Tuff's chest.

"Then what are these...." she jiggled and bobbled her new breasts, as Tuff's cheeks turned more and more red.

125311.content:
Tuffnut flashes an angry expression at Astrid, but still blushes.

"Yeah.... she's all girl."

As if she's naked, Tuffnut shifts her hands to cover her crotch, and turns her back on her sister.

"And you'll find Belch's saddle much more comfy too."

She gave her ass a resounding slap.

"Please. What happened?" Astrid took them each by the hair and pulled them down to her waist.

"Is Barf and Belch safe, do you even care about your dragon?" she snapped.

"Like of course." Tuff looked up.

"Besides, we've learned an important lesson. One that Fishlegs doesn't even know." Ruff added.

"Yah." Tuffnut looked up at Astrid, and she let them go.

"You know changewings...."

Astrid nodded, more than familiar with those particular dragons, masters of disguise and able to blend into any scenery.

"Well, they have other powers than their camouflage."

"Yeah... those leafy, things on their tails and the ones that trail behind their ears."

Again Astrid nodded, suspecting where this was going.

"Well you really shouldn't eat them...."

Astrid never thought the twins would be so reckless, but with a shrug had to admit that she wasn't really that surprised. However, she rode a Deadly Nadder, and the idea of eating her spines was .... well everyone knew how poisonous her spines were.

Although she suspected that Fishlegs was holding back some information from her. He did seem to change the subject a lot, and fidget whenever she brought up Stormfly's spines and attacks.

Barf and Belch crash lazily through the tree line. Belch smelling a little uncertainly her rider, before giving her a playful head bump.

"Well, we'd best head back to the village, before it's dark."

13.content:
Fishlegs was out riding Meatlug going from island to island trying to spot new and exotic dragons. He was quite studious -for a viking- and enjoyed sketching the various dragons. His illustrations were much appreciated by Hiccup, making the dragon book more accessible to the other riders.

He had spotted an island while on patrol, that he was sure hadn't been explored. It was quite big with a generous pine forest covering most of the surface. But what it lacked was what intrigued Fishlegs. There wasn't a single usable beach anywhere along the coast. The entire island was a precipitous cliff edge.

Also he saw that for such a safe spot there were remarkably few seagulls or any type of birds in the trees.
13.title: Fishlegs

choices: Unfortunately, the dragon spotted Fishlegs and sprang into the air.
The dragon spotted it's prey, and attacked mercilessly.
Snotlout arrived, rather loudly on Hookfang.
132.content:
As he flew over the meadow, he saw a dragon racing through the long grass. It was going at the pace of a galloping horse. It had a serpentine body, and it's wings were pressed tight to it's sides.

He focused on it with his full attention, he wanted to be able to draw it when he got home. The gronkle was hovering quietly sensing it's riders fascination.

Suddenly the dragon on the ground froze and slowly raised it's head sniffing the air. It was at that point that Fishlegs realised that this was probably the fastest dragon.... in existence. It's sleek body was designed for speed, it's armour was light, and it didn't seem to have much in the way of offensive weaponry. It lacked Meatlug's heavy tail, so couldn't bash anything, and the teeth while impressive weren't exceptional. But what was exceptional was .... and he had to look a second time to be sure... it had two sets of wings.

Fishlegs gave an appreciative whistle as he tried to estimate how fast it would be in the air too. Doubtless faster then Astrid's nadder and probably even faster than Hiccup's dragon.

He didn't even want to think what it breathed for a weapon. Dreading the idea that he might find out the hard way. With a gentle kick he guided Meatlug back towards Berk.
132.title: He saw a fast moving dragon... at least he thou...

choices: Fishlegs went to get his friends.
Slowly he approached where he saw the mysterious dragon.
133.content:
Fishlegs knew that Meatlug wasn't the fastest or most agile of the dragons. However, he was the one who could land and take off in the tightest spaces. So he flew onward looking for any small gap in the trees. He saw a flash of scales as his eyes peered deep into the green. He knew... He hoped that any dragons found here would be unique. His heart raced as he spotted a meadow. The steady fluttering of Meatlug's tiny wings a constant joy to his rider.

Although when he wanted Meatlug could fly almost silently, if a little slowly. That was something even Toothless couldn't do. Fishlegs puffed up a little at the thought is if this was down to his skills as a rider. Deep in his heart he knew that for all the strengths that his dragon had, and he would happily defend Meatlug against any comers. He knew that in a fight, a Gronkle wasn't going to win.

As they landed, he leaped off and landed in the swaying grass. Meatlug rolled over and gave himself a dust bath. Fishlegs stood nearby, his eyes watched the trees around the edge. A flash of green scales was all he saw..... before the head snuck back back behind the tree.

Fishlegs was almost dancing with delight. He hadn't recognised it at all. This was a totally new... totally unknown dragon...

He wanted to appear non-threatening, and gave Meatlug a tickle under the chin. A gurgling purring noise came out, and every muscle in the dragon went limp and relaxed.
133.title: He prepared to land.

choices: The creature fixed him with it's wide, deep hypnotic eyes.
Fishlegs was disappointed at what he saw...
Fishlegs was face to face with a completely unknown dragon.
1332.content:
Fishlegs was very fond of all dragons. Moreover, he was convinced that they only attacked when provoked or scared. Feeding could be a scary time for the uninitiated, but as long as you kept away from their food, they were perfectly lovely creatures. He knew that dogs reacted the same way too. Especially where there food was involved.

So he waited, patiently taking the time rest and catch some sun with Meatlug. The grass in the meadow was wild and tall in patches.

When he heard a rustling he turned and looked. The rustling stopped with the quiet crunching of a grass being flattened. He saw a scaly tail flick agitatedly from side to side, before settling down.

Deliberately, he turned his back on the noise and went back to scratching his dragon behind the ears. The rustling returned getting closer and closer to him. He kept calm as he guessed it was less than 10 feet away.

When Meatlug groaned in pleasure, hearing a snap. He glared past Fishlegs ready and willing to fight for his rider. But a steadying hand on the back of his neck settled the dragon. The huff of hot breath set the hair on the nape of his neck twitching.

With his heart racing, and on the verge of fainting, he turned slowly to see the new dragon.
1332.title: He sat down and waited.

choices: Thankfully, Astrid and Fishlegs could still speak.... with an effort.
His stomach grumbled, as he looked over the delicious rocks...
Astrid crashed into him, fury at her transformation clear in her bearing...
Sharing a glance, the three gronkles flew back to Berk.
13321.content:
Fishlegs was lost in it's eyes. They soothed and calmed him, even holding Meatlug in place. Distantly, he heard some awful noises, but they couldn't break him free from the fascinating expression of this incredible new dragon.

He was locked in place, as winds buffeted around him. The gust of a dragon landing, and flying straight off. He recognised Astrid's voice, a battle cry, and then it was silent. Torn with emotions, he managed to shift his gaze briefly. Spotting Astrid frozen in place, as he realised he was too.

His hope seemed to fly away with Stormfly who was heading back to Berk. But those damned eyes snapped him back to immobility. He was powerless to do anything, as it approached the frozen Astrid, his wide mouth, filled with razor sharp teeth easily big enough to bite her in half. But, it's nostrils snuffled, as it sniffed her from head to toe.

Her breathing was irregular and strained. Fishlegs realised, she was still fighting, and that renewed his resistance. Though he was still held immobile. Ponderously, it approached him next. Before he got a whiff of it's fishy breath, snorted in his face.

Tilting it's head one way then the other it seemed to muse on what to do. Returning to the new grunting Astrid, she sounded like she was making progress. But as soon as the dragon faced her down, the grunts decreased in volume. Nodding it's heavy head with resolution. It took a few steps backwards.

Unsure whether he expected it or merely dreaded it. He couldn't even cry out, as a gout of emerald green flames gushed from it's open mouth. In moments engulfing Astrid, the flames erased her from his sight, before reaching him, too.

Instinct shut his eyes, and he felt the heat wash over his body. He felt his clothes turn to cinders, and those cinders be blasted away, too.

It felt like his body was melting... No! Not melting, transforming. His eyes refused to open, as he felt himself topple forward, still frozen he landed on hands and knees, as the flames continued to consume him.

Finally the warm wind seemed to stop. Fishlegs looked around, spotting only a Gronkle where Astrid had stood. Feeling his muscles unlock, he raced off. Ignoring the gust of the dragon's wings as it took to the air. It took him a few moments to realise he was running on all fours.

The thunderous pounding of a Gronkle followed, it had to be Meatlug. It was only as he reached the safety of a nearby rock, that the rush of adrenalin left his body. As his pulse returned to something more normal, he was finally able to take in his own change.

Looking around, he realised he was a beautiful sturdy Gronkle. A second look, revealed a scowl on purple gronkle's face that identified it as Astrid.

https://64.media.tumblr.com/2de8967a1fe9...
13321.title: The creature fixed him with it's wide, deep...

choices: It started to cocoon Fishlegs in the gunk.
The slime bound Fishlegs to his gronkle.
13323.content:
As Fishlegs's eyes took in as many details as possible, he still couldn't work out where this dragon's strengths and weaknesses were. It was sleek like a nightfury, but the head was topped with a crown of spines that went from the top it's forehead before running down the dragon's 'spine'.

Besides the spineback, it was an iridescent emerald green. With wide eyes it looked intelligent, and his wingspan promised great speed. Well great speed compared with a gronkle, he hugged his dragon affectionately even as he admired the new one.

As the dragon opened his mouth, 'Oh boy' was all that he got out. He was looking for cover until he noticed there wasn't any light at the back of the creature's throat. He clearly wasn't planning on frying the young viking.

He decided it was safer to take a little initiative, and quickly stepped to the side of the creature's head. Before it could butt him aside he started to tickle it under the chin.

With a cough it started to purr a deep throaty noise, undeniably pleased. Fishlegs spotted something had come out it's mouth with the initial cough. A slick gooey mess lay in the dirt. It was the size of his two fists, and sticky to his cautious touch.

As Fishlegs went to his gronkle, he wanted to sketch this wonderous new discovery. With such evidence he could convince the others of his worth and show them his new dragon. He hoped to be the one to name it, he looked it over for inspiration. Yeah, Spineback was going to be it's species. As he rummaged through the saddlebags, a roar announced a new arrival. A shadow flew across the grass, and Fishlegs turned his back to Meatlug, looking up to see if this was another new dragon.

An almost avaricious thought struck him. Why did it need to be only one dragon per viking, he was sure to find enough time for both Meatlug and this beautiful fellow.

Unfortunately, he hadn't accounted for the effect on the Green Spineback. His mouth open wide in fear, he vomited forth huge quantities of white slime.
13323.title: Fishlegs was face to face with a completely unk...

133231.content:
Even though he was being covered in goo, he tried to calm the Green Spineback. "It's okay girl, she's not landing...." His eyes went up to the sky just to confirm.

All that was visible were a few clouds. Next he tried to wipe the gunk away, but with the dragon still vomiting up copious amounts of whatever it was he felt like he was bailing out a boat with a teaspoon.

He took some comfort in the fact that Meatlug didn't seem worried and wasn't rushing to defend his rider. He merely crunched away on some quartz he had dug up. However, as the moments passed Fishlegs noticed a tingling in his fingers and legs. Holding his hands up in front of his face, they dripped excess goo, he felt they were losing sensitivity. Cautiously he touched his chest to confirm, and while he felt it, there wasn't any sensation from his fingers themselves.

He tried fleeing, but the white gunk was sticky and only let him writhe around. Next he felt his legs collapse as all sensation vanished from them entirely. As he lay in the long grass, his head became heavy as his neck struggled to support the weight.

The dragon was still letting loose the horrible stuff as it advanced. Fishlegs thoughts turned to the creatures name as it finally closed it's enormous mouth. He was covered from his throat down to his toes. More a cocooner than a Spineback, he thought as he lost consciousness.
133231.title: It started to cocoon Fishlegs in the gunk.

1332311.content: What kind of weied dreams?
1332311.title: Fishlegs has some weird dreams.

choices: Fishlegs spots that there have been some changes.
Looking up he sees an impatient Astrid and Hiccup nearby.
1332312.content:
When Fishlegs woke up, he felt cosy and warm. He gave a happy sigh, it was only at this point that he realised he wasn't at home, and with a tremor recalled where he was.

The cocoon had dried by this point and was even a little powdery and weak. As he thrashed about, he felt the material break away exposing his skin to the chill air. With all his strength he wrenched his head and neck muscles until he could breathe through a crack in the surface of the cocoon. With effort he expanded that into a full hole for his mouth. He felt a degree of calm come back as he took in deep lungfuls of air.

Once his arms were free, he pried the rest off his body, revealing his legs and torso. His skin felt smoother to his blind touch, perhaps taking off the loose hair and leaving that bound in the cocoon.

Carefully he peeled the last of the white powdery shell from his face, and he was greeted by Meatlug. The dragon gave it's master a friendly lick to see his face again.
1332312.title: Fishlegs wakes invigorated.

13323121.title: Fishlegs spots that there have been some changes.

choices: Fishlegs freaks out, in complete denial.
Meatlug comes over and gives the viking a cautious sniff.
13323122.content:
Astrid gasps in shock as Fishlegs sat up. He was still a little groggy, and gave a little cough.

"Oh hey, guys... I just had the weirdest dream." he coughed bringing up a little white powder. His throat felt very sore and he rubbed it tentatively with one hand.

His voice sounded ... well a little -off-. He tried again. "Hey Astrid... Hey Hiccup."

His voice was definitely a little wrong, it sounded too high to his own ears. Even though he was still sleepy he felt something was wrong, his friends were never this quiet. He also felt lighter than usual as well... perhaps he was just invigorated from his sleep.

"Fishlegs you're... you're..." Astrid started uncertainly.

Hiccup carefully put a hand on her shoulder, "Start slowly." he advised.

"Fishlegs... your hair is longer now..." she fell silent as he pulled it in front of his own eyes. He looked ready to laugh at some prank, but as he saw his new straw blond locks, the answering tug from his skull confirmed that it wasn't a wig.

He looked confused, and dropped it. With a yawn he stretched.

"It's long .... long like Ruffnut's hair." Hiccup added trying to encourage his friend towards the right answer.

Finally Astrid lost her patience.

"Fishlegs.... you're a girl now." she said with an exasperated sigh. Her tone was also tinged a little with jealousy and anger.
13323122.title: Looking up he sees an impatient Astrid and Hicc...

choices: "Meatlug... will she still know me?" Fishlegs asked.
"Maybe, Goethe can fix this?" Hiccup suggested, weakly.
Hiccup snapped off a piece of the cocoon for analysis.
"She's in shock." Astrid said, "She'll fly back with me and Stormfly."
133231221.content:
"I won't be FishWife." Fishlegs protested. Her voice rising in pitch, as her panic mounted. Hiccup tried to ignore how her chest shimmied and shook as she got angrier, and more agitated.

"I'm not... not a girl."

Fishlegs ripped open her leather top, on the second attempt. Her arms not as beefy, her fingers not as strong as before.

She seemed just as shocked as Astrid and Hiccup at the sight of her new bust.

"I'm a girl." she half-whispered, in surprise.

Astrid pulled her top back together.

"There are some things that we don't do." She said. Her attention, snapping between Fishlegs, and Hiccup. Her gaze daring Hiccup to leer, or Fishlegs to protest further, or provide them with another display of her charms.

Hiccup spotted an interesting cloud floating overhead, as he saw Fishlegs' hand slip down beneath her bust. He only looked back to his friends, on hearing the sound of Astrid slapping Fishlegs' wandering hand.

"You. are. a. girl.... now." Her tone losing it's patience, and sympathy.
133231221.title: Fishlegs freaks out, in complete denial.

choices: Astrid gets jealous, as she sees the full changes.
The three friends head back to the village.
Fishlegs is unsteady on her feet and needs help standing up.
133231222.content:
"No... you're just kidding right.... I'm not really a girl." her voice was getting higher and shriller as she spoke.

"Now, Fishlegs...." Hiccup started.

Astrid however was more direct. She strode over, stopping as she stood over Fishlegs, her legs either side of the cocoon.

"You're not suggesting being a girl is a bad thing are you." Her fist cocked and ready.

Fishlegs tried to wriggle her arms free of the cocoon, but couldn't manage it. She flinches and turned away as Astrid's hand slashed forward.

But she didn't land a blow, with -crack-, she managed to pull away a large chunk of the white residue. Fishlegs managed to pull her arms free. She put them up to block any attacks. Even as she did, she couldn't deny the odd sensations as they'd brushed over her chest. It felt very, very sensitive.

As a larger viking, he was used to a little excess flesh. However, this felt like there was even more than that. Looking down between his upraised arms, he saw a generous cleavage.

It might just be an illusion. It might just be the way he was holding his arms. Dropping them to his side, he saw Astrid's face clearly, and he looked across to Hiccup. Both his friends had the same concerned look on their faces.

It was impossible to deny, Meatlug had to admit that he was now .... was now a she.

The cleavage was the largest she'd seen, and with the unexpected angle she was starting to shiver and panic. Taking a second look, she saw that her arms too had changed. They were now much skinnier, skinnier like Astrid's.

She put her head in her hands and burst into tears.

Hiccup and Astrid kept pulling apart the remains of the cocoon, although Hiccup was a little cautious about accidentally touching Fishlegs and further upsetting her.

While the vikings worked away, Meatlug nuzzled against the side of her head, tentatively licking and reassuring the newest female flyer.

Hiccup pulled away the last piece, and allowed Fishlegs to pull her legs and feet free.

choices: Snotlout noticed the glowing gem near the ruts his feet had dug in the ground.
A stray trickle of blood ran down from his scalp. One spine had grazed him.
"Soon, she'll see me as a friend." he assured Hookfang, "and then...." he mused.
Ruffnut popped up her head from it's hiding place...
1411.content:
Snotlout dived behind a rock for protection from the volley of spines that Stormfly had just unleashed. He was sure it was just playful, but those things were sharp. Hookfang was watching with amusement, as they flew towards his rider. Flames licking around his lips, puffing unevenly in draconian laughter.

The spines ricocheted off the rock, and stuck in the trunk of a tree. He felt sure one had whooshed through his hair. 'There was playful, and too playful.' Snotlout thought.

"Take a hint." Astrid's voice reaching him, as a gust of wind reached him. Stormfly leapt into the air with a powerful beat of his wings, working hard to get aloft. It sent a flurry of moss and loose, light twigs flying towards his face, as it peeked above the protection of his rock.

Enjoying the sight of Astrid's back, or bum if he was being honest with himself. Standing in her saddle, her legs flexed, as she settled in steady flight lowering her beautiful inspiring bum into the saddle.

"She's weakening... she's talking to me, now." he told himself. Dismissing the volley of spines, as a display of Stormfly's precision. If she'd meant them as anything other than a tease, he'd have been skewered by them. Happier, and lighter on his feet, he smiled his triumph to Hookfang.

It was then....

choices: Snotlout dreamed of giving the stone to Astrid, and winning her heart.
Hoping for an opening as a friend, he snuggled into Hookfang.

As Snotlout slept, the stone smoked, tendrils embracing his slumbering form.
14111.content:
The gem glowed with an emerald light. When Snotlout picked it up, he saw it wasn't just catching the Sun. It seemed to have an inner glow, a spark that danced in the centre. It fitted comfortably in the palm of his hand, as he wondered if it could be valuable. If he found more he could sell them to the village women... or give one as a present to Astrid.

Spending the rest of the afternoon hunting, he was tired and frustrated as the Sun dipped behind the mountains. He still only had that one gem. He tried and failed to stifle a yawn. Returning to the warmth of Hookfang's body, he rested against his dragon's flank. The warmth eating away at his last reserves. Settling himself, he looked deep into the stone, Hookfang's tail curling around to keep him cosy.

It felt hypnotic, the light dancing within, hinting at but never revealing a sinuous dancer. A smile spread over his features, as he dropped off. Eyelids getting impossibly heavy, Snotlout fell asleep, to dream in the comfortable, safe embrace of his dragon's warm body.
14111.title: Snotlout noticed the glowing gem near the ruts ...

141113.title: Snotlout dreamed of being a dragon, free to soa...

choices: A stray emerald sparkle caught Astrid's attention.
Worrying he might be stuck, Snotlout hunted for his gem!
Hookfang wakes and recognises Snotlout as friendly, if not his rider.
"I'm sure, Fishlegs could tell us, if it's a young one, or a mate." Astrid said.
1411131.content:
Alert even though he was asleep. Snotlout opened one eye a crack looking around for whatever, or whoever had disturbed his sleep. Hookfang's scales surrounded him in a lazy coil giving an extra layer of protection.

Not wanting to reveal he was awake, he tried not to move. Sniffing the air, he thought he could smell who it was.

A gust of wind swept over him, and he closed his eyes against the flying dust. The sound a dragon landing was unmistakable. The sound of clawed feet approaching was familiar.

"Good landing, Stormfly." Astrid's voice called from beyond the scaly wall blocking his view. "Let's see if we can find that lazy lug... He can't be far if Hookfang is here."

He heard the sound of Astrid dismounting. Poking his head above the snaking scales hiding him from sight.

"I'm not sleeping." he said in a grumbling voice.

Well! That's what he tried to say. Instead a gout of fire erupted from his mouth.

As Snotlout tried to stand up, he found his neck extending far farther than it should, going almost cross-eyed with effort. Snotlout took in the green scaly snout he'd developed.

Finding himself nose to nose with Stormfly, was a worry. The glare of the Nadder not liking the unwelcome burst of flames.

https://66.media.tumblr.com/f952ca103a4a...

Trying to raise his hands, open and wide, as a calming gesture, to show he was unarmed. Snotlout found instead wings stretching between his joints. Linking his arms to his torso, the corner of his eyes revealed his wings spread wide as a proud display.

Astrid was readying her axe, a stern look on her face. Clearly she didn't want to kill you.

She didn't want to slay any wild dragon. Trying talking again, this time in a whisper. Flames merely licked about his teeth and nostrils.

Forcing himself to draw his neck back in, he took a lower posture. Stormfly calmed at this submissive display, and Astrid looked more at ease too.

Astrid stroked Stormfly's broad muzzle. "Do you think they're mates?" she mused aloud.

"Or is that a young nightmare he's looking after?"

Snotlout felt offended by the suggestions, offended by her failure to recognise him despite this... this disguise? ... this ... whatever it was?

"Are we sure, Hookfang was male?"

"It looks immature in any case." Astrid decided.
1411131.title: Snotlout's sleep was interrupted.

choices: It takes Snotlout time to get a feel for the air currents,
Snotlout feels cocky as he flies, and tries some aerobatic moves...
The journey is uneventful, and they arrive to be greeted by the other riders.
14111313.content:
Hookfang woke with a languid stretch, taking the chance to rub against Snotlout marking him with his musk. Marking him as friendly, Snotlout stretched his wings once more, realising he'd have to fly back himself... as himself, rather than riding Hookfang.

As Astrid returned to mount Stormfly, the nadder gave Snotlout a warning glare.

https://66.media.tumblr.com/f27dcfbf0164...

Snotlout knew where he stood as a human. That basically the nadder knew who he was, a companion. But in this form, he wasn't sure how familiar he could be with them.

A kick of Astrid's heels against Stormfly's flank, and the nadder bounded a couple of steps before leaping into the air with a powerful swoop of her wings. As they started to climb Astrid looked over her shoulder to see if the two monstrous nightmares would follow.

She was half-sure that Hookfang would follow her, but had no idea if the wild dragon would follow at all. As she turned back to see where she was flying, she thought she saw the unknown dragon give her a wink.

Dismissing it, as a mistake or an illusion, she settled down to let Stormfly take her back to Berk. It was an easy journey, and she just relaxed trusting her to manage without too much interference.

Snotlout found new instincts rise up, as he stretched his wings wide. A couple of cautious flap, grazed the grass and gave him an idea how much lift he could expect. Like Stormfly a couple of stomping, bounding steps, and he took to the air. Behind him he heard the sound of Hookfang rise up to follow him. Keeping a watchful eye on the newest flier.
14111313.title: Hookfang wakes and recognises Snotlout as frien...

141113131.content: when they all take off snotlout is surprised to find that he knows exactly what to do in order to start flying without even thinking about it.
141113131.title: Snotlout finds flying comes naturally.

choices: Snotlout landed and bowed a powerful head, half-expecting praise.
His aerobatics had been noticed by the other dragon riders.
"I found this wild female." Astrid said as she dismounted Stormfly.
Snotlout went cross-eyed, as Fishlegs approached, hand outstretched.
Snotlout went cross-eyed, as Hiccup approached, hand outstretched.
1411131313.content:
Knowing the route, Snotlout flew with confidence growing by the second. The view was spectacular, and it felt different as a dragon than a rider. The powerful beating of his wings, feeling the air currents beneath his leathery wings. The gentle corrections from his powerful tail guiding and correcting his flight.

Suddenly, it hit, that he was flying, and he started to flap his wings wildly. A fear of falling gripping him. Instead, he soared, climbing quickly, with strong beats of his wings driving him higher and higher. His downdraft buffeting Astrid and Stormfly.

Just as quickly, Snotlout found a sense of calm. His panic banished, he realised he couldn't fall from his saddle. A smirking smile twisted his draconian mouth, as he looked down. Tucking his wings close to his body, he dove, swooping past the Nadder, diving low until he was brushing the treetops.

It was intoxicating, the control he felt, the response, his own instincts were sharper, the reactions quicker. With mighty effort he rose again, this time working in acrobatic turns and twirls. Rolling, and snapping rapid direction changes. It was magnificent, but his sharp draconian sight took in Astrid's observations, she was watching his behaviour. Judging him like a wild dragon...

"What else?!" thought Snotlout to himself. But, he was performing very human feats, and manoeuvres.

Too soon, they crested the last ridge. Snotlout's flight so low, it disturbed the snow on the rocks. Astrid flew higher, and behind him Hookfang watched with an expression that was hard to read.
1411131313.title: Snotlout flew with balletic grace.

she see’s hookfang differently than before

141113131333.content:
A brief flash of flames burst from beneath Snotlout's teeth, as she craned her long serpentine neck to double-check. With scales, it was so hard to tell male from female. She hadn't noticed... had assumed. But her eyes widened in shock, in fear... about what this meant.

Looking around the village, Snotlout suddenly felt exposed and naked. A lick of flame came from Hookfang's mouth, a hint of a smile on his hard-to-read face. The monstrous nightmare understood enough basic English, and draconian body-language to see the surprise on Snotlout's face.

A harsh, throaty chuckle came from Snotlout's former mount. The sound eerie, and unsettling like rocks being ground together.

"Do you think she's Hooky's mate?" Astrid asked, as Hiccup wandered up. Fishlegs was more concerned with measuring and comparing the new arrival against the male.

Snotlout's head shook 'No', at the thought, another gout of flames bursting from her mouth.

"Mate or not, she's scared!" Hiccup confirmed.

Stepping closer, with his hand-outstretched. Snotlout's heavy head bumped against the obstruction. Caution made him pause, resting his head against Hiccup's comforting hand. It was unsettling how calming it made her feel. A simple soothing touch, the familiar, a friendly hand from her blind-spot.

She might not be able to communicate in words... But, this was the closest connection she had, and she wanted it to endure.
141113131333.title: Snotlout's reaction...

1411131313331.title: Snotlout found herself bonding with Hiccup, as ...

choices: She was soothed, as Gustav stroked her long neck.
All the riders mounted, to hunt for ... him.
"Can I do my first flight alone?" Gustav asked excitedly...
"He's already a better rider than Snotlout." The twins guffawed!
1411131313334.content:
Snotlout barely felt the difference, as Hiccup's hand slid to the side and away. The smaller hand of Gustav replacing it.

https://i.pinimg.com/564x/3e/1f/ee/3e1fe...

Opening her eyes a crack, she snuffled as she took in the sight as well as the scent of Gustav. Too short, and too young to truly be a dragon rider, his aroma filled her nostrils as they grew and shrank. Snotlout taking his re-assuring smell deep into her lungs.

A little distance away, she heard Astrid and Hiccup talking. They missed him already, the overheard conversation was satisfying too. He always knew they never survive without him. Her mood upset, as the twins arrived, dismounted their Zippelback.

They exchanged a high-five, when Astrid told the pair was celebrating his absence.

"Woah... we got lucky..." Ruff said, slapping their hands together.

"Maybe permanently..." Tuff agreed.

The siblings discussed various ways he could've died, in delighted graphic detail. A lick of angry flame spilled from the corner of his long, wide mouth. No risk to Gustav, but drawing a nervous spike in his scent.

Hiccup also caught the uncontrolled flicker of fire. Telling off the twins, with a nod towards Gustav and Snotlout.

"We'll hunt for him later... once Gustav's bonded with the female." Hiccup told the twins, and assured Astrid.

"Yeah..." Fishlegs agreed, "I'm sure Hookfang will guide us back to where he was... and where he probably still is."

- - - - -

A new scent reached Snotlout, and she started to drool. 'Chicken'.

With an encouraging nod to Gustav from Hiccup, the young viking made a whole roast chicken appear from behind his back. Opening her mouth wide, she could smell his rising fear... Allowing a deep throaty purring laugh to escape, to calm his future rider. Snotlout's long draconian tongue lashed out, and wrapped around the chicken taking it, and swallowing it in one satisfied gulp.

It had never tasted so good. Snotlout paused, as she savoured it's delicious snack. She'd.... She'd thought of herself as Gustav's mount.

Knowing she should be offended... she couldn't summon the anger. He felt like a worthy rider, until she was back in human form. Besides, it was flattering he wanted the same breed as his hero.

"It's good the females are so docile." Fishlegs said.

Hiccup and Astrid agreeing. Was a little humiliating... She was a wild viking... well, dragon at the moment. But he was a wild viking, and she was a wild, untamed dragon.

She bristled, as she felt Hiccup and Astrid help the newest dragon rider to mount, and settle atop her long muscular neck.

1441121211111.content:
Toothless made multiple wishes at once, the first being that Hiccup would turn into a female Night Fury.

After all, there was no one he trusted more than his small human, and if he were to ever sire hatchlings, he wanted to have a chance with someone he truly cared about and trusted.

His second wish was that his tail fin would be healed. After all, if Hiccup became a dragon, he would no longer be able to control the fake tail fin. And instead of flying with Hiccup, he could fly WITH Hiccup.

Toothless' third wish was that the people of Berk would remember the battle of the nest differently: that Hiccup and Snotlout died there, and that shortly after that two new female dragons showed up -- a Night Fury and a Monstrous Nightmare.
