choices: Getting closer, they saw two Ruffnuts, and no Tuffnuts bickering.
"I'm still a guy." Ruffnut seemed to protest.
Getting closer, they saw a glowing stone held in Ruff's hand.
15211.content:
The six dragon riders had landed just outside the forest. The trees were too densely packed to fly between them... and with the prize that Fishlegs hoped to find, and Hiccup wanted to meet.

Leaving their dragons on the verge of the forest, they had to search on foot, looking for caves, hollows, and any hiding places they could find in the thick tree cover. The ground was overgrown, forcing them to cut their way through the bracken and heather as they hunted.

Hiccup had thought he'd spotted one on the ground as he flew over the area. A second visit by Astrid on Stormfly only confirmed it.

A Changewing... and if Fishlegs was right, probably injured. The hope being to make contact with and hopefully train a Mystery class dragon. If they had a tame, one they could examine it closely... It's variable coloured scales, giving it a chameleon-like ability to disappear into the background.

The use the riders could make of colour-shifting armour would aid their own attempts at stealth. Their own dragon scale armour was light and strong. Another bond with their mount. But, the potential for near-invisibility was too tempting.

Splitting into three groups, they naturally paired up. Astrid and Hiccup taking one direction, cutting their way through the undergrowth with her axe, and his flaming sword. Fishlegs, and Snotlout were a team, neither wishing to work with either of the Thorston twins. Fishlegs' gronkle iron sword making light work of the plant-life, and Snotlout using brute force to kick and beat his way through.

The twins charged into the green dappled darkness, as an unstoppable force of nature. Bickering and fighting, they didn't seem to notice the hazards as they vanished into the depths of the woods.

- - - - -

It was a long and frustrating search with the riders finding little of interest. The Changewing too well hidden from their random thrashing search. Sadly, lots of biting and stinging insects seemed to find them easily.

Fishlegs found a cave, and traces of the acid green spittle of the dragon burning hollows in the floor. Proof that their definitely was one in the area. Calling out, Hiccup and Astrid joined them in the cave, agreeing that the burns seemed to be recent, and that the dragon seemed to be injured... or at least not flying far.

A quick search confirmed there weren't any eggs in the cave, and they agreed they'd need more people to have a chance of finding the dragon. As Snotlout wanted to boast about his discovery of the cave, the burn, and all his conclusions he looked around for the twins.

- - - - -

It was then that the cries of the twins reached them. The sound not of their usual chaotic action. But with an added tone of terror filling their shouts.

"They've found it..." Astrid and Hiccup said at the same moment, before all four raced from the cave towards the sounds of the shouts. It was hard to tell where they were in the forest, but the shouts were getting closer, as they hacked their way forward.

The group burst free of the forest, into the sunlight, surprising even themselves. Quite near the waiting dragons. Looking around they spotted the twins near Barf and Belch. Blinking for the brightness of the Sun, they were relieved to see they didn't look to be in immediate danger.

Without a reason to return to their search, they headed towards their dragons. But, there was still something about the tone of the arguing twins that was.... well, wrong.
15211.title: A wishing stone

choices: Tuff fished a glowing crystal from her leather pouch.
"She did it! She did it! She did it!" Tuff erupted, jabbing at Ruff mercilessly.
"Well..." Ruff (The real Ruff) started with a smirk.
The pair immediately started talking over one another telling the story...
152111.content:
Seeing their friends approach, Ruffnut angrily pointed. "Hiccup... Astrid... Make her change me back..."

The group slowed their approach realising the cries had simply being the twins fighting. But, they stumbled to a halt, in a disorderly rabble as a second Ruffnut appeared from the far side of Barf and Belch.

"Nuh-Uh..." she protested. "It wasn't me..." She shook her head in denial.

Everyone looked from one to the other. No sign of Tuffnut, but two Ruffs, was making them all wonder if they were worse off or better off. Only Snotlout seemed to find this in any way pleasing.

A stubby arm pointing towards the first one, "So... You're saying... you're Tuff?" He asked uncertainly.

"Duh...well, obviously." She snapped back, planting her hands defiantly on her hips.

"Can't you tell, despite this body, My manliness shining through." She tried to strike a dramatic pose, shoulders back and chin held high.

Alas it only made her look ... as she was... just more like Ruff.

Tearing off a strip of cloth, Hiccup handed it to her.

"Well," he started uncertainly, "If you could wear this... just for the rest of us. So we can tell you apart." he finished in a softer voice.

"Gah!" Tuff objected, but tied it around her upper arm.

Knowing it'd end badly, Hiccup took a deep breath before asking. He found Astrid at his side, a comforting presence.

"So... what happened?"

1551312.content:
Toothless didn't expect to see a house, as he was walking alone through the outskirts of the New Berk island. And yet, there it was, in the deep dark part of the forest, blending in with the trees and shrubbery. It looked alike to viking houses that he used to see in the village, but lacked ornaments and decorations, like dragon heads, which most of Berk structures had, and it definitely was here before they moved to this new island.

Toothless got curious and proceeded to walk towards the house. The windows were shut, but he was interested in what's inside, got to the door and began to sniff the crack under it. His nose caught multiple scents that he didn't ever smell before, some were pleasant, and some were disturbing. Toothless bumped his head into the door, groaning lightly, as if asking the owner to open the door. He was a bit careless to worry the humans who maybe lived there, alone, but he was sure that the island was safe, after many-many patrols that vikings swept it with.

He soon heard the footsteps on the wooden floor inside, and in a moment the door was opened inwards. A beautiful young woman was standing at the doorstep, seemingly glad to see the bold dragon.

"OH GODS, a NIGHT FURY!" - she exclaimed with joy. "I thought I will never see one, and here you are, knocking in my door by yourself". A sincere smile could be seen on her face, and she reached her hands towards Toothless' muzzle, which made him growl at her at first, but, as he looked at her palms, he got strangely peaceful and let her touch him.

As the girl's hands touched his cheeks, Toothless felt soothed, and something was convincingly telling him that she could be trusted. She pulled his cheeks a bit, as if inviting him to her house, and he walked in, staring at her face and smiling.

"My name is Erica, big guy." - she introduced herself, as if it had much meaning for the dragon. "I live here since... some years ago, and study dragons. And I am really glad that I am lucky to see a majestic Night Fury in the flesh, and not just on rare drawings from the books!". After looking at Toothless with wide eyes for some moments, she hurried to another room: "Please, wait here, I will get you something to eat!".

Toothless was left alone in what he thought was her living room. There were anatomical drawings of dragons hanging on the walls, some strange formulas and runes that he couldn't understand. Vials of liquid of different colors, some even glowing, were scattered around on her shelves and what seemed like her working table. As he was looking around her room, something caught Toothless' attention, and he glanced back at the table. A small, but incredibly realistic statue of a Deadly Nadder was standing on it. It was smaller than his paw, in diameter, with its tail slightly curled towards its body, but it looked just like an alive dragon, and was amazing him. It even had the scent of a live dragon, and, as he carefully touched its flank, it seemed warm and soft to him...

The girl returned and found Toothless eyeing and sniffing the statue closely. "Oh, you find the previous addition to my collection interesting? She is a good girl. Such a beauty." - she stared at the statue with him. "But a Night Fury like you would surpass it. I've got something to eat for you, please open your mouth." - she told him gently, and Toothless, soothed by the unknown force, still not without a little hesitation, did as she asked.

The girl kneeled down in front of him, and unstrapped a vial of half blue-half purple sparkling liquid from her belt, uncorking it, and shoving it inside his mouth, along with her hand. Toothless felt a cold bitter liquid pour onto his tongue, and began to shake uncontrollably. He snapped out of his half-trance, pulled his head away, and hurried towards the still open door, to the outside.

But even though distance was really small, he found himself only getting farther away from the door at first, as the house's furniture was getting higher around him, and he felt his prosthetic tail slipping away, which got him puzzled. The girl giggled behind him, as he reached the porch, and, turning around, he saw a big pair of feet stepping towards him. He traced them up, and of course found them attached to the girl. Whatever magic was soothing him, no longer worked, and he tried to fire a plasma blast at her, to protect himself, but it just fizzled out after leaving his mouth.

The girl crouched in front of Toothless, and grabbed the shrunken night fury, who was now a bit smaller than a housecat. Toothless tried to break free, clawing at her arms, which only made her seemingly angry, and made her put more strength into holding him. "Fierce boy, huh? I didn't expect you to get wary so quickly, and to shrink so much and so quick from just one vial. Good think I extinguished your fire... Still, you need to be made smaller." - she told as she unstrapped another vial from her belt, uncorking it and bringing it by the already shrunken Toothless's mouth. "Open wide." - she told him, to which he, of course, only snarled. "Okay, if you want to play rough...". She forced his mouth open, hooking his lower jaw with her fingers and forcing it down, which made Toothless cry. Right after it she poured the entire vial into his gaping mouth, and even though a lot of liquid missed its target, as Toothless squirmed around in her grasp, he still got fed with much, much more, relative to his new size, than from his previous dose.

Immediately, Toothless started to shrink again, getting to the size of a rat, then mouse, in mere ten seconds or so. Erica was content with his new size, but was surprised as he continued to shrink, standing on her palm and looking at her face with fear, stopping at the size of an ant, maybe just a centimeter in length.

"I never shrunk a dragon to such a small size before..." - Erica thoughtfully said. "This Nadder took me ten doses of shrinking potion to get to that size. See, the smaller the creature gets, the more potion it needs to be fed to be shrunk even smaller, but with you it seems different. You will surely make a fine test subject for my experiments!" - she exclaimed, fascinated by the tiny Night Fury perched on her palm, barely visible to the naked eye.

Erica slowly walked to one of her cabinets, carefully holding her palm and watching Toothless, fearing that he may get blown away by the air currents of her moving body, or try to fly away. "Need a smaller flask for you... A-ha, here it is!" She took Toothless by his front paws, and dropped him into a tiny tube, which he was having troubles moving through, as it was too thin to allow him to stand horizontally, even at his current insignificant size. The girl closed the tube with a cork, took a needle, and punched some holes in it, for the tiny dragon to breath. The swarf was falling on Toothless below, who was standing on his hind legs, with his front paws leaned on the glass wals, and looking at the looming surroundings with fear, through the refracting and slightly dull glass of the tube.

She placed the tube into a stand, opened the closest cabinet, and then lifted it there, placing it on one of the shelves.

Toothless found himself surrounded by tens of shrunken dragons of different species and sizes, trapped in vials and flasks of different sizes and forms. There was a Terrible Terror larger than a Monstrous Nightmare, and Deadly Nadders smaller than said Nightmare, right in front of him. And still, they all towered over the shrunken Toothless.

"I will get you out when it's time. Oh, and also, don't worry, you won't have to eat or drink. You will just consume your original size's energy, so, if I grow you back, you will be smaller than you should, the longer you remain shrunk. I don't know if it is true for Night Furies though... You are my first one, and already surprise me. See ya!" - Erica told him and closed the cabinet's door.

15513211.content:
The Night Fury growled in the small human's face.

Just a few minutes ago, this human had shown himself, positively jubilant at the sight of the downed dragon.

For some reason though, the human had released him.

As the dragon was contemplating why, he felt something in his tail.

Or rather, a lack of something.

The dragon's rage bubbled as he realized that the only reason this human had released him was because he could not fly away, and would be easy prey.

'Well,' the dragon thought darkly, 'you stole my life from me, it's only fitting I take yours.'

With that thought, the Night Fury called upon his magic, and jumped into the human's much smaller body.

Hiccup's body spasmed for a few minutes, before it stood up with a smirk, "Life for life, human. Now, I better learn how to act like the hatchling."

The (former) dragon that Hiccup would have named Toothless smirked as he walked towards his former body. Once close enough, he looked around, and seeing that no one was nearby, he spoke, "So, what do you think of that body, Hiccup?"

Hiccup looked up at his former body from his place on the floor of the cage and gaped at him.

The former Night Fury continued, "You see, when you shot me down, you tore off one of my tail fins, which are imperative for flying. In other words, you practically sentenced me to a slow death. So, rather than go down that path, I decided to use my magic and swap bodies with the one who shot me down."

Smirking, he pointed at his former body, "Which, as I'm sure you're aware of, is you. I even used a bit of extra magic to grant me all of your memories."

The now scrawny viking turned around and began to walk away, "True, I may never get to fly again, but that is a small price when it comes to survival. But now, I have to be a better Hiccup than you ever were, and you..."

He looked back so the dragon could see the smirk on his face, "...can just be another dragon locked up in the kill ring. Farewell, dragon."

15533.content:
The night was dark and cold. The snow was blowing horizontally through the village, and everybody had retreated to their homes for the night. Even the livestock was safely protected in the barns and outbuildings of Berk.

Hiccup was getting Toothless to blast a rock repeatedly just to heat his home. Stoick had considered keeping everyone in the Great Hall, but it wasn't best suited for all the villagers to sleep there and he was sure the storm would blow itself out... well some time during the night.

Toothless looked mournfully at the door. He enjoyed riding the wild currents of a stormy sky... what dragon didn't enjoy the wind beneath it's wings.

Hiccup felt sympathy for his dragons desires, but really didn't fancy taking him up in this weather. He felt sure that his father was right, and it would be rather hard to hide his absence when they were both in such close proximity.

Besides even with his warmest clothes and riding Toothless, he didn't have the same fire in his belly as a dragon. He curled up in the living room, resting his head against Toothless's flank, he was soon asleep. Hiccup didn't wake when his father covered him with a blanket, although Toothless had a wary eye on him.

Satisfied that his son was warm and protected he went off to bed himself. After all, it had been a tiring day preparing the village for the storm. But the black clouds on the horizon hadn't been hard to read.

The only thought that troubled him was Gobber. His friend had chosen to stay at his forge, rather than shelter at home.

But Gobber was resilient, and he assured himself wise enough to go home if he needed. With a deep yawn, he stretched and headed off to bed.

Outside the wind howled, the clatter of an unsecured shutter punctuation the night. But this was no ordinary storm.

Pink sparkles glittered among the snow, as it crashed through Berk, bringing unforeseen consequences to the village.
15533.title: Other choices

choices: "There's a BIG problem,... everyone's a woman..." she squeaked.
"Astrid, Thank Odin, I found you." Hiccup said.
"Did you see that approaching fleet?" she asked breathlessly.
155332.content:
Astrid woke early, Stormfly curled around her keeping her warm and cosy like a baby dragon. The first light of day was barely brightening the horizon, but she was eager to be up and about.

http://images6.fanpop.com/image/photos/3...

Giving her a tickle under the chin. The dragon gave a gurgling chuckle at the attention, she knew what was coming next, and Astrid didn't disappoint. She tossed a whole roast chicken towards Stormfly. Snatching it out of the air, she gave a draconian purr, deeper and more resonant than any cat.

She needed no reason to ride her dragon, but she was always eager to keep her edge, and stop Hiccup getting too cocky. She soon had Stormfly saddled, and it was hard to tell which of them was more eager to get airborne. A gust of chill air billowed into the house, as they went outside. The freshly laden snow blanketing the village, only the warmth of the Gobber's forge showed any green around it's dry ground.

With a powerful leap, her thigh muscles propelled her into the air. Astrid guided her dragon towards the sea stacks. A swift slalom should be enough to shake off the cobwebs. Already the fresh air was tickling her lungs as she gulped down a lungful of the freshest air.

Swooping low over the village, it was clear she was the first one to be up and awake. Stormfly raced down the hill, sweeping between the building only five or so feet off the ground. The sheep cowered, as the pair flew past, a rippling of frosty flakes trailing in their wake.

There was a faint pink hue to the snow, which Astrid put down to the low Sun. The sky was hers and hers alone.

They had the sky to themselves, with Stormfly trailing her claws in the water, Astrid trusted her mount instinctively. Objecting to the occasional splash as an unwary fish found it's end scooped up into Stormfly's maw. Astrid held tight to Stormfly, pulling her legs close around the dragon's neck. She soaked up the warmth, as the wind sped past her. Flat out, there weren't many dragons who could beat Stormfly for speed. However, one of them was Toothless, and so she trained hard to offer him competition... and for the chance of her own victory, their own victory.

Out of the blue, a plasma bolt shot past her shoulder. Hiccup was awake. Glancing back over her shoulder she made out the shape of Hiccup, and clutching tightly to his neck, the rider. She waved a challenge to Hiccup, and swerved towards the stacks. Another plasma blast flew past her shoulder, this time closer than she liked. She felt the heat as it passed. It also stopped her from reaching the stacks.

She was pissed, it was too close. She did an evasive turn, and was heading back into the village. It was no surprise that they were instantly on her tail. Air hissed angrily from her lungs, but if she was going to have an argument, she wanted some privacy. Landing on the beach, Stormfly kicked up sand as she landed.

Toothless landed with grace and poise, a wide smile on his face. Hiccup dismounted, a little less gracefully, and pulled off his helmet.

https://s-media-cache-ak0.pinimg.com/736...

A long mane of hair slipped loose. Hiccup was a girl her emerald eyes giving her a feline look that briefly made Astrid jealous, before she dismissed the thought. She was still too stunned to speak, Hiccup started....
